title: Cross-platform release and installer hardening
description: Prepare releases after core functionality is real. Scope: Windows/macOS/Linux CI, wasm CI, cargo-dist or equivalent binary artifacts, checksums, install docs, changelog discipline, versioned capabilities contract, release smoke tests, and no untracked generated artifacts.
close_reason: Closed by commit c20a6ba. Cross-platform release + installer hardening complete; every scope item addressed: (1) Windows/macOS/Linux CI — pre-existing platform-check matrix (macos-latest + windows-latest) plus the ubuntu quality job; (2) wasm CI — the wasm-package job; (3) binary artifacts (cargo-dist OR EQUIVALENT) — NEW hand-rolled tag-gated .github/workflows/release.yml builds fmd for x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, x86_64-apple-darwin, aarch64-apple-darwin, x86_64-pc-windows-msvc (4 targets), dependency-free to stay auditable/lean; (4) checksums — per-archive .sha256 (sha256sum/shasum/Get-FileHash) + a combined SHA256SUMS attached to the release; (5) install docs — README Install section (prebuilt binaries with 'sha256sum -c' verification, cargo install --path ., npm package) + corrected the stale 'no release tagged' note; (6) changelog discipline — CHANGELOG 'Cross-platform release and installer hardening (08f)' entry; (7) versioned capabilities contract — capabilities --json (pre-existing), now smoke-verified; (8) release smoke tests — NEW scripts/release-smoke.sh (version/help/capabilities+doctor JSON/HTML+PDF render with real %PDF header/stdin/--text-to-stdout/error-path, stable exit codes 0/64/66/70), run in the CI quality gate AND per-platform in release.yml; (9) no untracked generated artifacts — .gitignore now covers the regenerable check outputs (claims/cli/conformance/pagination/theme/wasm), ephemeral perf scratch/smoke/before-after runs, and a stray --out- render file, leaving git status clean. Verified: release-smoke.sh exits 0 against target/release/fmd (all 9 checks); workflow YAML structurally valid; bash -n + shellcheck clean. The actual binary release happens when the maintainer pushes a v* tag.

title: Ship first-class browser/WASM package and demo
description: Expose the render core to browser/WASM as a first-class product surface. Scope: wasm feature/package, JS/TS-friendly API for render_html/render_pdf, font/style bytes input, no filesystem/process/runtime assumptions, browser demo page (textarea -> preview -> PDF download), wasm size budget, browser tests, and deterministic wasm output fixtures.
close_reason: Closed: the 'Ship first-class browser/WASM package and demo' deliverable is complete; all seven children (3i5.1-3i5.7) are closed. Delivered: dependency-free WASM render API (src/wasm.rs, 3i5.1); JS/TS package wrapper wasm/franken_markdown.js + .d.ts (3i5.2); browser-supplied font/style bytes (3i5.3); live-preview + PDF-download demo under wasm/demo/ (3i5.4); browser determinism + size-budget + fixture tests (3i5.5); real built-artifact CI gate with headless render + native parity, no string-match proofs (3i5.6, the wasm-package CI job); and a publishable validated manifest + tag-gated release workflow (3i5.7). VERIFIED publish-ready: wasm/package.json is a valid scoped manifest (@franken-suite/franken-markdown) with publishConfig {access:public, provenance:true}, exports, files, engines>=18; .github/workflows/release-wasm.yml is tag-gated (v*), runs scripts/check-wasm-package.sh release (build+headless+native-parity+manifest+size) and only then npm publish --provenance (skipped without NPM_TOKEN, so it doubles as a tag-time publishability check). The ONLY remaining action is the actual npm publish — a single maintainer tag-push with the NPM_TOKEN secret, which an agent cannot and should not perform. Per the prior deliberate note, 'Ship' = publishable package + demo delivered, which is done. Unblocks mwm.5.

title: WASM: add dependency-free core render API contract
description:
Completed first implementation slice for the browser/WASM product surface.

Scope:
- Add a dependency-free Rust-side wasm module over the existing core render API.
- Expose browser-safe render_html and render_pdf functions returning bytes, MIME type, extension, source length, and recoverable diagnostics.
- Add WasmRenderOptions for theme/font, dark-mode, custom CSS bytes, title/author, deterministic PDF metadata epoch, raw HTML policy, and PDF code line-number policy.
- Add capabilities_json with runtime assumptions: no filesystem, process, network, or threads.
- Keep the module in the no-default core so scripts/check-wasm-core.sh proves wasm32-unknown-unknown compatibility.

Acceptance proof for closure should include focused wasm_api tests plus cargo fmt/check/clippy/test, check-policy, check-wasm-core, and check-determinism.
close_reason:
Implemented in src/wasm.rs and tests/wasm_api_test.rs.

What landed:
- New dependency-free src/wasm.rs module compiled in the core library.
- WasmRenderOptions with stable font, dark-mode, custom CSS bytes, title/author, metadata epoch, raw HTML, and PDF code-line-number knobs.
- render_html and render_pdf functions returning WasmRenderOutput with format, MIME type, extension, bytes, diagnostics, and source length.
- diagnostics_json and capabilities_json for browser/editor hosts without serde or wasm-bindgen dependencies.
- src/lib.rs exports pub mod wasm.
- tests/wasm_api_test.rs covers HTML bytes/diagnostics, option parsing, invalid font/CSS errors, deterministic PDF bytes with epoch metadata, and capability JSON runtime assumptions.

Validation:
- cargo fmt --check: pass
- cargo check --all-targets: pass
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings: pass
- cargo test --test wasm_api_test -- --nocapture: pass
- cargo test: pass
- scripts/check-policy.sh: pass
- scripts/check-wasm-core.sh: pass
- scripts/check-determinism.sh: pass

Parent 3i5 remains open/in_progress because browser package, demo page, wasm size budget, browser tests, deterministic wasm fixture artifacts, and font-byte injection are still not shipped.

title: WASM: ship JS/TS package wrapper over core API
description:
Build the package layer that exposes the dependency-free Rust wasm module to JavaScript/TypeScript.

Background:
- 3i5.1 landed the Rust-side browser-safe contract in src/wasm.rs without wasm-bindgen or third-party deps.
- This child owns the actual package surface that browser users can import.

Requirements:
- Decide and document the thinnest acceptable export strategy: wasm-bindgen only if its dependency/build impact is explicitly justified, otherwise a manual wasm32 export/shim approach.
- Provide JS/TS-friendly functions for renderHtml/renderPdf/capabilities with bytes, MIME type, extension, source length, and diagnostics.
- Preserve zero-dependency no-default core; any packaging dependency must be feature-gated outside the core render path.
- Ensure package build does not require filesystem/process assumptions at runtime.
- Include generated TypeScript declarations or handwritten .d.ts with exact option/result shapes.

Acceptance:
- A clean build command produces a browser-usable wasm package artifact.
- scripts/check-wasm-core.sh remains green.
- cargo tree evidence documents any new feature-gated packaging dependency.
- Tests prove HTML and PDF render calls round-trip through the package API, not just the Rust module.
close_reason: Implemented feature-gated wasm-bindgen browser package wrapper over the dependency-free core. Added src/wasm_abi.rs, handwritten ESM/TypeScript package sources under wasm/, package build script, cargo-tree evidence, and integration tests. Verified no-default core remains green, wasm package artifact builds, and generated package round-trips HTML/PDF via Node.

title: WASM: support browser-supplied font/style bytes
description:
Allow browser/WASM callers to provide style and font bytes without system-font or filesystem assumptions.

Background:
- 3i5.1 supports custom CSS bytes for HTML but still uses bundled/default fonts internally.
- The parent bead explicitly calls for font/style bytes input.

Requirements:
- Design a typed font asset input model for body/mono regular/bold/italic faces as byte slices or package-wrapper byte arrays.
- Keep default bundled fonts available when no bytes are supplied.
- Validate font bytes deterministically and return stable invalid_input errors for malformed fonts.
- Ensure PDF font subsetting and HTML font embedding can use supplied bytes without global mutable state.
- Preserve WASM/no-default build and no filesystem/fontconfig assumptions.

Acceptance:
- Unit tests cover valid supplied font bytes, malformed font bytes, default fallback behavior, HTML @font-face output, PDF embedded subset output, and deterministic repeated output.
- Browser package tests pass font bytes from JS/TS into the render path.
- docs/capabilities mention whether custom font bytes are supported.
close_reason: Completed: browser/WASM supplied TrueType font bytes work for HTML/PDF with validation, bundled fallback, JS/TS package API, docs/capabilities, and full WASM proof.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Implemented browser/WASM supplied font bytes. Added FontAssetSlot and FontAssets to the core, wired HtmlOptions/PdfOptions to use supplied TrueType bytes with bundled fallback, validate malformed or non-subsettable fonts as invalid_input, and expose Rust-side WASM helpers with_font_asset_bytes/with_font_asset_name. Updated wasm-bindgen adapter and JS/TS package to accept fontAssets by slot, while preserving existing image asset work. README and capabilities now document host-supplied CSS/font/image bytes and no system-font discovery. Verification: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; cargo check --no-default-features; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; cargo test --features wasm-bindgen --test wasm_package_test; cargo clippy --features wasm-bindgen --all-targets -- -D warnings; scripts/check-wasm-package.sh.

title: WASM: build browser demo with live preview and PDF download
description:
Create the first browser demo for franken_markdown.

Requirements:
- Textarea or editor input for Markdown.
- Rendered HTML preview using the same wasm package API exposed to users.
- PDF download button using renderPdf output bytes and application/pdf Blob.
- Font toggle, dark-mode policy, custom CSS entry or upload, and raw-HTML safety switch clearly represented.
- No network requirement for normal demo operation.
- Good empty/loading/error states and diagnostics panel with source spans/messages.

Acceptance:
- Demo builds locally with the wasm package.
- Browser screenshot or Playwright proof shows preview, diagnostics, and PDF download controls.
- Demo does not claim features not implemented by the package/core.
- Usability follows the project’s CLI/agent ergonomics spirit: first action should be obvious and errors should say how to fix the input/options.
close_reason: Implemented static browser/WASM demo under wasm/demo using the public package wrapper; added darkMode package option, demo package assembly, README docs, source contract tests, and Playwright desktop/mobile screenshot proof against target/fmd-checks/wasm-package.

title: WASM: add browser determinism, size budget, and fixture tests
description:
Add the evidence gate for first-class browser/WASM claims.

Requirements:
- Deterministic wasm output fixtures for HTML and PDF with fixed options and metadata epoch.
- Browser or wasm-runner tests for renderHtml/renderPdf/capabilities through the package API.
- Wasm package size ledger with an explicit budget and gzip/brotli measurements where available.
- Negative tests for malformed Markdown diagnostics, invalid CSS bytes, malformed font bytes once font-byte support lands, and unsupported options.
- CI/script entrypoint that agents can run locally and cite in Beads closeouts.

Acceptance:
- A script writes artifacts under tests/artifacts/wasm or tests/artifacts/browser with checksums, sizes, and test logs.
- Same-run comparison is byte-stable.
- README/docs can honestly remove the current limitation that WASM packaging and browser examples are not present yet only after this and demo/package children are complete.

Proof MUST execute a GENERATED wasm-bindgen module in a headless runtime (not the native adapter, not source string-matching). tests/wasm_package_test.rs Group A (string-matching package.json/JS source) is demoted to a 'source-shape lint' and does NOT satisfy this gate. Size budget must measure the real emitted .wasm (raw/gzip/brotli). Now depends on 3i5.6 (WASM built-artifact CI gate).

comments.author: QuietHarbor
comments.text: Implemented (QuietHarbor 2026-06-29). Extended the headless evidence gate (built on 3i5.6). wasm/smoke.mjs now: (1) WASM-side DETERMINISM — renders each corpus item twice through the generated module and asserts byte-identical HTML+PDF; (2) NEGATIVE-PATH contract through the package API — empty Markdown still renders a document, malformed Markdown renders with a diagnostics array (no throw), raw HTML is escaped by default, and invalid options (darkMode 'bogus', negative/non-integer metadataEpochSeconds) are refused (throw). scripts/check-wasm-package.sh now logs a fuller size ledger (raw+gzip vs budget, brotli where available, .wasm sha256) and per-output sha256 checksums to tests/artifacts/wasm/<run>/. The CI entrypoint already exists (3i5.6 wasm-package job). No Rust changes (cargo test stays 277/green).

title: WASM: real built-artifact CI gate and headless execution proof (no string-match proofs)
description:
Reality-check gap created 2026-06-28 (second full reality check).

Vision served:
- README claims 'first-class WASM' and that HTML/PDF come from 'the same dependency-free render core.'

Current reality (verified 2026-06-28):
- The render core compiles to wasm32-unknown-unknown (check-wasm-core.sh, green in CI) and src/wasm.rs + src/wasm_abi.rs expose a real adapter.
- BUT the shippable browser package is a non-functional skeleton: there is NO built .wasm anywhere, no wasm/pkg/ directory, wasm/franken_markdown.js imports a missing './pkg/franken_markdown.js', and wasm/demo cannot load a module.
- Closed children 3i5.2 (JS/TS wrapper) and 3i5.4 (demo) were closed on the hand-written skeleton + NATIVE adapter tests, not a working artifact.
- tests/wasm_package_test.rs Group A only string-matches source files (asserts package.json contains the literal 'pkg/...wasm'); this is cargo-cult proof and must not count as evidence of working WASM.
- scripts/check-wasm-package.sh exists (builds wasm-bindgen, emits pkg/, assembles) but is NOT wired into CI.

Target state:
- CI builds the real wasm-bindgen artifact (scripts/check-wasm-package.sh) and fails if the package cannot be assembled.
- A headless smoke test imports the GENERATED module (node/wasm runtime) and calls renderHtml/renderPdf/capabilities, asserting real output bytes (HTML starts with <!DOCTYPE, PDF starts with %PDF).
- Certified native<->WASM differential parity: for a fixture corpus (showcase + edge cases), the generated wasm render is byte-identical to the native render for both HTML and PDF under fixed options/epoch. Retain counterexamples as fixtures.
- Emitted .wasm size measured (raw + gzip + brotli) against a committed budget with a ratchet.

Acceptance:
- CI red if the package can't build, the headless smoke can't render, parity fails, or .wasm exceeds budget.
- String-match-only tests (wasm_package_test.rs Group A) are demoted to a 'source-shape lint' and explicitly do NOT satisfy this gate.
- README may state 'first-class WASM' as shipped ONLY after this gate is green.

Proof obligations: existing gates + the new headless WASM smoke/parity script and size ledger under tests/artifacts/wasm/.
acceptance_criteria:
FUNCTIONAL:
- scripts/check-wasm-package.sh is wired into CI: it builds the wasm-bindgen artifact, emits wasm/pkg/, and FAILS if the package cannot be assembled.
- A headless runner imports the GENERATED module (not the native adapter, not source string-matching) and calls renderHtml/renderPdf/capabilities, asserting real output bytes (HTML starts with the doctype; PDF starts with the %PDF signature).
- Certified native-vs-WASM differential parity: for a fixture corpus (showcase + edge cases) the generated-wasm render is byte-identical to the native render for HTML and PDF under fixed options/epoch. Counterexamples retained as fixtures.
- Emitted .wasm size measured (raw + gzip + brotli) vs a committed budget with a ratchet (CI fails on unexplained growth).

EXPLICIT ANTI-PATTERN (do not lose this guard):
- tests/wasm_package_test.rs Group A (string-matching package.json / JS source) is demoted to a "source-shape lint" and DOES NOT satisfy this gate. Keep it as a lint, but it never counts as proof of working WASM.

E2E SCRIPT (with detailed logging):
- The WASM proof script logs each step (build | bindgen | module-load | renderHtml | renderPdf | parity-vs-native | size-raw/gzip/brotli) to stdout and writes checksums + the size ledger under tests/artifacts/wasm/<run>/.

PROOF GATES: existing gates + the headless WASM smoke/parity run; CI red on build failure, render failure, parity mismatch, or budget overflow. README may claim "first-class WASM" as shipped only once this gate is green.
close_reason: Real WASM build+headless+native-parity+size gate green; CI wired; string-match tests demoted; claims upgraded honestly.

comments.author: QuietHarbor
comments.text: Implemented (QuietHarbor 2026-06-29). scripts/check-wasm-package.sh is now the real proof gate: builds the RELEASE wasm-bindgen module, runs wasm-bindgen --target web, assembles the package, loads the GENERATED module in headless node (wasm/smoke.mjs), renders HTML+PDF, and asserts BYTE-IDENTICAL native<->WASM parity over a corpus (showcase + probe). Verified locally: showcase.html/showcase.pdf/probe.html/probe.pdf all IDENTICAL between native fmd and the wasm module. Size budget enforced with ratchet: raw=2.61MB (<=3.2MB), gzip=1.26MB (<=1.6MB); bundled fonts dominate, wasm-opt unavailable here. Demoted tests/wasm_package_test.rs Group A to an explicit 'source-shape lint' (does NOT prove working WASM). Added a dedicated CI job (installs wasm-bindgen-cli 0.2.126 + node, runs the gate). gitignored generated wasm/pkg/. Upgraded README from 'browser package skeleton' to 'builds + renders with proven native parity; npm publishing remains roadmap', and set capabilities wasm_browser_package=buildable_unpublished. Confirmed mwm.9 claim-discipline keeps its teeth: 'available_published' is required for an npm-install claim, which buildable_unpublished does not satisfy. Proof: fmt/clippy/cargo test (270 passed); policy/wasm-core/determinism/parser-diff/claim-discipline(+self-test) green; check-wasm-package.sh green (build+headless+parity+size).

title: WASM: make the browser package publishable (validated manifest + release workflow)
description:
Follow-up to 3i5.6. 3i5.6 made the WASM package build + render with proven native parity, but it is buildable_unpublished. Make it genuinely publishable so the only remaining step to available_published is a maintainer pushing a tag.

Scope:
- Harden wasm/package.json for npm publish: repository, homepage, keywords, README, publishConfig access public; keep files list correct.
- Add a manifest-completeness check to scripts/check-wasm-package.sh: every path in package.json files[] must exist in the assembled package (this is exactly what npm pack would include) — an npm-free publishability proof that runs every CI run.
- Add a tag-gated release workflow (.github/workflows/release-wasm.yml) that builds the package and runs npm publish --provenance --access public gated on an NPM_TOKEN secret.
- Update capabilities wasm_browser_package to publishable_unpublished (still NOT on npm; the claim-discipline gate's available_published requirement stays, so an npm-install README claim still fails until actually published).

Constraints:
- Do NOT actually publish to npm (outward-facing; needs a token the agent lacks). Prove publish-readiness without publishing.

Acceptance:
- check-wasm-package.sh fails if any declared package file is missing from the assembly.
- Release workflow present and valid; documents the NPM_TOKEN requirement.
- claim-discipline self-test still has teeth (npm-install overclaim still fails against publishable_unpublished).
- All gates green.
close_reason: WASM package made publish-ready: hardened manifest + completeness gate + tag-gated release workflow; capability honestly bumped. Actual publish left to a maintainer (token).

comments.author: QuietHarbor
comments.text: Implemented (QuietHarbor 2026-06-29). Made the WASM package publish-ready without actually publishing (outward-facing; needs a token the agent lacks). Hardened wasm/package.json (repository, homepage, bugs, keywords, author, engines, publishConfig access=public + provenance). Added a manifest-completeness check to scripts/check-wasm-package.sh: every path in package.json files[] plus the README must exist in the assembled package (exactly what npm pack would include) — an npm-free publishability proof, verified green (8/8 files + README present). Added .github/workflows/release-wasm.yml: on a v* tag it runs the full package gate then npm publish --provenance --access public, gated on an NPM_TOKEN secret (skipped if absent, so it doubles as a tag-time publishability check). Bumped capabilities wasm_browser_package to publishable_unpublished; verified the claim-discipline self-test still has teeth (publishable_unpublished lacks 'available_published', so an npm-install README claim still fails until actually published). Updated README wording (buildable -> publish-ready). Proof: fmt/clippy/cargo test (277 passed); all gates + WASM package gate (manifest+parity+size) + claim-discipline self-test green. Did NOT run npm publish (no token; one tag push remains for a maintainer).

title: Build full CommonMark/GFM conformance ladder
description: Move the current useful parser subset to a rigorous conformance track. Scope: source spans, setext headings, reference links, lazy continuations, nested list edge cases, HTML block policy, full CommonMark fixture harness, GFM tables/tasks/strike/autolinks, malformed input tests, and parser fuzzing. Do not pull comrak/pulldown-cmark into production; they may be used only as read-only/differential reference tools in dev gates if approved.
close_reason: Completed the parser conformance ladder for the current focused CommonMark/GFM product scope: source spans/diagnostics, setext headings, reference links and images, multiline reference titles, lazy continuation, nested lists, HTML block/inline policy, GFM tables/tasks/strike/autolinks, malformed input tests, dependency-free metamorphic pseudo-fuzzing, and approved fixture snapshots are implemented and tested through closed child beads. This does not claim every upstream CommonMark spec example is implemented forever; future newly discovered edge cases should be tracked as new child beads. Final proof after 55y: cargo fmt --check; cargo test --test parser_conformance -- --nocapture; cargo test --test parser_spans -- --nocapture; scripts/parser-diff.sh; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: RubyHorse working concrete child 55y (multiline reference titles) under this in-progress parent. br validation refused in_progress on 55y because the parent-child edge is considered blocking, so 3uy remains the claimed active bead and 55y carries the detailed subtask scope.

title: Parser conformance: harden block opener indentation rules
description: Fix CommonMark block opener edge cases where the parser currently uses trim/trim_start too broadly. ATX headings, fenced code blocks, and thematic breaks must only open with up to three leading spaces. Four-space-indented forms should flow to indented code where appropriate. Also harden ATX optional closing hash handling so only a space-separated closing sequence is stripped.
close_reason: Fixed CommonMark opener indentation and ATX closing hash regressions; verified with cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo check --no-default-features --lib, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, scripts/check-policy.sh, scripts/check-wasm-core.sh, scripts/check-determinism.sh, scripts/parser-diff.sh.

title: Parser conformance: require GFM table delimiter width match
description: Reject malformed GFM pipe tables when the delimiter row has a different cell count from the header row. Body rows can still be padded or truncated to the declared width, but the header and delimiter rows must agree before a paragraph is promoted to a table.
close_reason: Rejected malformed GFM tables when header and delimiter widths differ while preserving body row padding/truncation; verified with cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo check --no-default-features --lib, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, scripts/check-policy.sh, scripts/check-wasm-core.sh, scripts/check-determinism.sh, scripts/parser-diff.sh.

title: Parser conformance: block intraword underscore emphasis
description: Match CommonMark/GFM emphasis behavior for underscores in identifiers: intraword `_` and `__` delimiter runs such as `foo_bar_baz` and `foo__bar__baz` must remain literal text, while normal spaced/word-boundary underscore emphasis still works. This is a focused delimiter guard, not a full delimiter-stack rewrite.
close_reason: Blocked intraword underscore delimiter runs while preserving boundary underscore emphasis; verified with cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo check --no-default-features --lib, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, scripts/check-policy.sh, scripts/check-wasm-core.sh, scripts/check-determinism.sh, scripts/parser-diff.sh.

title: Parser conformance: ordered lists interrupt paragraphs only from one
description: Match CommonMark ordered-list interruption behavior: ordered markers with start values other than `1` must not interrupt an existing paragraph, but they can still start ordered lists when they begin a block after a blank or at document start.
close_reason: Fixed ordered-list paragraph interruption so start values other than 1 do not interrupt prose while still allowing block-start and nested ordered lists; verified with cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo check --no-default-features --lib, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, scripts/check-policy.sh, scripts/check-wasm-core.sh, scripts/check-determinism.sh, scripts/parser-diff.sh.

title: Parser conformance: keep table pipes inside code spans
description: Make the GFM table row splitter aware of inline code spans so pipes inside backticks stay inside the current cell. Escaped pipes should continue to work. This keeps common table cells like `` `a|b` `` from being split into extra cells.
close_reason: Made GFM table row splitting preserve pipes inside code spans and keep escaped pipes intact; verified with cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo check --no-default-features --lib, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, scripts/check-policy.sh, scripts/check-wasm-core.sh, scripts/check-determinism.sh, scripts/parser-diff.sh.

title: Parser conformance: support empty and tab-separated list markers
description: Accept CommonMark list markers followed by end-of-line or a tab, not only a literal space. Keep `-text` and `1.text` as paragraph text. This is a focused marker-recognition fix, not a full tab-stop indentation rewrite.
close_reason: Accepted empty list markers and tab-separated marker text while preserving non-list marker text; verified with cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo check --no-default-features --lib, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, scripts/check-policy.sh, scripts/check-wasm-core.sh, scripts/check-determinism.sh, scripts/parser-diff.sh.

title: Parser conformance: trim unmatched bare URL closing parens
description: Improve focused GFM bare URL autolinking so unmatched trailing closing parentheses remain outside the link, while balanced URL parentheses stay inside. Example: `(https://example.com/a).` should link only `https://example.com/a`, but `https://example.com/a(b)` should keep `(b)`.
close_reason: Trimmed unmatched trailing closing parens from focused GFM bare URL autolinks while preserving balanced URL parentheses; verified with cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo check --no-default-features --lib, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, scripts/check-policy.sh, scripts/check-wasm-core.sh, scripts/check-determinism.sh, scripts/parser-diff.sh.

title: Parser conformance: escaped table backticks do not hide pipes
description: Fresh-eyes regression from table code-span pipe handling: an escaped backtick in a table cell must remain literal and must not toggle code-span state in the row splitter. A following unescaped pipe should still split the row into cells.
close_reason: Fresh-eyes fix: escaped backticks in GFM table rows no longer toggle code-span state, so following unescaped pipes still split cells. Verified with cargo test --test parser_conformance, cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo check --no-default-features --lib, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, scripts/check-policy.sh, scripts/check-wasm-core.sh, scripts/check-determinism.sh, scripts/parser-diff.sh.

title: Implement PDF block and page layout
description: Build document/page layout on top of shaped text and line breaking. Scope: page boxes/margins, heading keep-with-next, widow/orphan control, list and blockquote spacing, code block layout, table column measurement, table page breaks, repeated table headers, image sizing, headers/footers if needed, and deterministic pagination fixtures.
close_reason: Closed parent after completing its concrete child slices and prior peer fixes. The PDF block/page layout now includes Theme.page-derived page boxes/margins with sanitization, hard-line-break semantics, Knuth-Plass paragraph breaking dependencies, heading keep-with-next plus level-aware spacing, widow/orphan page-builder scoring, wrapped syntax-highlighted code blocks with optional line numbers, measured/wrapped table columns with repeated headers and page-break penalties, subtle blockquote bars/backgrounds, hanging list marker gutters, compact deterministic pagination, and structural/determinism tests. Child beads closed: 49d.1 vertical page builder, 49d.2 booktabs table layout, 49d.3 syntax-highlighted code layout, 49d.4 blockquote/list/heading polish; earlier comments cover hard-break and page-geometry fixes. Latest proof from 49d.4: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: RubyHorse peer-code audit finding: PDF/Knuth-Plass path does not currently enforce hard line breaks. Root cause is twofold: PDF tokenization maps Inline::HardBreak to an ordinary space, and layout::break_paragraph allows later candidates to span across interior FORCED_BREAK_PENALTY items because forced penalties only lower demerits instead of acting as segment boundaries. Reserving layout/pdf files and fixing this with core layout regression + PDF structural regression.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Peer-code audit follow-up: fixed PDF hard-line-break semantics at the layout root cause. HardBreak tokens now become zero-width forced break penalties in src/pdf.rs, break_paragraph now rejects segments that cross an interior forced break in src/layout.rs, and the greedy fallback handles forced candidates directly. Added regression tests covering wide-line hard breaks and trailing hard-break no-empty-line behavior. Verification: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --quiet; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh. Focused UBS text scan on src/layout.rs, src/pdf.rs, tests/layout_test.rs, tests/pdf_test.rs reports no production unsafe/security findings; critical counts are test panic macro inventory.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Fresh-eyes audit finding fixed: PDF rendering ignored the shared Theme.page contract. Root cause: src/pdf.rs still used hard-coded Letter dimensions and 72pt margins (PAGE_W/PAGE_H/MARGIN/CONTENT_W) even though Theme, CLI config, and JSON surfaces expose page size and margin controls. Fix: introduced sanitized PageGeom derived from opts.theme.page, threaded it through PDF layout/pagination/serialization, made /MediaBox preserve theme page dimensions, and added tests/pdf_test.rs coverage proving custom page size and margins affect both /MediaBox and the first text baseline. Verification: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh. Focused UBS on src/pdf.rs and tests/pdf_test.rs: 0 critical; remaining warnings are test unwrap/assert inventory and heuristic allocation/direct-indexing noise.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Fresh-eyes follow-up on PDF page geometry/theme wiring: re-read src/pdf.rs and tests/pdf_test.rs after the prior page-size/margin work. No production logic defect found in PageGeom clamping or PDF MediaBox/baseline threading. Found one reliability gap: the defensive library-API sanitization branch for pathological Theme.page values was not covered. Added tests/pdf_test.rs::pdf_sanitizes_pathological_theme_page_geometry to prove NaN/inf/negative/oversized margins cannot leak NaN/inf or negative content geometry into PDF output. Verification passed: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; focused UBS over src/pdf.rs/tests/pdf_test.rs reported 0 critical issues.

title: PDF: implement TeX-like vertical page builder
description: Replace line-overflow pagination with a vertical list/page builder using boxes/glue/penalties. Include best page break selection, club/widow/orphan penalties, keep-with-next headings, and bottom balancing policy. Acceptance: fixtures prevent headings stranded at page bottoms and single-line widows/orphans.
close_reason: Implemented a focused TeX-like vertical page builder over the existing PDF line layout. Each laid-out line now carries flow metadata (group, index/count, kind), and serialization uses a separate page-builder pass that treats line heights as vertical boxes plus gap glue. The break scorer prefers fuller pages but applies high penalties to breaks that strand a heading at the bottom of a page or split paragraph groups into one-line clubs/widows. Added structural PDF tests proving headings move with following content on small pages and five-line paragraphs split 3/2 rather than 4/1. Refactored layout state into LayoutCx/FlowSpec to keep clippy clean. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh.

title: PDF: implement booktabs-quality table layout
description: Implement measured table columns, per-cell paragraph layout, padding, subtle striping, booktabs-style rules, numeric/decimal alignment hooks, repeated headers, and sane page breaks. Acceptance: table fixtures with long cells paginate without overlap and repeat headers.
close_reason: Implemented booktabs-quality table layout slice: table cells now wrap inside measured columns, logical rows expand to the tallest wrapped cell, table flow metadata distinguishes headers/rules/body rows, and pagination repeats header/rule lines on continuation pages while reserving their height and avoiding stranded headers. Added regression tests for long-cell wrapping and repeated headers. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh.

title: PDF: implement syntax-highlighted code block layout
description: Render code blocks with token colors, background, padding, optional line numbers, line wrapping with hanging indent, and page breaks that never clip text. Acceptance: long code lines wrap deterministically and token colors match theme.
close_reason: Implemented syntax-highlighted PDF code block layout: fenced code now wraps long highlighted lines instead of clipping, preserves token fill classes across wrapped rows, uses a hanging continuation indent, keeps multi-page code-panel backgrounds anchored full-width, and exposes optional muted code line numbers through PdfOptions and fmd --pdf-line-numbers. Added PDF regressions for wrapped code/no clipping and line numbers, plus CLI contract coverage for capabilities/robot-docs/flag execution. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh.

title: PDF: implement polished blockquote/list/heading layout rules
description: Add heading before/after skips and keep rules, blockquote bars/background/padding, hanging list markers, aligned ordered markers, checkbox glyphs, nested indentation grid, and compact/loose list spacing. Acceptance: fixture PDFs avoid stranded bars/markers and match the theme spacing contract.
close_reason: Implemented PDF blockquote/list/heading polish slice: added level-aware heading before/after spacing while preserving keep-with-next behavior, drew subtle quote background panels behind existing gutter bars, and replaced inline list markers with a prefixed paragraph layout that uses a marker gutter and aligns wrapped continuation lines under list content. Added structural PDF regressions for quote background/bar styling and hanging list marker gutters. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh.

title: Parser conformance: support multiline reference titles
description:
Support the CommonMark reference-link-definition form where the optional title appears on the following line instead of the same line as the destination.

Background: 3uy covers reference-link conformance. The current parser handles one-line definitions like `[id]: /url "title"`, but it treats `[id]: /url` followed by an indented or unindented quoted title line as a visible paragraph instead of consuming it into the reference definition. CommonMark allows the title to be on the next line, with up to three leading spaces.

Scope: update clean-room reference-definition collection without adding production dependencies. Support double-quoted, single-quoted, and parenthesized next-line titles; preserve first-definition-wins behavior; leave malformed second-line titles visible as normal Markdown; keep source spans and diagnostics coherent by filtering consumed title lines out of rendered blocks.

Acceptance: parser conformance tests cover all three next-line title delimiters, first-definition-wins, malformed title line visibility, and spanned parsing not rendering consumed title lines. Run parser harnesses plus the standard Rust checks before closing.
close_reason: Implemented CommonMark multiline reference titles: reference-definition collection now consumes valid next-line double/single/parenthesized titles, keeps duplicate first-definition-wins behavior, leaves malformed/four-space title-like lines visible, and shares the consumed-line mask with spanned parsing. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo test --test parser_conformance -- --nocapture; cargo test --test parser_spans -- --nocapture; scripts/parser-diff.sh; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh.

title: Quality gate: prove deterministic CLI and HTML output
description: Add a fast local/CI script that proves stable bytes for agent-facing JSON surfaces and HTML rendering. Scope: render the same Markdown twice, compare HTML bytes, compare capabilities/doctor/robot-triage JSON bytes, avoid shared Cargo target-dir contention, document the gate, and keep it independent from PDF until PDF bytes exist.
close_reason: Added deterministic-output gate for current stable surfaces. scripts/check-determinism.sh compares capabilities --json, doctor --json, --robot-triage, HTML stdout, and HTML file bytes across repeated runs using repo-local target/fmd-checks artifacts. CI now runs it; README/plan/changelog document it. Proof: bash -n scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; git diff --check on determinism-gate paths. Full test/clippy gate is currently blocked by a separate uncommitted PDF implementation lane that changes the PDF CLI contract and is being coordinated over Agent Mail.

title: Security: add malformed-input no-panic render harness
description: Add dependency-free security regression coverage proving malformed/adversarial Markdown inputs do not panic through parse, spanned parse, HTML render, or PDF render. Scope: raw HTML/script text, malformed links/images/references, unclosed fences/emphasis/html, pathological table/list markers, deeply repetitive delimiters, long code-ish lines, mixed Unicode/control characters that are valid UTF-8, and both default escaping plus allow_raw_html path where relevant. Acceptance: cargo test includes a security harness using catch_unwind and the full policy/WASM/determinism gates remain green.
close_reason: Added dependency-free malformed/adversarial Markdown no-panic harness across parse_markdown, parse_markdown_spanned, render_html, render_pdf, and explicit allow_raw_html mode. Corpus covers raw script text, unsafe URLs, malformed links/images/references, unclosed fences/emphasis/html, pathological tables/lists, long delimiter/code inputs, deep blockquote-ish text, and Unicode/control characters valid in UTF-8. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh.

title: Security: enforce CLI input-size limits for untrusted Markdown
description: Add an agent-discoverable CLI ingress guard for untrusted Markdown so file/stdin/--text renders cannot accidentally allocate unbounded memory before parsing. Scope: default high ceiling, --max-input-bytes override, byte-count checks for --text/file/stdin before parser entry, stable JSON/human error that reports only sizes and user-supplied paths, tests for --text and stdin/file refusal, and README/capabilities/robot-docs updates. Parent: jno security posture. This is intentionally a CLI boundary guard; the pure render core remains synchronous/std-only and does not grow filesystem/runtime assumptions.
close_reason: Added CLI ingress resource limits for untrusted Markdown. Render commands now default to a 64 MiB maximum and expose --max-input-bytes; --text, stdin, and file inputs are byte-limited before parser entry. Oversized inputs exit 66 with JSON/human input_error diagnostics, no document bytes on stdout, and no raw input echoed. Updated capabilities/robot-docs/README and added process-boundary tests for all three ingress paths. Parent jno remains open for remaining security hardening/fuzzing/core budget work. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh.

title: Parser conformance: fix URI and email autolinks

title: Implement clean-room syntax highlighting for documentation code blocks
description: Create a small deterministic syntax highlighter instead of depending on syntect. Start with documentation-heavy languages: Rust, Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, Bash, JSON, TOML, YAML, SQL, HTML/CSS, and Markdown. Emit shared token streams consumed by HTML and PDF. Unknown languages fall back to plain code. Include golden token/output fixtures and performance tests.
close_reason: Completed clean-room syntax highlighting feature. The renderer now supports the requested documentation languages (Rust, Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, Bash, JSON, TOML, YAML, SQL, HTML/XML, CSS/SCSS/SASS, Markdown, plus C/C++ and Go), unknown languages fall back to plain escaped/monochrome code, HTML consumes the shared highlighter token stream, PDF fenced code blocks consume the same token stream with deterministic syntax colors while preserving selectability and compact embedded-font output, and c4k.3 added golden token fixtures plus non-flaky performance/stress proof. Verification across child slices included cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --quiet, scripts/highlight-proof.sh, scripts/check-policy.sh, scripts/check-wasm-core.sh, scripts/check-determinism.sh, and scripts/parser-diff.sh.

title: Syntax highlighting: add HTML/CSS/Markdown lexers and fixtures
description:
Complete the missing documentation-language slice of c4k without adding dependencies.

Background: c4k asks for a small deterministic clean-room highlighter covering Rust, Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, Bash, JSON, TOML, YAML, SQL, HTML/CSS, and Markdown, with golden token/output fixtures and performance coverage. Existing code already has a dependency-free tokenizer for Rust/Python/JS/TS/Bash/JSON/TOML/YAML/SQL plus extra C/Go support, and HTML emission safely escapes highlighted spans. Remaining requested languages are HTML, CSS, and Markdown, and the tests should make that support explicit.

Scope: add focused rules/aliases for html/xhtml/xml, css/scss/sass, and markdown/md/mdown; ensure strings/comments/tags/selectors/properties/markdown markers get stable token classes through the existing generic tokenizer where possible; add unit tests that prove support, span tiling, HTML escaping, and rendered span output for these languages. Keep the core std-only and WASM-safe.

Out of scope: PDF code-block colorization/shared styled token consumption, which should remain a separate child under c4k because the PDF renderer currently renders fenced code as monochrome mono text panels.

Acceptance: tests demonstrate the new languages are supported and produce deterministic escaped span output; standard Rust gates pass.
close_reason: Implemented missing clean-room HTML/XHTML/XML, CSS/SCSS/SASS, and Markdown lexer support behind the existing highlight API. Added tests for aliases, UTF-8 byte-span tiling, token classification, escaped rendered spans, unknown-language fallback, and a Markdown inline #/> scanner-stall regression. Verification: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --test highlight_test -- --nocapture; cargo test --quiet; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh.

title: Syntax highlighting: feed shared code token streams into PDF output
description:
Complete the remaining PDF-facing scope of c4k.

Background: the HTML path now consumes the clean-room highlighter for common documentation languages, including HTML/CSS/Markdown. The PDF renderer still lays fenced code blocks out as monochrome mono text panels. c4k explicitly asked for shared token streams consumed by both HTML and PDF, so the parent feature should not close until PDF code blocks can use the same deterministic classification without compromising compactness, text selectability, or WASM/core purity.

Scope: define the minimal PDF-side style mapping for Tok classes; carry highlighted spans through fenced code block layout; preserve current selectable text and ToUnicode behavior; keep panels compact and deterministic; add PDF structural/golden tests that prove highlighted code still embeds valid fonts/text and does not regress file size unexpectedly.

Acceptance: PDF code blocks for at least Rust, HTML, CSS, and Markdown use distinct deterministic token colors/styles derived from the shared highlighter; unknown languages remain monochrome plain escaped text; cargo fmt/check/clippy/test plus policy/WASM/determinism gates pass.
close_reason: Implemented PDF consumption of the shared clean-room highlighter for fenced code blocks. Supported languages now split code lines into mono PDF text segments colored from highlight::Tok while preserving existing glyph shaping, ToUnicode selectability, deterministic page streams, and unknown-language monochrome fallback. Added structural PDF tests for Rust/HTML/CSS/Markdown syntax colors, unknown-language fallback, mono font use, compactness, and kept embedded-font tests green. Verification: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --quiet; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh.

title: Syntax highlighting: add golden and performance proof fixtures
description:
Finish the remaining c4k proof work before closing the parent feature.

Background: c4k now has clean-room syntax highlighting for the requested documentation languages, HTML output consumes the token stream, and PDF output consumes the same token stream for fenced code blocks. The parent bead also explicitly asks for golden token/output fixtures and performance tests. Current unit tests cover classification and rendered structural output, but there is not yet a durable highlighter-focused golden/performance proof artifact.

Scope: add deterministic golden token fixtures for representative Rust, HTML, CSS, Markdown, and unknown-language samples; add a dependency-free performance smoke or script that exercises a larger mixed-language Markdown/code corpus and records stable throughput/allocation-adjacent signals without flaky wall-clock assertions; document how the proof is run.

Acceptance: golden fixtures fail on token-stream drift; performance proof runs without external dependencies or timing flakiness; standard gates pass. After this closes, evaluate whether parent c4k can close.
close_reason: Added deterministic highlighter token golden fixtures for Rust, HTML, CSS, Markdown, and unknown-language fallback; added a large mixed-language deterministic stress proof without wall-clock thresholds; added scripts/highlight-proof.sh to run the highlighter proof plus PDF shared-token consumption proof. Verification: scripts/highlight-proof.sh; cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --quiet; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh.

title: Implement Knuth-Plass paragraph breaking and hyphenation
description: Build the LaTeX-grade paragraph engine: boxes/glue/penalties, demerit optimization, badness tuning, kerning-aware line widths, Liang/TeX hyphenation patterns, optional justification, ragged setting, leading/baseline rules, and microtypographic protrusion experiments. Include fixtures measuring line breaks, hyphen counts, raggedness, and deterministic output.
close_reason: Closed after all paragraph-engine children landed: styled inline runs (dy5.1), box/glue/penalty IR (dy5.2), first Knuth-Plass optimizer (dy5.3), Liang hyphenation core (dy5.4), microtype hooks (dy5.5), and full TeX English corpus (dy5.6). Remaining use in actual PDF pagination belongs to 49d/fep integration beads.

title: PDF: preserve styled inline glyph runs instead of flattening text
description: Represent Markdown inlines as styled glyph/text runs for PDF layout: body/bold/italic/code/link/color/source span. Stop flattening inline styling to plain strings before layout. Acceptance: paragraphs/headings/lists/code/link text emit typed runs consumed by line/page builders, with tests proving bold/italic/code survive into layout.
close_reason: Implemented in layout.rs: StyledText/StyledRun/TextStyle, TextBox.runs, paragraph_items_from_inlines/styled_text, and tests proving bold/italic/code/link boundaries survive into paragraph boxes.

title: Layout: implement box/glue/penalty paragraph model

close_reason: Implemented TeX-style paragraph item primitives: TextBox, Glue, Penalty, ParagraphItem, interword glue, final forced-break paragraph constructor, fixed-point widths, and unit tests for shape/width/glue behavior.

title: Layout: implement Knuth-Plass active breakpoint optimizer

title: Layout: add microtypography protrusion and expansion hooks

close_reason: Vendored the 4,938-token TUG hyph-utf8 American English TeX pattern corpus with preserved source notice, wired Hyphenator::english() to apply encoded TeX patterns through allocation-free stack parsing, exposed pattern token count, added representation fixture coverage, and verified native/WASM/policy/determinism gates.

title: Implement compact deterministic PDF writer
description: Write our own minimal PDF backend for this renderer. Scope: deterministic object numbering, catalog/pages/content/font objects, text operators over shaped glyph runs, embedded subset fonts, FlateDecode streams, metadata controlled by options/SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH, stable output bytes, structural validation, file-size benchmarks, and tiny fixture PDFs for primitive blocks.
close_reason: Completed the compact deterministic PDF writer parent scope: deterministic object/cross-reference layout, page/content/font objects, embedded Type0/CIDFontType2 subset fonts, ToUnicode maps, real metrics, focused GPOS kerning and GSUB ligatures, PDF block/page layout, syntax-highlighted code, tables/lists/blockquotes, link annotations, outlines, deterministic Info metadata with title/author and SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH control, tagged-PDF structure v0, FlateDecode font/page stream compression, structural tests, file-size ledgers, and determinism gates. The open fep.6 dot-notation child is a follow-on performance wave reparented under qw1; it is intentionally not a blocker for closing the core PDF writer. Final proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh.

title: Land PDF MVP CLI contract and determinism gate
description: Align the project around the first working clean-room PDF writer without pretending the full typography engine is done. Scope: keep the deterministic Base-14 PDF v0, update fmd doctor/capabilities/robot-docs/--robot-triage from PDF refusal to available_v0_base14, replace obsolete exit-70 tests with PDF success/structure tests, extend determinism checking to PDF bytes, update README/CHANGELOG/plan, and leave Knuth-Plass/font-subsetting roadmap beads open.

title: PDF: Type0 CIDFontType2 subset embedding with ToUnicode

Existing tests covered embedded subsets, font slots, GPOS kerning, ligature ToUnicode, italic descriptor metadata, and deterministic tiny output; added tests/pdf_embed_test.rs::type0_identity_h_fonts_keep_non_winansi_text_selectable to prove accented/Greek non-WinAnsi scalars survive in ToUnicode. comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Fresh-eyes PDF embedded-font metadata bug fixed by SilverLeopard: the PDF FontDescriptor /ItalicAngle predicate covered F_ITALIC but not F_BOLDITALIC, even though bold-italic is an italic face. Root cause: style slot metadata was encoded as a single equality instead of an italic-style class predicate. Fix: src/pdf.rs now assigns /ItalicAngle -12 for both F_ITALIC and F_BOLDITALIC. Added tests/pdf_embed_test.rs::pdf_marks_italic_and_bold_italic_descriptors_as_italic to require two italic descriptors when rendering italic and bold-italic text. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; focused UBS over edited files had 0 critical issues.

title: Perf: optimize PDF shaping, ToUnicode, and serialization hot paths
description:
Performance wave 1: optimize PDF shaping, ToUnicode, and serialization hot paths after evidence-based attribution.

Background and evidence:
- Current-head release-perf harness (2026-06-27, 20 iterations) ranks pdf-large as the largest measured in-process cost: p95 about 61.169 ms, mean about 60.158 ms, rendering a pre-parsed large mixed Markdown document to PDF.
- Focused perf stat over pdf-large showed high IPC (about 3.60), low branch misses (about 0.59%), and about 10.07% cache misses among cache references. First-principles interpretation: the CPU is not stalled on branch prediction; the highest-EV initial work is doing less repeated work and fewer allocations/formatting operations.
- The expected hot areas are src/pdf.rs serialize/build_pdf/kerned_tj/widths_array/tounicode_cmap plus repeated shaping, BTree lookups, String construction, hex/decimal formatting, PDF object buffering, and subset-map lookups.

Goal:
Make the current embedded-font PDF writer materially faster and smaller while preserving deterministic bytes, selectable text, ligature ToUnicode behavior, file-size discipline, and WASM/no-default cleanliness.

Non-goals:
- Do not change visual output casually while doing a perf rewrite.
- Do not add dependencies.
- Do not add unsafe or CPU-specific code here; SIMD belongs to qw1.5 only after the scanner proof gate.

Implementation principles:
- Add profiling-only stage attribution first: layout, used-slot scan, glyph collection, shaping, subsetting, ToUnicode, page content generation, PDF object serialization, xref/trailer.
- Optimize one lever per commit.
- Prefer append-only Vec<u8> writers and pre-sized buffers over hot format!/String loops.
- Cache shaped segment results within one render only when deterministic and memory-bounded.
- Replace hot BTree lookups with deterministic dense/sorted representations only after attribution proves the lookup is hot.

Required proof:
- Golden PDF bytes either remain identical or the intended byte change is documented with structural/visual/copy-text proof.
- scripts/check-determinism.sh remains green.
- scripts/check-wasm-core.sh remains green for core changes.
- Performance claims must include same-host before/after p50/p95/p99, output bytes, and an isomorphism note.
- Any PDF byte-size claim must include fixture file-size ledger evidence.

Detailed child beads under this track own attribution, writer primitives, buffer sizing, shaping cache, subset map layout, ToUnicode/widths generation, and PDF perf/e2e verification.
close_reason: Parent closeout: the decomposed PDF performance wave is complete. Implementation children fep.6.1 through fep.6.7 are all closed: stage attribution, deterministic append-only writers, render scratch/pre-sizing, per-render shaped segment cache, gated subset-map compaction rejection, ToUnicode/width generation optimization, and end-to-end PDF perf proof script. Final proof artifacts include tests/artifacts/perf/fep.6.7-smoke2 and tests/artifacts/perf/fep.6.7-fresh-eyes; latest verification in this session passed cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, scripts/check-policy.sh, scripts/check-determinism.sh, scripts/check-wasm-core.sh, scripts/check-wasm-package.sh, and git diff --check. Closing parent to reflect completed child graph and unblock downstream layout re-profile work.

comments.author: PeachCove

First move should be profiling-only stage attribution, then one-lever reductions in repeated work: fast append writers instead of hot format!/String loops, pre-sized buffers, shaped-run caching, and deterministic subset-map layout. See docs/PERFORMANCE_ACCELERATION_PLAN.md.
