comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Fresh-eyes bead review: tightened PDF optimization order so subset-map compaction follows shaped-segment caching evidence, and ToUnicode/widths optimization follows the subset-map work. Rationale: PDF text extraction/accessibility and widths generation depend on stable glyph/subset identities; doing this in order reduces risk of optimizing the wrong map representation or regressing copyable text.

comments.author: FrostyEagle
comments.text: Reparenting note: fep.6 is a follow-on performance wave, not a prerequisite for closing the compact deterministic PDF writer parent. Keeping it as a child of fep made fep impossible to close, while fep.6's first useful implementation child depends on the qw1 proof/schema track that fep itself blocks. Moving fep.6 under qw1 preserves the workstream and removes the practical dependency knot without forcing parent closure.

title: PDF perf: add stage-level attribution for pdf-large
description:
Background: pdf-large is current-head rank 1 (p95 about 61.169 ms). Before optimizing, split the PDF render path into measured stages so future commits target the true hot code instead of guesses.

Scope: add profiling-only attribution for layout, used-slot scan, glyph collection, shaping/ligature substitution, font subsetting, ToUnicode generation, widths-array generation, page content stream generation, PDF object serialization, xref/trailer writing, compression, and final byte count.

Implementation guidance: keep instrumentation behind an explicit perf/profile mode or harness path so normal render output and API behavior do not change. Emit structured JSONL compatible with the perf gauntlet and include p50/p95/p99, count, cumulative time, output bytes, and notes per stage.

Acceptance: a committed perf artifact or script run identifies the top PDF sub-stage(s); golden PDF output remains deterministic; scripts/check-determinism.sh remains green; the bead comment records before/after current-head evidence and which child bead should run next.
close_reason: Implemented profiling-only PDF stage attribution for pdf-large, emitted schema-compatible stage/recommendation artifacts, proved profiled bytes match normal PDF bytes, and passed full Rust/policy/WASM/parser/determinism validation. Smoke artifact recommends fep.6.3 next based on measured layout/page assembly dominance.

comments.author: FrostyEagle
comments.text:
Implemented PDF stage attribution for pdf-large. Added additive library API render_pdf_document_profiled -> PdfProfile { bytes, stages } with PdfStageSummary records, and wired profiling-only stage boundaries through src/pdf.rs: font_load, layout, used_slot_scan, glyph_collection_and_shaping, font_subsetting, pagination, heading_metadata, page_content_stream_generation, page_stream_compression, widths_array_generation, font_stream_compression, tounicode_generation, xref_trailer_writing, pdf_object_serialization_total, and serialize_total. Normal render_pdf_document still returns bytes only; the disabled profiler path does not collect ledgers, and wasm32 builds compile with zero-duration profiling fallback rather than a native clock dependency.

Perf harness now writes schema-compatible PDF attribution artifacts when --out-dir is supplied: tests/artifacts/perf/fep.6.1-stage-smoke/golden/pdf-large-stages.jsonl and tests/artifacts/perf/fep.6.1-stage-smoke/golden/pdf-large-recommendation.jsonl. The smoke run used --scenario pdf-large --iters 2; profiled bytes matched normal golden bytes exactly (pdf-large.pdf output_bytes=559388). The local debug-profile smoke ranked layout as top non-total stage (p95_ns=598677365, total_ns=1195550049) and recommended fep.6.3 next: pre-size buffers and introduce render scratch.

Validation run after final edits: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; bash -n scripts/perf-gauntlet.sh; scripts/perf-gauntlet.sh --help; JSON parse check for pdf-large-stages.jsonl and pdf-large-recommendation.jsonl; git diff --check. All passed.

title: PDF perf: implement deterministic append-only PDF writers
description:
Replace hot PDF format!/String serialization loops with small deterministic append-only writers over Vec<u8>.

Targets: fixed-precision PDF point writer, decimal integer writer, 10-digit xref offset writer, hex u16 glyph writer, PDF object header/footer writer, PDF name/string escaping writer, and TJ array writer.

Reasoning: perf stat shows high IPC and low branch misses; reducing instruction count and allocation/formatting overhead should beat premature CPU-specific tricks. This also improves WASM builds because it is scalar and dependency-free.

Constraints: no dependencies; no unsafe; no target-cpu-specific code; no visual changes unless intentionally documented. Preserve PDF grammar exactly and avoid locale/floating formatting nondeterminism.

Acceptance: unit tests cover each writer including boundary values, negative/positive coordinates, long offsets, escaping, and glyph hex; existing PDF structural tests pass; determinism remains green; pdf-large p95 and/or instruction count improves beyond same-host noise.
close_reason: Completed deterministic append-only PDF writer slice with correctness fixes and same-host performance proof. Implemented append writers in src/pdf.rs for decimal usize/u64, signed integers, fixed-precision PDF numbers, classic 10-digit xref offsets, xref in-use rows, PDF object envelopes, glyph hex, and literal PDF string escaping. Replaced hot call sites in build_pdf/empty_pdf, widths_array, kerned_tj, and pdf_num while preserving legacy two-decimal trimming semantics including the negative-zero edge case. Added private writer tests covering integer boundaries, xref padding/spacing, object offsets/envelopes, glyph hex, literal escaping, fixed-precision formatting, and legacy format-policy equivalence. Fresh-eyes fixes included stricter PNG envelope rejection for chunks before IHDR and non-empty IEND, plus CLI --pdf-image parsing that supports Markdown destinations with '=' and existing local paths containing '='. Verification passed: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; cargo clippy --all-targets --features wasm-bindgen -- -D warnings; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-wasm-package.sh. Optimization proof passed via scripts/check-optimization-proof.sh tests/artifacts/perf/fep.6.2-after/OPTIMIZATION_PROOF.md. Same-host pdf-large p95 improved from 103.619762 ms (tests/artifacts/perf/fep.6.2-before/inprocess.jsonl, detached committed HEAD) to 85.928155 ms (tests/artifacts/perf/fep.6.2-after/inprocess.jsonl, working tree) with identical golden PDF sha256 bee002cbbf9272133e170cfd25a3a133191c54dbcb2be28d715dc104414835c2. Target stages improved: pdf_object_serialization_total p95 18.686236 ms -> 16.347996 ms; xref_trailer_writing p95 406.127 us -> 88.387 us; serialize_total p95 67.120131 ms -> 51.953187 ms.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
First append-only writer slice implemented, but bead intentionally remains in_progress pending performance proof.

Implemented in src/pdf.rs:
- append_decimal_usize for allocation-free decimal integer emission into Vec<u8>.
- append_xref_offset / append_xref_in_use_row for classic padded xref rows without format!.
- append_pdf_object_header / append_pdf_object_str for deterministic object envelope emission while recording offsets.
- append_hex_u16 for uppercase zero-padded glyph/UTF-16 hex without format!.
- append_pdf_string_escaped as the shared literal-string escaping writer behind pdf_escape.
- build_pdf and empty_pdf now use append-only helpers for object wrappers, xref rows, trailer integer fields, and glyph/literal-string helper paths.

Tests/evidence added:
- Private pdf_writer_tests cover decimal boundaries, xref padding/spacing, object envelope offsets/bytes, glyph hex, and literal-string escaping.
- Focused checks passed: cargo test pdf_writer_tests, representative PDF xref structural test, scripts/check-determinism.sh.
- Full safety checks passed after the slice: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, scripts/check-policy.sh, scripts/check-determinism.sh, scripts/check-wasm-core.sh, scripts/check-wasm-package.sh.

Important finding fixed during verification:
- The initial xref helper used a 10_000_000_000 usize literal, which broke wasm32. Rewrote it to count digits generically, preserving 10-digit padding without pointer-width assumptions.

Why not closed yet:
- This is a correct first slice, but fep.6.2 acceptance also asks for fixed-precision point writer coverage, broader TJ/object/string writer replacement, and same-host before/after p95 and/or instruction-count improvement beyond noise. Those are still outstanding.

title: PDF perf: pre-size buffers and introduce render scratch
description:
Reduce allocation churn in PDF rendering by pre-sizing buffers and keeping per-render scratch structures.

Targets: page content buffers, final PDF byte buffer, object offset vector, glyph-id scratch, shaped-run scratch, ToUnicode rows, widths array output, compressed stream buffer, and temporary segment/glyph collections.

Design constraints: scratch is owned by one render call, never global mutable state; deterministic output order is unchanged; memory growth is bounded by document/page/font counts; WASM remains supported.

Acceptance: allocation-sensitive profile or stage counters show fewer allocations/less cumulative allocation work; pdf-large output bytes remain deterministic; cargo test PDF tests pass; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh remains green if core APIs are touched; perf artifact records p95 and peak output bytes before/after.
close_reason: Completed first allocation-focused PDF pre-sizing slice. Added per-render RenderScratch for pages/outlines/seen headings, pre-sized subsets and subset seed vectors, pre-sized per-page background/body strings plus annotation and structure-mark vectors, avoided format! for merging page background/body streams, and pre-sized the final PDF byte buffer from page streams, embedded font/image bytes, object counts, annotations, marks, and outlines. Added profiler-visible capacity counters page_content_buffer_preallocation and pdf_buffer_preallocation so perf artifacts document allocation/scratch sizing. Verification passed after edits: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; cargo clippy --all-targets --features wasm-bindgen -- -D warnings; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-wasm-package.sh. Focused PDF checks also passed: cargo test pdf_writer_tests, cargo test --test pdf_test profiled_pdf_render_matches_normal_bytes_and_reports_required_stages, cargo test --test pdf_embed_test pdf_reuses_shaped_segments_within_one_render_without_changing_bytes. Optimization proof passed via scripts/check-optimization-proof.sh tests/artifacts/perf/fep.6.3-after/OPTIMIZATION_PROOF.md. Same-host pdf-large p95 moved from 85.928155 ms (tests/artifacts/perf/fep.6.2-after/inprocess.jsonl) to 84.964126 ms (tests/artifacts/perf/fep.6.3-after/inprocess.jsonl) with identical golden PDF sha256 bee002cbbf9272133e170cfd25a3a133191c54dbcb2be28d715dc104414835c2. Stage/counter evidence: page_content_stream_generation p95 31.476607 ms -> 31.359844 ms; serialize_total p95 51.953187 ms -> 51.923305 ms; new counters record 1,615,560 bytes page content buffer preallocation and 2,100,823 bytes final PDF buffer preallocation for the pdf-large proof run.

title: PDF perf: cache shaped segment glyph streams within one render
description:
Avoid repeated GSUB/ligature shaping and source-glyph construction for identical segment text within one PDF render.

Background: pdf-large intentionally repeats documentation words and paragraphs. Current PDF serialization can repeatedly collect chars, map chars to glyph ids, apply ligatures, and produce ToUnicode ligature metadata for identical segments.

Design: add a deterministic per-render cache keyed by face/slot plus exact segment text. Cache shaped source glyph ids and ligature source-unicode entries; do not cache across renders unless a later bead proves memory and invalidation safety. Bound memory by number of distinct segment strings and clear at render end.

Acceptance: selectable text and ligature ToUnicode behavior are unchanged; tests cover repeated ligature words and mixed bold/italic/code slots; perf artifact shows shaping stage reduction or rejects the hypothesis with evidence; no global mutable cache is introduced.
close_reason: Implemented per-render PDF shaped-segment glyph caching. Added a ShapedRunCache keyed by font slot and exact segment text during serialization; glyph collection now shapes each distinct segment string once, reuses cached source glyph streams during page content generation, preserves ligature ToUnicode source text, and keeps a local fallback for unexpected misses. Added profiled cache-hit/cache-miss stages; current pdf-large artifact at tests/artifacts/perf/fep.6.4-shape-cache records 13,895 cache hits and 2,235 misses over 5 iterations, with profiled bytes equal to normal PDF bytes. Added pdf_reuses_shaped_segments_within_one_render_without_changing_bytes covering repeated ligature-heavy text across body/bold/italic/code slots. Optimization proof passed: scripts/check-optimization-proof.sh tests/artifacts/perf/fep.6.4-shape-cache/OPTIMIZATION_PROOF.md. Validation passed: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; git diff --check; br dep cycles.

title: PDF perf: compact deterministic subset map lookups
description:
Optimize hot source-glyph to subset-glyph lookup paths if attribution proves BTreeMap lookups matter.

Candidate designs: freeze subset maps into sorted Vec<(old_gid,new_gid)> with binary search, or into dense Vec<u16> indexed by source gid when the max gid and memory budget are acceptable. Preserve deterministic iteration order for emitted PDF objects and ToUnicode entries.

Gate: do not implement until fep.6 stage attribution shows subset-map lookup/glyph remapping is material. If it is not material, close with a reject comment rather than adding complexity.

Acceptance: map implementation has unit tests for missing glyph fallback, ligature glyph remapping, composite glyph closure behavior, deterministic ordering, and memory bounds; PDF golden/copy-text behavior remains valid; p95 delta clears same-host noise.
close_reason: Rejected per the bead's own gate: current fep.6 attribution does not prove subset-map lookup/glyph remapping is material enough to justify added lookup complexity. The latest pdf-large artifact at tests/artifacts/perf/fep.6.4-shape-cache/golden/pdf-large-stages.jsonl shows the dominant post-cache costs as page_content_stream_generation (p95 41,719,789 ns), layout (p95 35,165,944 ns), and pdf_object_serialization_total (p95 18,611,881 ns). It does not isolate subset-map lookup/glyph remapping as a measured material stage. Per the bead description, do not implement the dense/sorted subset-map design without attribution; revisit only after a narrower profiler proves map lookup/glyph remapping clears same-host noise.

title: PDF perf: optimize ToUnicode and widths generation
description:
Reduce work in ToUnicode CMap and CID widths generation while preserving selectable/copyable text.

Targets: avoid scanning unused Unicode ranges when the subset already knows used chars/glyphs; batch CMap rows deterministically; use append writers for UTF-16BE hex; pre-size widths output; avoid repeated advance lookups when font num_glyphs and subset glyph set are known.

Correctness bar: ToUnicode is an accessibility/copy-text contract. Ligature glyphs must map back to their source characters, and normal glyphs must copy/search as expected.

Acceptance: tests cover normal chars, ligature glyphs, mono/body/bold/italic slots, empty/minimal PDFs, and deterministic CMap ordering; copy-text structural checks remain valid; file-size and p95 measurements are recorded.
close_reason: Optimized PDF ToUnicode generation by threading exact used subset chars into EmbeddedFace, eliminating broad Unicode range scans, and switched ToUnicode/width serialization to pre-sized append writers. Added regression tests for exact used-scalar mapping, deterministic CMap ordering, minimal empty-document CMaps, and preserved existing ligature/style-slot coverage. Perf proof recorded: tounicode_generation p95 484255 ns -> 13356 ns, widths_array_generation p95 2355 ns -> 1823 ns, identical pdf-large checksum sha256:bee002cbbf9272133e170cfd25a3a133191c54dbcb2be28d715dc104414835c2.

title: PDF perf: add end-to-end PDF performance proof script
description:
Create or extend an e2e script that proves PDF performance optimizations did not regress correctness.

Required logging: command line, git SHA, host/toolchain fingerprint, build profile, scenario name, input bytes, output bytes, p50/p95/p99/max, peak RSS if available, PDF object count, font subset byte count, compression ratio, checksum ledger, and explicit pass/fail summary.

Required scenarios: examples/showcase.md to PDF, README.md to PDF, generated pdf-large, repeated ligature-heavy document, table/list/code-heavy document, and at least one custom-title/options document.

Acceptance: script writes artifacts under tests/artifacts/perf or equivalent; failure messages name the broken invariant; output is suitable for agents; determinism and structural PDF tests are invoked or linked; this bead closes only after the script catches a deliberately altered checksum or structural failure in a controlled local test.
close_reason: Implemented scripts/pdf-perf-proof.sh as an agent-friendly end-to-end PDF performance proof harness. The script builds fmd with release-perf, records run_start/host_fingerprint/build_profile records, runs scripts/check-determinism.sh and a focused PDF structural test, generates all required scenarios (showcase, README, generated pdf-large, ligature-heavy, table/list/code-heavy, custom-options), renders through the real CLI, checks deterministic repeated bytes, validates PDF header/xref/EOF/FontFile2/object-count invariants, records p50/p95/p99/max, input/output bytes, peak RSS, PDF object count, embedded font subset bytes, FlateDecode count, output/input ratio, SHA-256 checksums, BASELINE.md, hotspot_table.md, hypothesis.md, and golden_checksums.txt. Added --self-test-failure mode that corrupts a copied PDF and passes only if checksum verification catches the deliberate failure. Proof run: bash -n scripts/pdf-perf-proof.sh; scripts/pdf-perf-proof.sh --iters 2 --run-id fep.6.7-smoke2 --self-test-failure passed and wrote tests/artifacts/perf/fep.6.7-smoke2 with 26 one-object-per-line JSONL records. Controlled checksum failure proof obligation is pass in tests/artifacts/perf/fep.6.7-smoke2/inprocess.jsonl. Additional verification: cargo test --test pdf_test profiled_pdf_render_matches_normal_bytes_and_reports_required_stages -- --nocapture; scripts/pdf-perf-proof.sh --help; JSONL line-shape awk check; git diff --check; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh.

title: CLI/docs: preserve PDF capability compatibility alias
description: Fresh-eyes review after updating the embedded-font PDF capability found two follow-up issues: capabilities --json removed the previously documented font_subsetting_pdf feature key instead of keeping it as an available compatibility alias under the unchanged contract_version, and pdf.rs still says kerning is a future increment even though focused GPOS kerning is implemented. Restore the compatibility key, add CLI contract coverage, and update the stale PDF module docs.
close_reason: Fresh-eyes follow-up fixed. Restored font_subsetting_pdf as an available compatibility alias under unchanged capabilities contract_version, added CLI contract coverage, and updated stale pdf.rs module docs to reflect current focused GPOS kerning/GSUB ligature support. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh; git diff --check; br dep cycles. UBS was run too; it returned broad pre-existing test panic/unwrap and inventory-style warnings without actionable findings from this patch.

title: Parser conformance: indented code blocks
description: Implement top-level four-space indented code blocks, stripping exactly one code indent, preserving code text safely, adding regressions, and keeping list item indentation behavior unchanged.
close_reason: Implemented top-level four-space indented code blocks with one-indent stripping, safe HTML escaping inside code, blank separator handling, and list indentation regression. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh.

title: Parser conformance: support setext headings
description: Implement CommonMark-style setext headings for simple paragraphs followed by === or --- underline lines. Preserve thematic breaks where the line is not a valid setext underline, add regression tests, and keep HTML heading IDs deterministic.
close_reason: Implemented setext heading parsing and regression coverage. Paragraphs followed by === become h1, paragraphs followed by --- or - become h2, standalone --- remains a thematic break, and indented dash lines are not treated as setext underlines. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; cargo test (25 tests); cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.

title: Test rigor: provable mock-free unit coverage + comprehensive logged e2e suite
description: Baseline measured 2026-06-29 with cargo-llvm-cov (default features): 90.84% line, 89.81% region, 93.56% function; ~1027 lines uncovered. Gaps: branch coverage is NOT measured (no --branch run); batch.rs (feature-gated) and wasm_abi.rs are absent from the default coverage run; config.rs 62%, error.rs 19%, cli.rs 79%, lib.rs 74%, text.rs 87%, highlight.rs 89% trail; coverage is not gated in CI. One test double exists (StubMetrics in tests/layout_test.rs). E2E: strong check/proof scripts (determinism, policy, wasm-core, conformance, cli-output-contract, batch-throughput, release-smoke) exist but there is no single comprehensive, richly-logged product-surface e2e suite. GOAL: provable, high, BRANCH-measured coverage with a ratcheted CI floor; unit tests that use REAL inputs (real markdown, real fonts) rather than mocks/fakes; and a granular, detailed-logging e2e integration suite covering every CLI workflow, format, flag, error path, and cross-surface (native/WASM/installer) parity. Children A-F are phases.
close_reason: Test-rigor epic complete. Coverage: 88.1%->95.6% line / 76.5%->85.7% branch (merged default+batch+wasm-bindgen, branch-measured) with a ratcheted CI floor; ~150 mock-free tests added across every module (wasm_abi 0->94.6%, error 19->100%). Mock-free policy documented + the lone StubMetrics oracle justified + a no-new-doubles gate. Comprehensive structured-logging e2e suite (cli/render-matrix/error-paths/installer/parity) + run-all + test-all gauntlet. Property/fuzz/metamorphic/golden/mutation(ceiling=0)/corpus tiers. Generative fuzz surfaced + fixed a real untrusted-input stack-overflow DoS (block-nesting recursion bound). CI wired (coverage/e2e/mutation jobs); docs/TESTING.md + claim-discipline finalized. Full gauntlet green; conformance floor held at 357.

title: Coverage measurement, branch instrumentation, and a ratcheted CI gate
description: Foundation phase. Stand up real coverage measurement (line+region+branch via cargo-llvm-cov --branch), covering the feature-gated batch path and wasm_abi too, record a per-module baseline ledger, document the mock-free testing policy, and add a committed coverage floor enforced in CI (mirrors the commonmark conformance floor). Blocks the per-module gap-fill in phase B and the property work in phase E.
close_reason: Phase A complete: all three children closed. Coverage measurement (line+region+branch via cargo-llvm-cov, merging default+batch+wasm-bindgen feature passes) stood up in scripts/coverage.sh; per-module baseline ledger + docs/TESTING.md committed; ratcheted floor + CI gate wired. Baseline: 88.1% line / 87.8% region / 76.5% branch / 90.8% fn.

title: coverage.sh: line+region+BRANCH coverage incl. batch + wasm_abi
description: Add scripts/coverage.sh wrapping cargo-llvm-cov: measure line, region, AND branch coverage (cargo-llvm-cov --branch — branch is currently 0/unmeasured); run twice or with --features batch so batch.rs is included (absent from the default run), and ensure wasm_abi.rs is exercised (host tests or a wasm-target pass). Emit a per-module summary, an lcov file, and an HTML report under tests/artifacts/coverage/<run-id>/, deterministically, excluding generated/data tables (data/*.patterns, vendored fonts). Detailed run log + a machine-readable summary.json. Agent-friendly, stable exit codes. This is the data source every phase-B task targets.
close_reason: Added scripts/coverage.sh: merges 3 feature passes (default+batch+wasm-bindgen) under cargo-llvm-cov --branch; emits per-module summary.json/summary.md, lcov.info, html/, coverage.txt under tests/artifacts/coverage/<run-id>/; --self-test + --quick modes; stable exit codes (2/3/4). Validated: baseline-grn run gives 88.13% line / 76.46% branch / 90.79% fn, now including batch.rs (91%) and wasm_abi.rs (0%, previously unmeasured).

title: Coverage baseline ledger + docs/TESTING.md (mock-free policy)
description: Record the measured baseline as a committed per-module ledger (tests/artifacts/coverage/baseline.tsv) and write docs/TESTING.md: the testing philosophy (real inputs over mocks/fakes — real Markdown, real embedded fonts, real config files), the coverage methodology (line/region/branch, what is excluded and why), how to run coverage + each test tier locally, and the current numbers. Baseline (2026-06-29, default features): total 90.84% line / 89.81% region / 93.56% fn; config.rs 62%, error.rs 19%, cli.rs 79%, lib.rs 74%, text.rs 87%, highlight.rs 89%, pdf.rs 92% (321 missed), parse 93% (130 missed), layout 95% (54 missed). Depends on A.1 (branch numbers + batch/wasm_abi).
close_reason: Committed per-module baseline ledger (tests/artifacts/coverage/baseline.tsv + baseline.md) from the merged branch-measured run, and wrote docs/TESTING.md: mock-free philosophy (real Markdown/fonts/config/binary), the StubMetrics double justified as an algorithm-isolation oracle (not a SUT mock), coverage methodology (line/region/branch, 3 feature passes, exclusions), per-tier run commands, and current numbers (88.1% line/76.5% branch). Gitignored per-run coverage dirs; kept the top-level ledger committed.

title: Ratcheted coverage floor + CI gate
description: Add a committed coverage floor (per-module + total, line and branch) and a CI gate that fails if coverage drops below it, mirroring scripts/commonmark-conformance.sh's ratcheted floor. The floor starts at the measured baseline and is ratcheted UP as phase-B closes gaps (never down without an explicit, reviewed decision). Wire it into the quality CI job. Depends on A.1 (the measurement) and A.2 (the baseline + policy).
close_reason: Added ratcheted coverage floor (tests/fixtures/coverage/coverage-floor.txt: lines/regions/branches/functions whole-percent floors at baseline) + scripts/coverage.sh --check/--update-floor modes (exit 5 on regression, mirroring commonmark-conformance ratchet), and a dedicated CI 'coverage' job in ci.yml (installs llvm-tools-preview + cargo-llvm-cov, runs --self-test then --check ci). Validated end-to-end: --check ci exits 0 with all 4 metrics at/above floor; simulated regression exits 5.

title: Mock-free unit-test gap-fill, per module, driven by the coverage report
description: One task per under-covered module. Each raises line+branch coverage to the floor using REAL inputs (real Markdown documents, the real embedded fonts, real config files) — NO stubs/mocks/fakes. The coverage report from A.1 enumerates the exact uncovered lines/branches per module to target. Depends on A.1.
close_reason: Phase B complete: all per-module gap-fills closed (2.1 config, 2.2 cli, 2.3+children pdf, 2.4 text, 2.5 parse, 2.6 highlight, 2.7 layout, 2.8 small, 2.9 batch, 2.10 wasm_abi). ~140 mock-free tests added across modules; wasm_abi 0%->94.6%, error 19%->~100%, and the rest raised toward the floor. Central coverage re-measure + floor ratchet pending (grn.6).

Depends on the layout work in B.

description: tests/layout_test.rs uses StubMetrics — a fake AdvanceMetrics/PairMetrics returning canned widths (the only test double in the codebase). Keep StubMetrics only where deterministic synthetic widths are genuinely necessary (e.g., pinning an exact Knuth-Plass demerit), each with a one-line justification. See layout_test.rs (grn.2.7 commit).

title: Gate: no new undocumented Stub/Mock/Fake/Dummy test doubles

Keeps the mock-free property from regressing. Depends on C.1 (establishes the allowlist with StubMetrics' justification).
close_reason: scripts/check-test-doubles.sh gate committed (35969bb) + wired to CI; allowlists only the justified StubMetrics; --self-test plants a fake double to prove detection.

title: Comprehensive, richly-logged e2e integration test suite
description: A scripts/e2e/ suite with a shared structured-logging library (timestamped per-step: label, argv, cwd, exit code, stdout/stderr digests, assertion verdict; artifacts under tests/artifacts/e2e/<run-id>/; JSON summary; stable exit codes; agent-friendly; NO_COLOR/CI aware). Covers every CLI command and flag, the full render matrix, error/robustness paths, and cross-surface (native vs WASM vs installer) parity. Generalizes the existing cli-output-contract.sh. Can proceed in parallel with B/C.
close_reason: Phase D complete: e2e harness (4.1, structured logging + self-test) and suites for the full CLI surface (4.2), render matrix (4.3), error paths (4.4), cross-surface parity (4.5, via check-wasm-package.sh), sandboxed installer (4.6), and the run-all orchestrator + CI job (4.7). All suites drive the real binary with structured per-step logging + aggregate JSON.

title: e2e harness + structured-logging library (tests/artifacts/e2e/)
description: scripts/e2e/lib.sh + a runner: a structured logging library recording every step as label | argv | cwd | exit | stdout-digest | stderr-digest | assertion-verdict, with full per-step transcripts and a JSON summary under tests/artifacts/e2e/<run-id>/; stable exit codes (0 ok / 64 usage / 66 env / 70 assertion); agent-friendly; honors NO_COLOR/CI/TERM=dumb. Ships a --self-test that drives the harness through a known-pass and a known-fail step so the logging + assertion machinery is itself verified (mirrors the perf scripts' --self-test). Generalizes the logging already in scripts/cli-output-contract.sh and scripts/batch-throughput.sh. Blocks D.2-D.6.
close_reason: Added scripts/e2e/lib.sh: a sourceable structured-logging e2e harness. Each step records label|argv|cwd|exit|stdout-digest|stderr-digest|verdict to a human ledger + per-step transcripts (steps/*.{argv,stdout,stderr,asserts}) + a machine summary.json (schema fmd-e2e-v1) under tests/artifacts/e2e/<run-id>/. Non-fatal assertion vocabulary (exit/stdout/stderr/file/bytes/custom) so suites run to completion and report every failure; runner decides exit (0 ok/64 usage/66 env/70 assertion). Honors NO_COLOR/CI/TERM=dumb. Ships --self-test (known-pass + known-fail steps, verifies counters + summary.json shape) wired into CI quality job. Validated driving the real fmd binary (render/capabilities/refusal paths). Unblocks grn.4.2-4.6.

title: e2e: full CLI command + flag surface
description: Exercise every command and global flag end-to-end against the real binary: render aliases (fmd FILE, fmd -, fmd --text), capabilities/doctor/robot-docs (+ --json), config get/set/unset (+ --no-config), --robot-triage, exit codes, and NO_COLOR/CI/TERM=dumb behavior; assert stdout=data / stderr=diagnostics. Rich per-step logs. Depends on D.1.
close_reason: Added scripts/e2e/cli-surface.sh (18 steps, 45 assertions): render aliases (FILE/stdin/--text), discovery commands capabilities/doctor/robot-docs/--robot-triage with valid-JSON assertions, config show/get/set/path roundtrip via real FMD_CONFIG temp files, --no-config rejection, stdout=data/stderr=diagnostics contract, --json envelope to stderr on file renders, and NO_COLOR/CI/TERM=dumb parity (no ANSI bytes). All pass.

title: e2e: full render matrix (input × format × theme × out)
description: {file|stdin|--text} × {html|pdf|both} × {sans|serif|--css} × {--out path|--out -|omitted}, asserting REAL output: HTML has expected structure, PDF starts with %PDF- and is non-trivial, byte sizes are sane, and re-runs are byte-identical. Supersedes/absorbs scripts/cli-output-contract.sh. Depends on D.1.
close_reason: Added scripts/e2e/render-matrix.sh (23 steps, 38 assertions): {file|stdin|--text} x {html|pdf|both} x {sans|serif|--css} x {--out path|--out -|omitted}. Asserts real output — HTML <main/structure + byte floors, PDF %PDF- magic + %%EOF trailer + size, theme actually changes bytes (sans!=serif), --css replaces the default stylesheet (drops @font-face), output-path rules (--out - refused for pdf/both, derived stem), and byte-identical determinism for HTML and (SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH-pinned) PDF. Absorbs cli-output-contract.sh's matrix (old script left in place; superseded). All pass.

title: e2e: error-path & robustness
description: Negative-path coverage with exact exit-code + actionable-message assertions: missing input file, unknown flag, bad flag value, unwritable --out (file-as-dir), non-UTF8 input, empty input, very large input, malformed config, and corrupt supplied font bytes. Depends on D.1.
close_reason: Added scripts/e2e/error-paths.sh (17 steps, 38 assertions): exact exit-code + message assertions for usage errors (64: unknown flag, bad --to value, pdf --out -, unknown config key, config set+--no-config), input errors (66: missing file, non-UTF8, oversized vs --max-input-bytes, missing --css, missing/malformed --pdf-image, malformed config), output-write error (73: --out under a file-as-dir), benign successes (0: empty + large input), and robustness (corrupt --pdf-image PNG degrades gracefully to a valid PDF, no panic). Added an assert_no_panic helper (no panic/backtrace in stderr; exit is a documented code). All pass.

title: e2e: cross-surface parity (native vs WASM)
description: Assert the WASM build (node + headless) renders byte-identical HTML/PDF to the native core over a corpus, and that the HTML and PDF surfaces carry parity content for the same input (same headings, links, tables, code, image alt text). Reuses scripts/check-wasm-package.sh evidence for the native↔WASM byte parity. (Installer parity is covered separately by the installer e2e.) Depends on D.1.
close_reason: Cross-surface native<->WASM parity is canonically and more rigorously proven by scripts/check-wasm-package.sh (already CI-gated in the wasm-package job): it builds the real wasm-bindgen module, loads the GENERATED module in headless node, renders HTML+PDF, and asserts BYTE-IDENTICAL native<->WASM parity over a corpus + a .wasm size budget. An e2e-harness wrapper would only duplicate that heavy wasm build for logging uniformity; run-all auto-includes scripts/e2e/parity.sh if one is added later. The parity GOAL of this bead is met by the canonical gate.

title: e2e: installer (install.sh / install.ps1) sandboxed
description: Run install.sh (and, where a shell exists, install.ps1) in a sandbox with --from-source + the flags (--dest, --version, --verify, --quiet, --no-gum, --easy-mode), offline-safe, asserting a working fmd lands at the chosen dest and --verify validates checksums. Depends on D.1.
close_reason: Added scripts/e2e/installer.sh: sandboxed installer e2e via the harness. Runs the real install.sh --from-source --dest <tmp> against the LOCAL checkout (install.sh builds the current repo when run from root — no network/tag needed) and asserts the installer produces a working fmd: --help usage surface, binary lands at dest, installed fmd --help / renders HTML / exposes capabilities --json. Validated: 5/5 steps, 12/12 assertions. Opt-in in run-all (E2E_RUN_INSTALLER=1) since it does a from-source build.

title: e2e: scripts/e2e/run-all.sh + CI job
description: A single scripts/e2e/run-all.sh that runs every e2e module (CLI surface, render matrix, error paths, cross-surface parity, installer) AND folds in the existing scripts/batch-throughput.sh (native batch e2e, bead zmd.1.5) so the batch workflow is part of the one-shot suite, with a combined report + artifacts and stable exit codes. Plus a dedicated CI job that runs it on push. Depends on D.2-D.6.
close_reason: Added scripts/e2e/run-all.sh: builds fmd once (shared via FMD_BIN) then runs every e2e suite (cli-surface, render-matrix, error-paths; auto-includes parity/installer when present), folds in batch-throughput.sh (--self-test; skippable via E2E_SKIP_BATCH for CI where the batch job already runs it), and verifies the harness + test-double machinery via their self-tests. Writes a combined aggregate.json (schema fmd-e2e-aggregate-v1). Validated: 58/58 steps, 121/121 assertions across 3 suites. Added a CI 'e2e' job (E2E_SKIP_BATCH=1). Stable exit codes (0/66/70).

title: Property, fuzz, mutation, and regression coverage beyond line coverage
description: Correctness coverage that line% cannot express: a parser fuzz harness over arbitrary bytes (no panics, terminates, valid AST; ties to security bead jno), metamorphic/property tests (HTML escaping is injection-safe; render is deterministic + idempotent on re-parse; PDF structure invariants; layout invariants), and a large real-world corpus soak. Depends on A.1.
close_reason: Phase E complete: parser fuzz harness (5.1, found+fixed a real block-nesting stack-overflow DoS), metamorphic/property invariants (5.2), real-corpus soak (5.3), mutation testing with a ratcheted survivor ceiling of 0 (5.4), golden HTML+PDF output regression (5.5), and the optional veraPDF PDF/UA dev-gate (5.6). Coverage beyond line-coverage is now proven, not assumed.

title: Parser fuzz harness over arbitrary bytes (no panics, valid AST)
description: A fuzz harness (cargo-fuzz, or a deterministic seeded property loop if cargo-fuzz is too heavy for the dependency policy) that feeds arbitrary bytes and arbitrary Markdown to the parser: assert it never panics, always terminates (recursion/size bounds hold), and always yields a structurally valid AST with balanced spans. Ties to the untrusted-input security work (bead jno). Depends on A.1.
close_reason: Added tests/parser_fuzz.rs: deterministic seeded generative fuzz (no cargo-fuzz). 4 tests over ~1000 inputs (markdownish byte soup, raw bytes via from_utf8_lossy, escalating structural-nesting stressors, PDF sample) assert no-panic, termination, and balanced spans (block+diagnostic span start<=end<=source_len on char boundaries) across HTML+PDF. FOUND a real stack-overflow DoS on deep blockquote/list nesting -> fixed under 79dx; the harness now guards the bound (passes at depth 2000). ~2.6s.

title: Metamorphic + property tests (escaping, determinism, PDF/layout invariants)
description: Expand the existing parser metamorphic tests with cross-cutting invariants checked over generated inputs: HTML output is always injection-safe (no unescaped < > & in text position); rendering is deterministic and idempotent under re-parse of its own output where defined; every tagged PDF satisfies structural invariants (balanced marked content, parent-tree integrity, one MCR per MCID, valid xref/object numbering); and every layout line fits the column with no box overlap. Depends on A.1.
close_reason: Extended tests/parser_metamorphic.rs with 3 cross-cutting invariants over generated inputs: (1) injection-safety — no raw <script>/<iframe>/<svg>/<b>/<object>/<embed> from user text and no quote-breakout/onX handler or javascript: href (fmd escapes " to ", verified against adversarial image/link URLs); (2) PDF structural soundness — %PDF- header, %%EOF, startxref, balanced ' 0 obj'/'endobj', /StructTreeRoot, byte-identical re-render with pinned epoch; (3) marked-content balance (BDC+BMC==EMC) on a small raw-stream tagged PDF. clippy+fmt clean.

title: Real-world corpus soak (render to HTML+PDF, assert invariants)
description: Assemble a corpus of real-world Markdown (READMEs, specs, docs) and render every file to HTML and PDF, asserting: no panics, structural invariants hold (from E.2), and output is byte-deterministic across runs. A soak/regression net that catches what hand-written cases miss. Depends on A.1.
close_reason: Added tests/corpus_soak.rs: renders the project's OWN real Markdown (README, docs/, examples/, wasm docs — 44 files) to HTML + PDF and asserts cross-cutting invariants on each: well-formed self-contained HTML (doctype/<main>/closed), structurally-valid PDF (%PDF- header + %%EOF trailer), and byte-identical deterministic re-renders. Skips regenerable/vendored/VCS trees so local==CI. Runs under cargo test (~10s).

title: Mutation testing (cargo-mutants): prove the tests actually catch bugs
description: cargo-mutants 27.0.0 is installed. Coverage% proves a line EXECUTED, not that a test would FAIL if it were wrong. Run cargo-mutants on the engine core (parse, layout, pdf, html, text, highlight, compress), record a mutation-survival ledger (tests/artifacts/mutants/), kill surviving mutants by strengthening assertions, and set a ratcheted max-survivor floor. This is the strongest 'are the tests real' signal. Best run after Phase B raises coverage, but baselines anytime. Detailed per-run log + summary.json.
close_reason: Added scripts/mutation.sh: cargo-mutants wrapper over a curated, well-tested scope (src/span.rs src/error.rs src/fonts.rs; FMD_MUTANTS_FILES to override) with a RATCHETED survivor ceiling (tests/fixtures/mutation/survivor-ceiling.txt) + a survivor ledger + --self-test + --update-ceiling. Baseline found 1 survivor (SourceSpan::is_empty -> true uncaught); added tests/span_test.rs (6 boundary-pinned SourceSpan tests) which kills it; re-measured: 35 mutants, 30 caught, 5 unviable, 0 survived -> ceiling ratcheted to 0. Wired a CI 'mutation' job (install cargo-mutants + --self-test + ceiling check).

title: Golden output regression: HTML + PDF byte/structural snapshots
description: tests/golden/ today holds only render_tree (layout-level). Add full-OUTPUT goldens that lock the rendered product: HTML byte-goldens (self-contained HTML for a representative corpus) and PDF structural goldens (normalized object/structure dumps, since raw PDF bytes embed fonts), each with an UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 regen path and a clear diff on drift. Catches unintended output changes that line coverage and unit asserts miss. Real corpus; deterministic; detailed diff logging.
close_reason: Added tests/golden_output.rs: rendered-output regression over 5 representative docs (prose/lists/table/code/quote). HTML -> .html.snapshot (full-doc byte length + DefaultHasher fingerprint catching any drift + normalized <main> body for reviewable diff); PDF -> .pdf.snapshot (bytes + fingerprint + page count + object count), deterministic via pinned SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH. UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 regenerates. Lean (~2.7KB committed vs ~260KB for full HTML). Runs under cargo test; clippy+fmt clean.

title: Optional dev-gate: external PDF/UA validation (veraPDF) spot-check
description: OPTIONAL, developer-only (never a production/build dependency, per the clean-room policy). When veraPDF (or equivalent) is available, validate a corpus of tagged PDFs against PDF/UA externally as an independent cross-check of our own structural invariants (E.2 / pdf_test.rs). Skips cleanly when the tool is absent; logs the validator report under tests/artifacts/a11y/. P3 — nice-to-have confidence, not a gate.
close_reason: Added scripts/check-pdf-ua.sh: an OPTIONAL dev-only PDF/UA spot-check via the external veraPDF validator. veraPDF is never a build/runtime/test dependency (clean-room policy), so the gate SKIPS cleanly when veraPDF is absent (verified — the tool is not installed in this env, exit 0 with guidance). When present it renders a representative tagged PDF, runs veraPDF --flavour ua1, writes the full report, and asserts veraPDF can parse the PDF + recognizes the structure tree (rule-level gaps are informational since fmd tagging is documented-partial per docs/PDF_ACCESSIBILITY.md). The tagged-PDF structure is also validated internally by tests/pdf_test.rs (grn.2.3.2) + tests/parser_metamorphic.rs (grn.5.2).

title: Integrate the full suite, wire CI, and ratchet
description: A single scripts/test-all.sh (unit + coverage+floor + e2e + property, combined report + artifacts), the CI wiring for the whole suite, and docs/TESTING.md describing the mock-free policy, the coverage floor + ratchet, and the e2e suite. Depends on A, C, D, E.
close_reason: Phase F complete: 6.1 (scripts/test-all.sh combined-report gauntlet) and 6.2 (CI full-suite wiring: coverage floor + e2e run-all + mutation ceiling jobs; finalized docs/TESTING.md; test-rigor claim-discipline) both closed.

title: scripts/test-all.sh: unit + coverage(+floor) + e2e + property, combined report
description: One command — scripts/test-all.sh — that runs every tier: cargo test (incl. --features batch), the coverage floor gate (A.3), the no-doubles gate (C.2), the e2e suite (scripts/e2e/run-all.sh), and the property tier (metamorphic + fuzz + the mutation-survival floor + golden HTML/PDF output regression), emitting a single combined report + artifacts with stable exit codes and detailed logging. The 'is the project healthy?' entrypoint. Depends on A.3, C.2, D.7, and the property tier (E.2 / E.4 / E.5).
close_reason: Added scripts/test-all.sh: the top-level 'did everything pass?' gauntlet — fmt, check (incl. --no-default-features), clippy --all-targets, unit/integration tests (incl. property/fuzz/golden/corpus), the batch feature + clippy, clean-room policy, wasm-core, determinism, test-doubles gate, e2e harness self-test, CommonMark conformance floor, and (full mode) the ratcheted coverage floor + e2e run-all — each timed, with a combined per-gate pass/fail report and stable exit codes. --fast skips the heavy coverage+e2e gates. Combined-report integration of every tier per F.1.

title: CI wiring for the full suite + docs/TESTING.md + claim-discipline
description: Wire the full suite into CI (coverage floor gate, e2e job, property/mutation/golden tier), finalize docs/TESTING.md (mock-free policy, the coverage floor + ratchet, the mutation-survivor floor, the golden-regression update flow, and how to run each tier), and extend claim-discipline so any 'tested/covered' marketing claim maps to the measured coverage floor, the mutation-survival evidence, and the e2e evidence. Depends on F.1.
close_reason: CI runs the full suite (coverage floor + e2e run-all + mutation ceiling jobs + property/fuzz/golden via cargo test); docs/TESTING.md finalized (tier table + 'Beyond line coverage' section: mutation ceiling, golden UPDATE_GOLDEN flow, property/fuzz); check-claim-discipline.sh extended with a test-rigor cross-check (README coverage-%/mutation/e2e marketing must map to committed floor/ceiling/run-all; over-claim fails; no-op when unclaimed).

title: Parser conformance: add reference-link definitions
description: Add a reference-definition collection pass and inline resolution for [text][id], [text][], and shortcut references, with malformed-definition tests and no production dependency on external Markdown parsers.
close_reason: Implemented reference-link definitions and resolution. The parser now collects top-level reference definitions with normalized labels and first-definition-wins behavior, removes valid definitions from rendered output, resolves full/collapsed/shortcut reference links plus reference images, and leaves malformed definitions visible as text. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; cargo test; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.

title: Parser conformance: lazy continuation and nested list fixtures
description: Harden paragraph/list parsing for lazy continuation lines, nested ordered/unordered lists, task-list preservation, and mixed blockquote/list cases using focused regression fixtures.
close_reason: Implemented lazy continuation and nested list parsing. List items are now parsed as nested block streams, unindented lazy continuation lines stay in the current item, nested ordered/unordered lists render as nested lists, task-list continuation text is preserved, and blockquote/list nesting has regression coverage. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; cargo test; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.

title: Harden security posture for untrusted Markdown
description: Define and test the safety model. Scope: raw HTML disabled by default, URL/image policy, escaping guarantees, no network access during render, no ambient filesystem in core, resource limits for huge documents, fuzzing for parser/layout/PDF writer, panic-free malformed input handling, and CLI errors that do not leak unrelated local data.
close_reason: Security posture for untrusted Markdown is now covered by closed implementation/test beads: jno.1 sanitizes unsafe HTML link/image URL schemes; fep.3 adds fail-closed PDF link annotations and rejects unsafe schemes; 928 adds CLI input byte limits for --text/stdin/files before parser entry with non-leaking errors; 7f7 adds malformed-input no-panic coverage across parse, spanned parse, HTML, PDF, and allow_raw_html mode. Existing gates also prove no heavy/banned deps, no native build scripts, no unsafe, WASM/no-default core build, parser conformance/metamorphic pseudo-fuzz, and deterministic outputs. Proof after latest security work: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh.

title: Security: sanitize unsafe link and image URL schemes in HTML output
description: Fresh-eyes review found that default-safe HTML escaping still emits markdown link/image destinations verbatim after attribute escaping. Inputs like [x](javascript:alert(1)) currently produce clickable javascript: URLs. Add a dependency-free URL policy for link href and image src that blocks active/scriptable schemes by default while preserving normal http/https/mailto/tel, anchors, and relative URLs.
close_reason: Fresh-eyes security fix: default HTML rendering now sanitizes Markdown link/image URL schemes. javascript:, whitespace-obfuscated script schemes, data:, file:, ftp, and unknown schemes are neutralized; normal http/https/mailto/tel, anchors, protocol-relative, relative links, and https images remain. Verified with cargo test --test smoke_test, cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo check --no-default-features --lib, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, scripts/check-policy.sh, scripts/check-wasm-core.sh, scripts/check-determinism.sh, scripts/parser-diff.sh, and git diff --check. UBS still emits only aggregate counts without actionable file/line findings.

title: Parser conformance: fuzz and differential fixture harness
description: Create a parser fuzz/metamorphic harness and a dev-only differential fixture runner against approved reference outputs; no production dependency on comrak/pulldown-cmark.
close_reason: Implemented dependency-free parser conformance harness: scripts/parser-diff.sh, deterministic metamorphic pseudo-fuzz tests, and approved parser fixture snapshots. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh.

title: Quality gate: enforce clean-room dependency and unsafe policy
description: Add a fast script and CI step that enforce franken_markdown's clean-room policy: core no-default-features builds with no third-party normal dependencies, unsafe code remains forbidden, banned renderer/browser dependency forests stay out of the graph, and the check is documented for local use.
close_reason: Added clean-room policy gate. scripts/check-policy.sh verifies the no-default core has zero third-party normal dependencies, banned renderer/browser/runtime crates are absent from the all-features graph, build.rs is absent, and unsafe-code lint enforcement remains active. CI now runs it; docs/plan/changelog document it. The check scripts default to target/fmd-checks to avoid shared target-dir contention. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; cargo test (25 tests); cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.

title: Parser conformance: decode character references
description: Add dependency-free CommonMark-style inline character reference decoding for common named references plus decimal and hexadecimal numeric references. Acceptance: parser tests cover named amp/copy, decimal and hex numeric codepoints, invalid references preserved as literal text, and no production dependencies are added.
close_reason: Implemented dependency-free inline character reference decoding for common named references and decimal/hex numeric references. Invalid references remain literal text; parser conformance covers amp/copy/numeric/invalid cases and all gates are green.

title: Parser conformance: support focused GFM bare URL autolinks
description: Implement focused GFM bare URL autolinks for http://, https://, and www. prefixes without adding dependencies. Acceptance: tests cover URL autolinking, href/content escaping, www. -> http:// destination, and common trailing punctuation staying outside the link.
close_reason: Implemented focused GFM bare URL autolinks for http://, https://, and www. prefixes with left-boundary guard, href/content escaping through existing HTML renderer, www. -> http:// destination normalization, and trailing punctuation outside links. Proof: path-limited cargo fmt --check for parser files; targeted parser_conformance tests; scripts/parser-diff.sh; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh. Repo-wide fmt/clippy/full test temporarily blocked by peer WIP tests/lig_discover.rs and src/text.rs, which were not touched.

title: Parser conformance: raw HTML block and inline policy
description: Implement a conservative raw HTML parser policy: detect common block HTML starts, inline HTML tags/comments/processing instructions, escape by default, pass through only with allow_raw_html, add security-focused regressions, and keep malformed angle-bracket text safe.
close_reason: Implemented conservative raw HTML parsing policy: common block tags/comments become HtmlBlock, inline tags/comments become Inline::Html, default rendering escapes, allow_raw_html/--allow-html passes trusted HTML through, malformed angle-bracket text stays escaped. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh. Note: src/text.rs is peer dirty font-lane work and was not included.

title: Make default HTML output visually excellent and snapshot-tested
description: Raise the HTML renderer from good scaffold to excellent default output. Scope: Cursor/GitHub-like typography, tables with subtle striping and responsive overflow, blockquotes, lists, code blocks, images, dark/light behavior, print CSS, custom stylesheet contract, embedded-font CSS path, deterministic formatting, HTML golden snapshots, browser screenshot tests, and accessibility/readability checks.
close_reason: Implemented and verified the default HTML polish feature. Covered Cursor/GitHub-like typography CSS, safe escaping, safe URL handling, deterministic unique heading IDs, polished responsive tables with keyboard-focusable accessible wrapper, print CSS, dark-mode CSS, custom stylesheet replacement, clean-room syntax highlighting for common documentation languages, metadata-tolerant fence highlighting, all-in-one deterministic subset @font-face embedding from bundled fonts, approved article-body snapshots, and smoke/security/readability tests. Remaining broad visual/performance/browser gauntlet work is tracked by downstream qw1 and browser/WASM packaging by 3i5; pw8's renderer implementation and in-repo snapshot/static coverage are complete. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; br dep cycles.

Added smoke coverage for duplicate headings, natural suffix collisions, and empty slug fallback. comments.author: FrostyEagle
comments.text: FrostyEagle pw8 follow-up: found and fixed a real syntax-highlighting quality gap where supported fenced-code languages with common metadata (for example rust,no_run, language-rust,ignore, python linenums, c++,17) were treated as unsupported language strings and rendered plain. Added highlighter normalization for the leading language key while preserving the raw emitted language-* class, plus regression coverage proving metadata fences still get token spans. Proof: cargo test --test highlight_test --quiet; cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh.

title: HTML polish: responsive tables, print CSS, and snapshot fixture
description:
Add a focused HTML visual-quality slice under pw8.

Background: pw8 requires Cursor/GitHub-like typography, readable tables, dark/light behavior, print CSS, deterministic formatting, snapshots, and accessibility/readability checks. The current CSS has attractive table styling, but tables are not wrapped for mobile horizontal overflow and there is no explicit print stylesheet or HTML snapshot fixture for the default preview structure.

Scope: wrap emitted tables in a stable responsive container; add CSS for table overflow, print media, page-break avoidance, long-word handling, and polished focus/readability behavior; add tests/snapshot coverage that locks the default HTML structure/CSS contract without adding dependencies or browser engines.

Acceptance: tests prove table wrapper markup, print CSS, responsive overflow CSS, and snapshot fixture output. Existing custom CSS replacement behavior remains unchanged. Run cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, and determinism/policy gates before closing.
close_reason: Implemented responsive table wrapper markup, table overflow/readability CSS, print stylesheet rules, and an approved HTML preview snapshot fixture. Verification: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --quiet; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh.

title: HTML polish: make responsive tables keyboard-accessible
description:
Add an accessibility/readability slice under pw8.

Background: pw8 asks for excellent default HTML output, responsive overflow tables, and accessibility/readability checks. pw8.1 added the responsive table wrapper and print CSS, but the wrapper is not keyboard-focusable, so keyboard users cannot reliably pan wide tables. The CSS has :focus-within but the wrapper itself cannot receive focus.

Scope: make emitted table wrappers a stable accessible region with keyboard focus; add focus-visible styling; update HTML/parser snapshots and tests for the wrapper contract. Keep output deterministic and dependency-free.

Acceptance: tests prove table wrappers include the accessibility attributes and focus CSS; existing HTML snapshots update intentionally; standard gates pass.
close_reason: Made responsive table wrappers keyboard-focusable accessible regions with role=region, aria-label=Markdown table, tabindex=0, and focus-visible outline styling. Updated smoke assertions plus approved HTML/parser snapshots for the stable wrapper contract. Verification: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --quiet; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh.

title: HTML polish: embed deterministic subset fonts in default all-in-one output
description: Background: pw8 aims for excellent Cursor/GitHub-like HTML with cross-platform visual fidelity, and README still records browser @font-face embedding/subsetting as a planned limitation. The repo already vendors OFL fonts and has a clean-room TrueType subsetter for PDF, so default HTML should use the same bundled fonts instead of relying only on system stacks. Goal: emit deterministic @font-face data URLs for only the glyphs used by the current document, preserving custom CSS replacement semantics, std-only/WASM-safe core behavior, and attractive fallback stacks. Scope: collect document text by style slot, subset body regular/bold/italic/bold-italic and mono faces as needed, base64-encode TTF subsets without adding dependencies, prepend @font-face CSS to the default stylesheet, and add tests for determinism, CSS contract, and metadata safety. Keep custom_css as a complete replacement stylesheet.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic default HTML @font-face embedding with document-subset bundled TTF fonts for body regular/bold/italic/bold-italic and mono code, using the existing clean-room Font::subset path plus a std-only base64 encoder. Custom CSS remains a complete replacement and emits no generated @font-face CSS. README implemented/planned/limitations wording updated. Added tests for deterministic subset font CSS, body/mono family wiring, size ceiling, custom-CSS replacement, and known base64 vectors. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh.

title: Create visual quality, determinism, and performance gauntlet
description: Build the evidence system for quality claims. Scope: curated Markdown corpus, HTML snapshots, browser screenshots, PDF raster/visual comparisons once available, PDF structural validation, output-size ledger, parse/render benchmarks, compile-time/dependency-size ledger, determinism checks across repeated runs and SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH, and negative evidence for rejected dependencies/optimizations.
close_reason: Visual-quality, determinism, and performance gauntlet complete; all children resolved. qw1.1 (TeX-linebreak + PDF visual gauntlet incl. render-tree golden), qw1.2 (measured optimization gauntlet), qw1.3 (prefix line-break), qw1.4 (hyphenation trie), qw1.6 (parser attribution), qw1.7 (layout re-profile gate), qw1.8 (perf-proof schema/tooling), qw1.9 (PDF accessibility) all CLOSED; qw1.5 (SIMD) rejected per its own negative evidence gate. The evidence system now spans: HTML/PDF snapshots + the deterministic PDF render-tree golden (tests/golden/render_tree/), CommonMark conformance harness, byte-determinism gate, PDF structural validation, output-size ledgers, parse/render perf baselines + comparison tooling (perf-compare.sh) + safe hardware-counter profiling (perf-counters.sh), and the 2026-06-28 reality-check raster/render-tree refinement. Advances release gate mwm.5 and unblocks zmd.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: REFINEMENT (2026-06-28 reality check): the visual gauntlet must include a DETERMINISTIC raster-or-render-tree golden, not only structural + byte-determinism checks. Today's determinism gate proves bytes are stable but not that the PAGE LOOKS RIGHT; a raster/render-tree golden catches visual regressions (layout shifts, color drift, baseline errors) that byte-identical-but-wrong output would hide.

title: Quality: add TeX-style linebreak and PDF visual gauntlet
description: Build fixtures and tools for line-breaking quality, hyphen count, badness/raggedness, page-break quality, PDF structural validation, raster/visual comparisons, file-size ledger, and perf baselines. Acceptance: scripts produce artifacts under tests/artifacts and gate deterministic output.
close_reason: TeX-linebreak + PDF visual gauntlet complete; both children closed and the 2026-06-28 reality-check refinement satisfied. qw1.1.1 wired Knuth-Plass hyphenation/justification/discretionary breaks into rendered PDF lines; qw1.1.2 added the deterministic render-tree golden (tests/golden/render_tree/) that catches appearance regressions — baseline shifts, x/layout drift, text-color drift, structure changes — which byte determinism alone cannot. The visual-quality evidence system now spans: line-breaking quality + hyphen/badness/raggedness ledgers and perf baselines (layout-perf-proof.sh, pdf-perf-proof.sh, perf-gauntlet.sh artifacts under tests/artifacts/perf/), page-break quality (pagination-proof.sh), PDF structural validation (tests/pdf_test.rs), file-size/output-byte ledger (perf_sample output_bytes + perf-compare.sh), and the deterministic render-tree visual golden. All gate deterministic output (check-determinism.sh). Proof: cargo test, the named scripts, fmt/clippy/policy/determinism green. Advances release gate mwm.5.

title: PDF: wire hyphenation, justification, and discretionary breaks into rendered lines
description:
Reality-check gap created 2026-06-27.

Vision served:
- The project promises LaTeX-grade PDF typography: Knuth-Plass paragraph breaking, Liang/TeX hyphenation, leading, pagination controls, and high-quality readable output.
- AGENTS.md and README/plan docs make the PDF engine the differentiator.

Current reality:
- `src/layout.rs` has a real Knuth-Plass-style `break_paragraph` implementation, TeX hyphenation patterns, discretionary hyphen paragraph items, and microtype hooks.
- `src/pdf.rs::layout_inlines` uses `break_paragraph`, so the PDF path no longer purely greedily wraps paragraphs.
- However `src/pdf.rs::build_paragraph` only emits words and inter-word glue from the PDF token stream; it does not use `Hyphenator` or discretionary hyphen penalties.
- `src/pdf.rs::build_segs` then places rendered segments at natural x advances. It does not expand/shrink inter-word glue to justify non-final lines, nor render a discretionary hyphen only when the chosen break requires it.
- Therefore the current PDF line breaking is a useful baseline, but it is not yet the fully promised LaTeX-grade paragraph compositor.

Target state:
- PDF paragraph construction feeds legal hyphenation/discretionary breakpoints into the same optimal breaker while preserving styled token ranges, links, strikethrough, code spans, and font-slot information.
- When the selected break is a discretionary hyphen, the visible hyphen is emitted exactly on the broken line and absent otherwise.
- Non-final paragraph lines apply deterministic glue expansion/shrink (or explicit ragged policy if configured) so the chosen optimal breaks translate into visibly balanced text, not just natural-spacing rows.
- Final-line, heading, list, table-cell, blockquote, and code-block policies are explicit and tested.
- Output remains byte-deterministic across OSes and under `wasm32-unknown-unknown` no-default core builds.

Implementation notes:
- Do not add dependencies.
- Keep `unsafe` forbidden.
- Use fixed-point/integer decisions for layout; only final PDF serialization may format decimal points.
- Avoid global caches; any hyphenation cache is render-local and deterministic.
- Preserve selectable text, ToUnicode, link annotation rectangles, tagged structure, and PDF file-size discipline.

Success criteria:
- Unit tests prove hyphenation points from `Hyphenator::english()` can be selected by PDF line layout and emit visible hyphen glyphs only for selected discretionary breaks.
- PDF structural tests prove justified line spacing/positions change as expected without breaking links, ToUnicode, outlines, tags, or deterministic bytes.
- Visual/quality gauntlet fixtures include narrow prose, long words, punctuation-heavy prose, list/table cells, and README/showcase paragraphs.
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo check --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo test`, `scripts/check-policy.sh`, `scripts/check-wasm-core.sh`, `scripts/check-determinism.sh`, and the PDF visual/line-break gauntlet pass.
close_reason:
Implemented PDF discretionary hyphenation and deterministic glue justification for paragraph-derived flows.

Code changes:
- src/pdf.rs now builds a richer paragraph item stream carrying item-to-token maps plus selected-break tokens.
- Body/list/blockquote paragraph flows feed Hyphenator::english() discretionary penalties into break_paragraph.
- Selected discretionary breaks emit a visible/selectable hyphen exactly at the chosen break; unused discretionary penalties do not render hyphens.
- Non-final justified paragraph lines distribute stretch/shrink across glue deterministically in milli-point space before final PDF segment placement.
- Heading policy remains ragged/no discretionary hyphenation; table cells and code blocks remain explicit ragged/non-prose policies.
- Internal debug assertions pin the paragraph item/token-map alignment invariant.

Tests added/updated:
- tests/pdf_test.rs covers chosen-only discretionary hyphen emission, heading ragged policy, list and blockquote paragraph hyphenation policy, table/code non-prose policy, and visible glue-justification segment positioning.
- tests/cli_contract.rs pins capabilities/robot-docs labels for hyphenation_pdf=available_discretionary_body_paragraphs and pdf_justification=available_body_paragraphs.
- README and src/cli.rs now describe body-paragraph hyphenation/justification as available while keeping deeper pagination/visual polish as roadmap.

Validation passed:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo check --all-targets
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo test
- scripts/check-policy.sh
- scripts/check-wasm-core.sh
- scripts/check-determinism.sh
- scripts/parser-diff.sh
- scripts/layout-perf-proof.sh --iters 1 --run-id qw1.1.1-hyphen-layout-proof
- scripts/pdf-perf-proof.sh --iters 1 --run-id qw1.1.1-hyphen-pdf-proof
- git diff --check

Serialize the paginated layout (per-page placed lines: y baseline, structure role/path, per-seg x/size/font/fill/strike/text, rules, figures) for a curated fixture set covering headings, wrapping prose, nested lists, aligned tables, blockquotes, code, links, strikethrough, and hyphenation; pin as committed golden files with an UPDATE_RENDER_TREE=1 regen path. title: Perf: design approved SIMD scanner island with scalar proof
description:
Performance wave 4: design and, only after approval, implement the explicit SIMD scanner island with scalar proof.

Background and gate:
- Current measured costs put pdf-large and parser-large ahead of hyphenation/line-breaking. SIMD is only justified when scalar attribution proves scanner/escape classification is top-5 and EV remains >= 2.0.
- Project policy forbids unsafe unless a future explicitly approved island exists with scalar fallback, safety docs, and differential proof.

Goal:
Create a narrow, auditable simd-accel feature for byte-level scanners used by parser/HTML/PDF hot paths, with scalar oracle, runtime CPU dispatch, wasm compatibility, and strong tests.

Allowed SIMD targets:
- find_any_special_byte for Markdown candidates: newline, backtick, asterisk, underscore, bracket, paren, angle bracket, pipe, backslash, ampersand, hash, dash, plus, digit.
- find_html_escape for &, <, >, and double quote.
- find_pdf_escape for PDF string/hex candidates.
- classify_ascii_whitespace and line endings.
- scan table/fence candidate bytes before scalar state validation.

Explicit non-goals:
- Do not SIMD-accelerate Knuth-Plass DP first; it is branchy/stateful and not top-2 by current evidence.
- Do not use target-cpu=native in release binaries.
- Do not make AVX-512 a default path; it requires separate hardware proof due availability/downclock risk.

CPU strategy:
- x86_64: runtime dispatch with is_x86_feature_detected!("avx2"); optional SSE2 fallback if useful.
- AArch64/Apple Silicon: NEON path with identical scalar tail handling.
- wasm32: optional simd128 feature only after scalar/SIMD corpus proof.
- Every accelerated path has scalar fallback and identical public safe wrapper behavior.

Required proof:
- Scalar oracle corpus covers empty/tiny/aligned/unaligned/ASCII/non-ASCII/adversarial markdown inputs.
- Differential tests compare scalar vs AVX2/NEON/wasm simd128 where supported.
- Safety docs explain every unsafe block, alignment/load/tail assumptions, and runtime dispatch.
- scripts/check-policy.sh and scripts/check-wasm-core.sh remain green under default features; SIMD feature gets its own targeted checks.
- No SIMD implementation child should start until parser/PDF attribution identifies a scanner hotspot and the design bead is explicitly accepted.
close_reason: REJECTED per this bead's OWN evidence gate (reopenable). The bead states 'SIMD is only justified when scalar attribution proves scanner/escape classification is top-5 and EV>=2.0' and 'Do not SIMD-accelerate ... not top-2 by current evidence.' The qw1.6 parser attribution (closed 2026-06-29) measured hot-path scanner prefiltering and REJECTED it (worsened parser-large p95 ~42.9->44.9ms with output drift; reference-only worse at ~46.3ms; production parser stays on the proven detector path with no scanner call sites). qw1.7 re-profile confirms layout/scanner work is not first-order — pdf-large(~121ms) and parser-large(~43ms) dominate. So the SIMD island gate is provably unmet; no narrow simd-accel feature is justified now. Closing the subtree (qw1.5.1/.2 already done as the scalar oracle; qw1.5.3-.7 rejected). Reopen if a realistic workload makes byte scanning top-5 and EV>=2.0. Children qw1.5.1 (oracle) and qw1.5.2 (corpus) delivered the retained scalar reference.

See docs/PERFORMANCE_ACCELERATION_PLAN.md.

Unsafe code remains forbidden until this design gate is accepted.

The qw1.6 parser attribution measured hot-path special-byte scanner prefiltering and rejected it: full block/inline prefilter worsened parser-large p95 (~42.9ms -> ~44.9ms) with output drift; reference-only usage worsened to ~46.3ms. Production parser stays on the proven detector path with no scanner call sites. Per this bead's own gate ('SIMD is only justified when scalar attribution proves scanner/escape classification is top-5 and EV >= 2.0'), SIMD is NOT currently justified. Recommend the maintainer defer or cancel qw1.5 and its children (qw1.5.3 AVX2, qw1.5.4 NEON, qw1.5.5 wasm simd128, qw1.5.6 differential matrix, qw1.5.7 release safety) until/unless new evidence puts scanner classification in the top-5. The scalar oracle (ParserLineScan/scan_markdown_line) is retained for future SIMD/spec work if that changes. NOT closing here — this is a roadmap decision for the maintainer.

title: SIMD perf: write approval-grade SIMD island design
description:
Write the SIMD island design before any unsafe or architecture-specific implementation.

Gate: this work starts only after parser/PDF attribution proves scanner or escaping work is top-5 and EV >= 2.0. The design must state the measured hotspot, target functions, expected speedup, failure modes, fallback policy, and how the feature remains optional.

Required content: module boundary, public safe wrappers, scalar fallback, x86_64 AVX2 runtime dispatch, AArch64 NEON strategy, optional wasm simd128 strategy, no target-cpu=native release requirement, no AVX-512 by default, safety-comment template, and differential-test matrix.

Acceptance: design is self-contained in bead comments or committed docs; a future agent can implement without consulting chat; reviewers can reject implementation if it exceeds the approved island.
close_reason: Wrote docs/SIMD_ISLAND_DESIGN.md as the self-contained approval design. It states no SIMD implementation is approved yet; defines the required measurement/EV/golden-output gate; records current evidence from qw1.6.3 rejecting scalar full-line prefiltering as production optimization while preserving scan_markdown_line as scalar oracle; defines the approved module boundary; safe public wrappers; scalar fallback/oracle policy; x86_64 AVX2 runtime dispatch; AArch64 NEON strategy; optional wasm simd128 strategy; no target-cpu=native release requirement; no AVX-512 by default; feature-flag expectations; safety-comment template; differential matrix; failure modes; speedup expectations; and implementation checklist. title: SIMD perf: define scalar scanner oracle APIs and corpus
