Define scalar scanner APIs and corpus that every accelerated implementation must match byte-for-byte.

Candidate APIs: find_any_special_byte, find_html_escape, find_pdf_escape, classify_ascii_whitespace, scan_table_or_fence_candidate, and a small result type for candidate byte offsets/flags.

Corpus: empty inputs, one-byte inputs, all alignment offsets, long ASCII text, non-ASCII UTF-8, markdown punctuation storms, table/fence/link edge cases, HTML escaping cases, PDF escaping cases, CRLF/BOM/tabs, and generated large documents.

Acceptance: scalar oracle tests pass without SIMD feature; corpus is shared by parser/PDF/HTML scanner tests; output is stable on native and wasm core; no unsafe is introduced here.
close_reason: Implemented scalar scanner oracle APIs and corpus for future SIMD acceleration. Added the shared scanner module with byte-offset oracles for Markdown-special bytes, HTML escaping, PDF escaping, ASCII whitespace classification, Markdown line classification, and table/fence candidate classification; exported the APIs through the library surface; added scanner corpus tests covering empty and one-byte inputs, alignment offsets, long generated documents, UTF-8/non-ASCII, punctuation/table/fence/link cases, HTML escaping, PDF literal escaping, CRLF/BOM/tabs, and conservative parser-edge flags. No unsafe was introduced. Validation passed: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; git diff --check; br dep cycles.

title: SIMD perf: implement x86_64 AVX2 scanner path
description:
Implement the x86_64 AVX2 scanner path only after design and scalar oracle are accepted.

Requirements: runtime dispatch with is_x86_feature_detected!("avx2"), scalar fallback for unsupported CPUs, no target-cpu=native release assumption, safe public wrapper, isolated unsafe blocks, unaligned input handling, exact scalar tail behavior, and no semantic Markdown decisions inside vector code.

Acceptance: AVX2 differential tests pass on supported hardware; scalar fallback tests pass when AVX2 is disabled/unavailable; perf evidence shows scanner scenario improvement beyond same-host noise; scripts/check-policy.sh remains acceptable with the explicitly approved unsafe island.
close_reason: REJECTED (not won't-fix-forever; reopenable). Downstream of qw1.5, whose SIMD scanner island was never approved because the qw1.6 parser attribution evidence gate came back NEGATIVE: hot-path special-byte scanner prefiltering MEASURABLY WORSENED parser-large p95 (~42.9ms->44.9ms) with output drift, and special-byte scanning is not a top-5 cost. qw1.5's own acceptance requires 'scalar attribution proves scanner/escape classification is top-5 and EV>=2.0', which is provably false. No SIMD code is therefore justified. The scalar oracle (ParserLineScan/scan_markdown_line) is retained. Reopen this if a future workload makes scanner classification top-5. See qw1.6 closeout + qw1.5 comment (2026-06-29).

Acceptance: NEON differential tests pass on Apple Silicon/AArch64 hardware before claiming Apple gains; scalar fallback remains available for non-NEON targets; perf artifact records before/after scanner latency and CPU model.

No SIMD code is therefore justified. The scalar oracle (ParserLineScan/scan_markdown_line) is retained. Reopen this if a future workload makes scanner classification top-5. See qw1.6 closeout + qw1.5 comment (2026-06-29).

title: Perf: attribute parser scanner and allocation hot paths
description:
Performance wave 2: attribute and optimize parser scanner/allocation hot paths.

Background and evidence:
- Current-head release-perf harness ranks parser-large second after pdf-large: p95 about 51.460 ms on a generated 1 MiB CommonMark/GFM-like document.
- This bead is the evidence gate for SIMD scanner work. SIMD should not proceed merely because byte scanning sounds plausible; it only proceeds if parser/HTML/PDF scanner attribution proves special-byte scanning is top-5.

Goal:
Find and reduce parser cost without weakening CommonMark/GFM behavior, parser conformance, diagnostics, source spans, raw HTML safety, table behavior, link references, or WASM portability.

Attribution scope:
- Block scanning and line classification.
- Inline parsing and delimiter handling.
- Table delimiter/cell parsing and escaped pipe/backtick handling.
- Link reference collection and lookup normalization.
- Source-span and diagnostic paths.
- Allocation counts and String/Vec churn.

Optimization principles:
- Add stage timing and allocation evidence first.
- Preserve existing parser fixtures, parser-diff outputs, metamorphic tests, and approved article snapshots.
- Prefer borrowed ranges/spans, compact token structures, and a single classified scan over repeated full-line checks when behavior is provably unchanged.
- Keep scalar behavior as the canonical oracle for any future SIMD scanner.

Required proof:
- scripts/parser-diff.sh remains green.
- cargo test parser-focused tests remain green.
- Differential/golden fixtures are added before behavior-sensitive rewrites.
- Any speedup claim includes before/after p50/p95/p99 and output equivalence.
- No SIMD child may start until this bead (or its attribution child) records that special-byte scanning is top-5.
close_reason: Parser perf attribution wave complete: all children (qw1.6.1 stage/allocation attribution, qw1.6.2 differential corpus, qw1.6.3 scalar special-byte oracle, qw1.6.4 borrowed-span allocation reduction, qw1.6.5 regression script) closed with artifacts under tests/artifacts/perf/. SIMD EVIDENCE GATE RESULT: NEGATIVE — qw1.6.3 implemented the scalar scanner oracle (ParserLineScan/scan_markdown_line) but measured hot-path scanner prefiltering and REJECTED it: full block/inline prefilter worsened parser-large p95 (~42.9ms -> ~44.9ms) with output drift, reference-only usage worsened to ~46.3ms; production parser stays on the proven detector path with no scanner call sites. Conclusion: special-byte scanning is NOT a top-5 profitable target, so the qw1.5 SIMD subtree is not currently justified by evidence (see qw1.5 comment). Proof: parser-diff.sh, cargo test, check-policy.sh, check-wasm-core.sh, check-determinism.sh all green at child closeouts.

title: Parser perf: add parser stage timing and allocation attribution
description:
Background: parser-large is current-head rank 2 (p95 about 51.460 ms for a 1 MiB CommonMark/GFM-like document). This bead finds the true parser bottleneck before any scanner or allocation rewrite.

Scope: instrument block scanning, line classification, collect_link_references, parse_blocks, parse_table, parse_list, parse_inlines, reference lookup, span collection, diagnostics, and allocation-heavy String/Vec construction sites.

Output contract: emit structured JSONL or perf artifacts with stage name, count, cumulative time, p50/p95 if repeated, bytes processed, allocated object counts where feasible, and a hypothesis ledger.

Acceptance: artifact ranks parser substages; scripts/parser-diff.sh remains green; normal parser API/output is unchanged; bead comment states whether byte scanning, allocation, or semantic parsing is the next target.
close_reason: Implemented profiling-only parser stage/allocation attribution, emitted parser-large normal and spanned JSONL artifacts, proved profiled AST/HTML equivalence, and passed full Rust/policy/WASM/parser/determinism validation. Smoke artifact recommends qw1.6.3 next from block_parse_total dominance, after fixture-gate care.

comments.author: FrostyEagle
comments.text:
Implemented parser stage/allocation attribution. Added additive APIs parse_markdown_profiled and parse_markdown_spanned_profiled, backed by ParseProfile, SpannedParseProfile, and ParseStageSummary. Normal parse_markdown / parse_markdown_spanned continue through the same code path with a disabled profiler. The profiler is wasm-safe: wasm32 builds emit zero-duration timing if called rather than depending on native clocks.

Instrumented parser stages include line_split, reference_collection, block_parse_total, heading_block, setext_heading_block, paragraph_block, fenced_code_block, indented_code_block, blockquote_block, html_block, table_block, list_block, inline_parse, span_collection, and diagnostics_collection. Stage summaries include p50/p95/p99/max, work_count, input bytes, total input bytes, and approximate allocation/object counts.

Perf harness now emits schema-compatible artifacts when parser-large runs with --out-dir: tests/artifacts/perf/qw1.6.1-parser-stage-smoke/golden/parser-large-stages.jsonl, parser-large-spanned-stages.jsonl, and parser-large-recommendation.jsonl. Smoke run: cargo run --example fmd_perf_harness -- --scenario parser-large --iters 2 --out-dir tests/artifacts/perf/qw1.6.1-parser-stage-smoke/golden. Profiled parse matched the normal AST and rendered HTML exactly; profiled spanned parse converted back to the normal AST exactly. Normal parser-large top stage was block_parse_total, p95_ns=217922706, total_ns=419911913, allocation_count=25066. The generated next_target_recommendation points to qw1.6.3 with medium confidence: line/reference/block classification dominates; implement scalar special-byte classification scan after the fixture gate. Separate spanned-path evidence shows span_collection is expensive for editor/diagnostic flows (p95_ns=913367086), but it is not part of normal parse_markdown.

Validation after final edits: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; bash -n scripts/perf-gauntlet.sh; scripts/perf-gauntlet.sh --help; JSON parse checks for parser-large-stages.jsonl, parser-large-spanned-stages.jsonl, and parser-large-recommendation.jsonl; git diff --check; br dep cycles. All passed.

title: Parser perf: build differential fixture corpus for scanner rewrites
description:
Create parser fixtures that specifically protect scanner-sensitive behavior before any fast scanner rewrite.

Required cases: escaped table pipes and backticks, fence open/close edge cases, nested/indented lists, reference-style links/images, malformed references, raw HTML allow/escape behavior, inline emphasis delimiters, autolinks with punctuation, CRLF/BOM handling, tabs, thematic breaks, setext/ATX heading interactions, and large generated documents.

Required proof: fixtures must exercise both rendered output and parser diagnostic/span behavior where relevant. Include metamorphic checks for newline normalization and equivalent whitespace where the parser promises equivalence.

Acceptance: parser-diff includes the new corpus or a dedicated script with detailed logging; intentionally breaking one protected case locally produces a clear failure; future parser perf beads depend on this before changing scanner behavior.
close_reason: Implemented scanner-sensitive parser fixture corpus for future scanner rewrites. Added tests/fixtures/parser/scanner_edges.md plus approved article snapshot covering escaped table pipes/backticks, fenced close/indented-fence edge cases, nested and tab-indented lists, reference links/images, malformed references, raw HTML escaping, emphasis delimiters, autolinks with punctuation and www/email forms, tabs, thematic breaks, setext and ATX headings. Extended parser_differential logging and fixture-count assertion so parser-diff reports each approved fixture under --nocapture. Added UTF-8 BOM normal-render metamorphic coverage; existing newline-normalization and generated-large-document metamorphic tests cover CRLF/final-newline and large corpus stability. Added fixture-backed spanned-parser test asserting scanner_edges preserves normal AST parity, top-level spans for scanner-sensitive blocks, and malformed-reference diagnostic span/message. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; git diff --check; br dep cycles.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Fresh-eyes parser/span bug fixed by SilverLeopard: parse_document strips a UTF-8 BOM before parsing, but the spanned parser path collected source lines from the unstripped source. Root cause: AST construction and span/diagnostic collection normalized different inputs, so a BOM-prefixed file beginning with a reference definition could consume the reference in the AST while assigning that reference line as the span for the first visible block. Fix: source_lines now strips the BOM consistently while preserving original byte offsets by starting spans after the BOM. Added tests/parser_spans.rs::utf8_bom_is_ignored_without_skewing_spanned_blocks covering BOM-prefixed headings and BOM-prefixed reference definitions. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; focused UBS over edited files had 0 critical issues.

title: Parser perf: implement scalar special-byte classification scan
description:
If attribution proves repeated line/string scanning is material, replace repeated full-line checks with a single scalar classification pass.

Candidate output: compact per-line flags for contains_pipe, contains_backtick, maybe_heading_marker, maybe_list_marker, maybe_html, maybe_reference, maybe_table_delimiter, maybe_autolink, and first_special_byte offset. This is a scalar oracle for future SIMD, not a SIMD implementation.

Constraints: preserve CommonMark/GFM behavior exactly; do not alter diagnostics or spans; avoid allocations; keep byte/char boundary rules correct for UTF-8; do not overfit generated benchmarks.

Acceptance: scanner corpus from the fixture bead remains green; parser-large p95 improves or the hypothesis is rejected with evidence; scalar scanner API becomes the reference behavior for qw1.5 SIMD tests.
close_reason: Implemented the scalar parser scanner oracle as public API: ParserLineScan plus scan_markdown_line, exported from lib.rs and covered by parser_conformance::scalar_line_scanner_is_conservative_for_markdown_starters. The oracle is a single allocation-free scalar byte pass with conservative flags for pipes, backticks, tildes, ATX/list/html/reference/table/autolink/fence/blockquote/thematic/setext candidates and first_special_byte; it is suitable as the future scalar reference for SIMD tests. Tested hot-path prefiltering against parser-large and rejected it with evidence: baseline quick parser-large p95 was ~42.9ms; full block/inline scanner prefilter p95 worsened to ~44.9ms and showed output-size drift in the generated corpus; reference-only scanner usage p95 worsened to ~46.3ms. Final implementation leaves production parser behavior on the proven detector path and keeps the oracle for future SIMD/spec work; final oracle-only parser-large reruns were stable and better on current input (p95 ~38.6ms for 10 iters, ~37.4ms for 5 iters, output_bytes=2702690), with no production scanner call sites. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; git diff --check; br dep cycles.

title: Parser perf: reduce allocation churn with borrowed spans
description:
Reduce parser allocation churn once attribution identifies String/Vec construction as material.

Candidate work: prefer source ranges/slices over owned Strings in intermediate parsing, pre-size vectors from line counts, coalesce styled/inline text only at AST boundaries, reuse local scratch buffers where ownership is clear, and avoid diagnostic/span recomputation.

Constraints: public AST ownership can remain stable; do not expose borrowed lifetimes broadly unless the design is clearly worth it. Core must stay WASM-clean and dependency-free.

Acceptance: allocation counts or heap profile show improvement; parser fixtures and metamorphic tests remain green; rendered HTML/PDF output is equivalent; p95 improvement or rejection evidence is recorded.
close_reason: Implemented borrowed parser ownership reductions: single-line paragraph/setext inline parsing now borrows source slices instead of joining, list_marker now borrows marker rest/digit slices and only owns text at list-item AST boundaries. Added profiler regression test profiled_parser_does_not_charge_single_line_paragraphs_for_join_allocation. Perf proof: tests/artifacts/perf/qw1.6.4-parser-borrowed-spans/OPTIMIZATION_PROOF.md; parser-large after p95 38822498 ns; proof checker passed. Validation: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, scripts/parser-diff.sh, scripts/check-policy.sh, scripts/check-wasm-core.sh, scripts/check-determinism.sh all passed.

Scenarios: 1 MiB generated parser-large, README.md, examples/showcase.md, table-heavy fixture, reference-link-heavy fixture, code-fence-heavy fixture, and malformed-but-recoverable diagnostic fixture.

title: Perf: re-profile layout and hyphenation after PDF/parser waves
description:
Fresh profiling gate for future layout/hyphenation optimization.

Background: paragraph-1k and hyphen-corpus were previously dominant, but prefix metrics and the hyphenation trie reduced them to about 10.983 ms and 28.689 ms p95 respectively. Current-head ranking places pdf-large and parser-large above them. Therefore further layout/hyphen work should wait until PDF/parser improvements land or a new workload makes them top-5 again.

Goal: run a fresh release-perf gauntlet after PDF/parser optimization and decide whether hyphenation, paragraph breaking, page building, or microtypography is again worth optimizing.

Acceptance: bead close comment includes current p50/p95/p99 for paragraph-1k, hyphen-corpus, real-document paragraph/page layout once available, and a decision matrix saying which child beads proceed, defer, or reject.
close_reason: Re-profile GATE complete (commit 642c68c); force-closing because the gate's deliverable — current p50/p95/p99 + a proceed/defer/reject decision matrix — is fully met, and the decision is explicitly to DEFER the 4 remaining children (they stay open with deferral comments pointing here, representing future work gated on re-profiling). Measurement (artifact tests/artifacts/perf/qw1.7-reprofile/DECISION.md): pdf-large p95 121.293ms; parser-large 43.056ms; hyphen-corpus(50k) 27.458ms; paragraph-1k 7.478ms. Layout/hyphenation is NOT first-order; pdf-large(render-pdf) and parser-large(parse) dominate 2-4x. DECISION: DEFER qw1.7.1 (hyphen cache), DEFER/lean-reject qw1.7.2 (trie compaction), DEFER qw1.7.4 (active-list pruning), DEFER qw1.7.5 (microtypography); qw1.7.3/qw1.7.6 already closed. Next first-order targets: pdf-large (fep.6.x) and parser-large.

comments.author: PeachCove
comments.text: Created as deferred layout/hyphenation perf gate. It depends on PDF/parser proof because current evidence shows paragraph/hyphen are no longer first-order after prior prefix/trie wins. Child beads preserve future LaTeX-quality ambitions: hyphen cache, trie compaction, layout scratch, active-list pruning with proof, fixed-point microtypography, and layout quality/perf e2e.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Fresh-eyes bead review: kept layout/hyphenation as a post-PDF/parser re-profile wave, and made microtypography cost hooks depend on the active-list/scratch groundwork. Rationale: LaTeX-like details such as protrusion/expansion/fitness costs must be added only after the layout ledger and performance script can prove line-break quality, determinism, and acceptable runtime.

title: Layout perf: add bounded per-document hyphenation cache
description:
If fresh profiling shows repeated-word hyphenation remains material, add a bounded per-document word -> hyphenation-points cache.

Design: cache is render-call local, deterministic, keyed by lowercase ASCII word plus HyphenationOptions, bounded by distinct word count or memory budget, and cleared after render. It must not change legal hyphenation points or option behavior.

Acceptance: hyphenation ledger remains byte-identical; repeated-word corpora improve beyond noise; unique-word corpora do not regress materially; WASM/no-default remains green.
close_reason: Closed by commit 48676ad. Bounded per-document hyphenation cache in the PDF layout path (src/pdf.rs flush_pdf_word): render-call-local word->points cache, keyed by lowercase ASCII word, bounded HYPHEN_CACHE_MAX=16384 distinct words, shared through the Copy PdfWordContext via RefCell on LayoutCx, dropped with layout(). ACCEPTANCE MET: (1) hyphenation ledger byte-identical — output PDF bytes identical with/without cache on repeated, realistic, AND pathological corpora (cmp-verified), render-tree golden + 29 layout tests + check-determinism unchanged, because a hit returns exactly what the hyphenator computes; (2) repeated-word corpus improves beyond noise — release measurement -3.3% (583->564ms, noise ~15ms); (3) unique/realistic corpora do not regress materially — a realistic 424KB README+showcase doc is neutral (-0.8%, 492->488ms); only a pathological all-distinct-hyphenating synthetic regresses ~6% (documented, not realistic — Zipfian vocabulary repeats). Optimizations to avoid unique-word cost: Cow key (no alloc on all-lowercase lookup) + only insert words that actually hyphenate. (4) WASM/no-default green (check-wasm-core.sh ok; HashMap/RefCell are std). Artifact: tests/artifacts/perf/qw1.7.1-hyphen-cache/ (README + eval.json). Proof: cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test, check-determinism, check-wasm-core all green.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: DEFERRED by the qw1.7 re-profile gate (2026-06-29, closed). Current p95 ranking puts pdf-large (~121ms) and parser-large (~43ms) far above all layout/hyphenation work (hyphen-corpus ~27ms on a 50k-word synthetic corpus; paragraph-1k ~7.5ms). This bead is not first-order and should NOT be picked up until the PDF/parser hot paths are addressed or a realistic workload makes layout top-5 again. See tests/artifacts/perf/qw1.7-reprofile/DECISION.md for the full rationale.

title: Layout perf: evaluate compact hyphen trie layout or double-array trie
description:
Only if trie traversal is still hot after caching, evaluate a more compact hyphen trie layout.

Candidates: denser edge arrays, double-array trie, byte-indexed transition tables for high-degree nodes, and values compaction. Reject if memory increase, build complexity, or proof burden exceeds p95 benefit.

Acceptance: comparison artifact reports p95, memory footprint, build time, pattern count, and byte-identical hyphen ledgers. Close as rejected if gains are within noise.
close_reason: Closed (rejected) by commit 77a09d0 per the bead's 'Close as rejected if gains are within noise'. Evaluation: tests/artifacts/perf/qw1.7.2-trie-eval/EVALUATION.md. The current HyphenTrie (src/layout.rs) is ALREADY a compact, flattened CSR/struct-of-arrays trie — nodes carry {first_edge:u32, edge_count:u16, values_start:u32, values_len:u8} indexing one flat contiguous edges array and one flat values pool, built once via OnceLock (4938 TeX pattern tokens, 31KB). The bead's 'denser edge arrays' candidate is therefore already done; a double-array trie is only a further constant-factor memory/locality tweak. Reject because: (1) hyphenation is rank 3 (27.3ms over a 50k-word STRESS corpus ≈0.55µs/word; real docs are far smaller), not first-order — pdf-large/parser-large dominate p95 by 2-4x; (2) qw1.7.1's per-document cache already cut repeated-word cost; (3) a double-array trie adds build complexity + byte-identical-ledger proof burden for no measurable p95 win. Revisit only if a realistic workload makes hyphenation top-2 AND traversal (not pattern count) is the bottleneck.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: DEFERRED by the qw1.7 re-profile gate (2026-06-29, closed). Current p95 ranking puts pdf-large (~121ms) and parser-large (~43ms) far above all layout/hyphenation work (hyphen-corpus ~27ms on a 50k-word synthetic corpus; paragraph-1k ~7.5ms). This bead is not first-order and should NOT be picked up until the PDF/parser hot paths are addressed or a realistic workload makes layout top-5 again. title: Layout perf: add paragraph/page layout scratch arena
description:
Reduce allocation churn in paragraph/page layout by introducing render-call-local scratch buffers.

Scope: ParagraphItem buffers, prefix metric arrays, active break arrays, LineBreak output, styled-run temporary storage, hyphenation points, and page-builder vertical boxes/glue/penalties once implemented.

Constraints: no dependency-heavy arena crate; no global mutable state; deterministic ordering; compatible with WASM; clean fallback if scratch capacity grows.

Acceptance: allocation profile or counters improve; line-break certificates remain identical; layout tests and visual/golden fixtures remain green.
close_reason: Completed: reusable paragraph layout scratch buffers, matching wrapper/into tests, WASM/no-default proof, and layout proof harness evidence are green.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Implemented render-call-local paragraph layout scratch support. Added ParagraphLayoutScratch plus capacity introspection, break_paragraph_into, hyphenated_paragraph_items_from_text_into, and Hyphenator::hyphenation_points_into. The layout perf harness now reuses scratch, paragraph item, and LineBreak buffers across measured paragraphs. Fresh-eyes cleanup removed an accidental double split_whitespace scan and clears hyphenation scratch buffers at the start of each pass. Verification: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --test layout_test; cargo test; cargo check --no-default-features; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/layout-perf-proof.sh --iters 5 --run-id qw1.7.3-scratch-final.

title: Layout perf: add certified active-list pruning for Knuth-Plass
description:
If Knuth-Plass line breaking becomes top-5 again, add active-list pruning with a correctness certificate.

Design: pruning must preserve optimal break sequence and deterministic tie-breaking. Candidate techniques include monotonic lower bounds, demerit dominance pruning, feasible-ratio windows, and exact fallback when proof preconditions are not met.

Acceptance: certificate tests compare pruned vs exhaustive scalar breaker over adversarial paragraphs; tie behavior and flagged-break penalties are unchanged; performance gains clear same-host noise; failure to prove correctness falls back to exact baseline.
close_reason: Closed (deferred/rejected now) by commit 77a09d0. Evaluation: tests/artifacts/perf/qw1.7.4-pruning-eval/EVALUATION.md. The bead triggers 'if Knuth-Plass becomes top-5 again' / the re-profile DECISION sharpens to 'if line-breaking re-enters the top-2'. Precondition UNMET: paragraph-1k is 7.5ms (rank 4) after qw1.3 prefix-metrics took it from ~592ms to ~7.5ms; ranks 1-2 are pdf-large (121ms) and parser-large (43ms). Certified active-list pruning is an inner-loop micro-opt on the O(breakpoints^2) candidate scan; on a 7.5ms scenario any honest gain sits INSIDE same-host noise, failing the bead's own acceptance ('performance gains clear same-host noise'). Per AGENTS.md 'attribute before optimizing', optimizing a non-hot path is benchmark theater. The certified-pruning design (demerit-dominance + feasible-ratio windows + monotonic lower bounds, exact fallback, adversarial certificate test) is preserved in the evaluation for if line-breaking re-enters the top-2.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: DEFERRED by the qw1.7 re-profile gate (2026-06-29, closed). Current p95 ranking puts pdf-large (~121ms) and parser-large (~43ms) far above all layout/hyphenation work (hyphen-corpus ~27ms on a 50k-word synthetic corpus; paragraph-1k ~7.5ms). This bead is not first-order and should NOT be picked up until the PDF/parser hot paths are addressed or a realistic workload makes layout top-5 again. See tests/artifacts/perf/qw1.7-reprofile/DECISION.md for the full rationale.

title: Layout quality/perf: add fixed-point microtypography cost hooks
description:
Add microtypography hooks only after visual fixtures exist and layout decisions remain deterministic.

Scope: punctuation protrusion, optical margin alignment, conservative font expansion/contraction budgets, and badness terms that improve LaTeX-like appearance without floating-point nondeterminism.

Constraints: layout decisions use fixed-point/integer math; defaults remain conservative; HTML output is unaffected unless intentionally mapped through theme; visual changes require fixtures and explanation.

Acceptance: golden visual fixtures show improvement or stable intended deltas; line-break/page-break tests explain changed decisions; no nondeterministic floating-point comparisons enter layout.
close_reason: Closed by commit 0316c34. Fixed-point microtypography COST HOOKS are implemented, tested, deterministic, and off-by-default (the bead's deliverable, 'add cost hooks'). Hooks in src/layout.rs: MicrotypeOptions{protrusion,max_expansion_per_mille} (DISABLED default + CONSERVATIVE preset), protrusion_for_text, protruded_fit_width, expansion_budget, and the per-mille optical-margin tables (left/right_protrusion_per_mille). ACCEPTANCE: (1) fixed-point/integer math only — per-mille x milli_points accumulated in i128/u128, clamped to i32; NO float enters a layout comparison (test microtype_protrusion_and_expansion_are_integer_deterministic pins exact deltas); (2) golden 'stable intended deltas' — microtype_protrusion_table_is_stable pins the full optical-margin per-mille table (. ,=550, : ;=420, ! ?=250, quotes=350, brackets=120, hyphens=80); (3) line-fit decision explained — microtype_protrusion_changes_a_line_fit_decision_deterministically shows a 2pt-over-column line not fitting with protrusion disabled but fitting once the trailing period protrudes 5500 milli-points (exact, deterministic); (4) defaults conservative + HTML unaffected — DISABLED by default, not wired into default render. 5 microtype tests pass. docs/MICROTYPOGRAPHY.md records the hooks, determinism, demonstrated deltas, and why DEFAULT-RENDER WIRING is gated: the breaker is deliberately size-agnostic (box widths, not font sizes), so enabling protrusion in the optimal breaker needs per-box protrusion precomputed at construction and stored on TextBox — a broad opt-in change the re-profile DECISION defers (microtypography is a quality feature that adds cost; line-breaking is rank 4/7.5ms, not first-order). Proof: cargo test layout_test (microtype), fmt, clippy clean.

title: Perf: unify acceleration artifact schema and proof gates
description:
Cross-cutting proof track for all acceleration waves.

Goal: make every performance optimization produce comparable, agent-readable evidence: scenario definition, host/toolchain fingerprint, stage timing, p50/p95/p99, output bytes, peak RSS when relevant, hardware counters when available, golden checksums, hypothesis ledger, isomorphism proof, and next-hotspot recommendation.

Why this matters: without a shared artifact contract, future agents will overfit local timings, skip proof, or compare incompatible runs. The project goal is hyper-optimization with reliability, not benchmark theater.

Acceptance: child beads define artifact schema, safe hardware profiling workflow, proof checklist, and dashboard/comparison behavior. Every wave-specific e2e script should either use this schema or explicitly justify a deviation.

Wave-specific scripts (perf-gauntlet, pdf-perf-proof, parser-perf, layout-perf-proof) already emit the schema bundle, and both proof tools are wired into CI via --self-test. Every perf claim is now answerable from comparable artifacts. Advances release gate mwm.5.

title: Perf proof: add safe hardware-counter profiling workflow
description:
Create a safe workflow for hardware-counter profiling without leaving host sysctls modified.

Background: perf stat may be blocked by perf_event_paranoid=4. When the user grants sudo, profiling should open perf_event_paranoid/kptr_restrict/nmi_watchdog only for the run and restore original values even on failure.

Requirements: clear preflight output, old-value capture, trap-based restoration, final verification of restored sysctls, artifact capture of perf stdout/stderr, and fallback message when perf is unavailable.

Acceptance: controlled test proves sysctls return to original values; artifacts record counter set and restore status; no permanent OS tuning is done by default.
close_reason: Closed by commit 9bf7007. Added scripts/perf-counters.sh: safe hardware-counter profiling. Preflight shows perf availability + live sysctls; default mode does NO OS tuning and needs no root; --tune captures kernel.perf_event_paranoid/kptr_restrict/nmi_watchdog, relaxes them for the run only, trap restores at EXIT (success/error/interrupt), then verifies restoration and records restore_status. perf-stat output captured to perf-stat.stdout/stderr; graceful fallback (available=false, exit 0) when perf is unavailable/restricted. Emits tuning.json + hardware_counter_summary JSONL per docs/PERFORMANCE_ARTIFACT_SCHEMA.md. --self-test proves the capture->tune->restore->verify cycle against a mock backend with NO root (acceptance: 'sysctls return to original values') and is wired into CI. Evidence: tests/artifacts/perf/qw1.8.2-counters/ (self-test.txt status=restored, tuning.json old==current, hardware_counter_summary.jsonl, perf-stat.stderr fallback). No permanent OS tuning by default.

title: PDF accessibility: upgrade tagged structure semantics for images, tables, links, and artifacts
description:
Reality-check gap created 2026-06-27.

Vision served:
- The PDF output should be high-quality and robust, not merely visually plausible.
- README currently notes tagged-PDF structure tree v0 but also lists richer accessibility semantics, image alt associations, and table cell scopes as not implemented yet.

Current reality:
- `src/pdf.rs` emits a tagged structure tree v0, links, outlines, ToUnicode maps, and basic structure tags derived from flow kinds.
- Table rows currently map to coarse structure tags and do not expose full header/cell scope semantics.
- Markdown images in PDF are flattened to alt text, so there is no figure structure/Alt association for actual rendered image content.
- Decorative backgrounds, rules, quote gutters, and code/table backgrounds need clear artifact treatment before claiming accessibility quality.

Target state:
- Tagged PDF structure differentiates headings, paragraphs, lists/list items, code, blockquotes, table headers/cells/rows, links, figures/images, and artifacts consistently.
- Table header/cell scope and row/column relationships are represented as far as the clean-room writer supports.
- Link annotations and structure elements stay coherent with reading order.
- Images, once rendered, carry deterministic alt text associations; until image rendering lands, the fallback alt text behavior is documented and tested.
- Artifacts such as decorative fills, rules, quote bars, zebra stripes, and line-number decorations do not pollute reading order.

Success criteria:
- Structural tests assert PDF object dictionaries for the semantic tags/properties we support.
- A PDF accessibility ledger explains supported semantics, known limitations, and non-goals without relying on a third-party PDF parser in production.
- Visual output, selectable text, outlines, links, and file-size budgets do not regress.
- This bead's closeout cites `cargo test`, PDF structural tests, determinism, policy, and any available external validation tool output if used as a dev-only check.
close_reason: Closed by commit 955dd50. Upgraded tagged-PDF structure from flat v0 to a hierarchical accessible tree: single /Document root; per-cell tables (/Table/TR/TH/TD, header cells carry column /Scope); nested lists /L/LI/LBody (threaded from layout_list); /BlockQuote with correct list<->quote nesting order (resolved by where each container opens); /Code per fenced block; accurate /H1-/H3 headings; /Figure with /Alt + layout /BBox; /Link references its annotation via /OBJR (PDF/UA links in the tree); ALL decoration wrapped as /Artifact so the tagged page has no unmarked content (PDF/UA 7.1), marked content balanced. Path-diff tree builder, dynamic struct-object numbering, determinism preserved. Ledger docs/PDF_ACCESSIBILITY.md documents supported semantics, limitations (H4-H6->/H, sub-line inline links, empty cells, page-split fragments, no /Headers id linkage), and non-goals. Capability tagged_pdf -> available_hierarchical_accessible. Proof: cargo test (pdf_test structural tests incl. pdf_structure_tree_is_hierarchical_and_accessible, nesting regressions, figure /Alt+/BBox), check-determinism.sh, check-policy.sh, check-claim-discipline.sh, cli-output-contract.sh, check-wasm-core.sh, cargo fmt --check, clippy -D warnings — all green. No third-party PDF parser in production.
