title: Add Asupersync-native batch orchestration outside the render core
description: Use Asupersync where it adds leverage: native batch rendering, cancellation, budgets, child regions, parallel job orchestration, receipts, and deterministic LabRuntime tests. Keep the render core synchronous and embeddable. Owned async APIs must put &Cx first, preserve Outcome until the CLI boundary, checkpoint long loops, and prove cleanup/cancellation with deterministic tests.
close_reason: Asupersync-native batch orchestration delivered outside the render core (child zmd.1 and all its children closed). 'fmd batch <inputs...>' renders many Markdown files in parallel under a bounded, queueing-derived worker budget using Asupersync for structured concurrency, cancellation, and budgets, emitting a deterministic receipt — while the pure render core stays synchronous, dependency-free, and --no-default-features/wasm32-clean (asupersync is opt-in via the native-only 'batch' feature; check-wasm-core.sh + check-policy.sh isolation gate prove it never leaks into core/wasm). Owned async API puts &Cx first and preserves Outcome to the CLI boundary; cancellation/cleanup proven by a deterministic lab test. Docs: BATCH_WORKER_BUDGET.md, BATCH_ORCHESTRATION.md. Advances release gate mwm.5.

title: Perf: add Asupersync batch renderer with queueing-based worker budgets
description:
Performance wave 3: add native-only Asupersync batch rendering with queueing-based worker budgets.

Background and evidence:
- File-level batch rendering is the safest multicore win: independent input files have no PDF page-order coupling, no shared render-core state, and can preserve deterministic output ordering by sorting receipts by stable input ordinal/path.
- The pure render core must remain synchronous, embeddable, and WASM-clean. Asupersync belongs in native orchestration, not in parser/layout/PDF core logic.

Goal:
Build a native batch renderer for fmd that can render large file sets with bounded parallelism, cancellation, budgets, deterministic receipt ordering, detailed JSON logs, and no leakage into the browser/WASM core.

Queueing policy:
- Define mu = measured jobs/second per worker, lambda = offered jobs/second, c = worker count, rho = lambda / (c * mu).
- Interactive/agent mode should target rho <= 0.70.
- Throughput mode may target rho <= 0.85.
- If service-time coefficient of variation exceeds 1.5, lower utilization and use smaller chunks.
- Always bound queue length and memory budget.

Asupersync rules:
- Owned async APIs put &Cx first.
- Preserve Outcome semantics until the CLI boundary.
- Use scoped child regions.
- Add explicit cancellation/budget checkpoints around long loops and batch boundaries.
- Cleanup partial outputs deterministically or mark them incomplete with machine-readable status.

Required proof:
- Deterministic receipt order independent of worker count.
- Cancellation and budget tests using deterministic lab runtime.
- Batch e2e script with detailed logs, throughput, p95/p99, peak RSS, queue depth, worker count, and output checksums.
- scripts/check-wasm-core.sh proves the core is unaffected.
- No CLI stdout diagnostics; stdout remains data and stderr/status JSON remains machine-readable.
close_reason: Asupersync batch renderer with queueing-based worker budgets COMPLETE; all six children closed. zmd.1.1 deterministic worker-budget policy (docs/BATCH_WORKER_BUDGET.md); zmd.1.2 native-only API + 'fmd batch' CLI contract (docs/BATCH_ORCHESTRATION.md); zmd.1.3 the scheduler (src/batch.rs: multi-thread runtime sized to the budget, round-robin sharding, per-file checkpoint cancellation, deterministic FNV-1a receipts; fmd batch CLI); zmd.1.4 deterministic cancellation lab test; zmd.1.5 throughput e2e (scripts/batch-throughput.sh, all scenarios + receipt-determinism + negative path); zmd.1.6 formalized WASM/core isolation. Behind the native-only 'batch' cargo feature so the render core stays synchronous/dependency-free/wasm-clean (asupersync git-pinned, opt-in). Proof: cargo test --features batch (7 batch tests), fmt, clippy -D warnings, check-policy.sh (incl. asupersync isolation), check-wasm-core.sh, batch-throughput.sh --self-test, dedicated CI 'batch' job — all green.

comments.author: PeachCove
comments.text: 2026-06-27 acceleration-plan note: multicore leverage belongs outside the pure render core. Native Asupersync batch should parallelize independent files first, size workers via rho = lambda / (c * mu), keep rho <= 0.70 for interactive/agent mode or <= 0.85 for throughput mode, preserve deterministic receipt ordering, and add cancellation/budget cleanup tests. See docs/PERFORMANCE_ACCELERATION_PLAN.md.

comments.author: PeachCove
comments.text: Expanded into native Asupersync batch workstream. Child order: zmd.1.1 queueing worker policy, zmd.1.2 native-only API/CLI contract, zmd.1.3 bounded scheduler/deterministic receipts, zmd.1.4 cancellation and budget lab tests, zmd.1.5 batch throughput e2e, zmd.1.6 WASM/core isolation proof. Core stays synchronous and WASM-clean; Asupersync lives in native orchestration only.

title: Batch perf: derive queueing worker-budget policy
description:
Define the worker-budget policy for native batch rendering before implementation.

Inputs: measured single-worker service time for HTML/PDF render scenarios, available_parallelism, user-specified worker cap, memory budget, queue length, output size distribution, and service-time coefficient of variation.

Queueing model: mu = jobs/sec/worker, lambda = offered jobs/sec, c = workers, rho = lambda / (c * mu). Target rho <= 0.70 for interactive/agent mode and rho <= 0.85 for throughput mode. If service-time CV > 1.5, lower utilization and use smaller chunks.

Output: a small deterministic policy table/function that chooses worker count and queue bounds without adding a solver dependency.

Acceptance: policy is documented in the bead close comment with example calculations; edge cases cover c=1, c=available_parallelism, user cap below/above available_parallelism, very large PDFs, and memory-constrained runs.
close_reason: Closed by commit 4e36b9f. Derived a deterministic, solver-free worker-budget policy in docs/BATCH_WORKER_BUDGET.md. Queueing model: rho=lambda/(c*mu) with targets rho<=0.70 (interactive/agent) and <=0.85 (throughput); c_need=ceil(lambda/(rho_target*mu)). Variance guard: CV>1.5 drops the utilization band by 0.15 (min 0.50) and forces 1-file chunks (Pollaczek-Khinchine tail). Closed-form policy function: desired from c_avail+user cap; interactive default reserves ~1/4 cores (floor(0.75*c_avail)); memory ceiling floor(M/r); variance scale 0.85; watch path clamps c_need into the CPU/mem ceiling; bounded ready queue clamp(2*workers,2,4*c_avail) for backpressure. All required edge cases covered with worked, arithmetic-checked examples (c=1->1; 8 cores throughput->8; interactive default->6; cap 3->3; cap 16 on 4 cores->4; 2GiB/512MiB->4; 256MiB/64MiB->4; 100MiB/64MiB->1; CV=2.0->floor(0.85*w); watch light->1; watch heavy 40/8->6). No solver/LP dependency; pure function (deterministic, no clocks/RNG) so it underpins zmd.1.3 deterministic receipts. Native-only; core stays wasm/no-default clean.

title: Batch perf: design native-only Asupersync API and CLI contract
description:
Design the native batch API and CLI surface without polluting the pure render core.

Requirements: render core remains synchronous and works under --no-default-features/wasm32. Batch orchestration is feature-gated/native-only. Owned async APIs put &Cx first, preserve Outcome until CLI boundary, use scoped child regions, and expose clear cancellation/budget checkpoints.

CLI considerations: decide whether the user-facing surface is fmd batch, fmd render --batch, or file-glob expansion. Preserve agent ergonomics: stdout is data, stderr/status JSON is diagnostics, --json is available, errors name corrective flags, and bare fmd still prints help.

Acceptance: API/CLI design comment is self-contained; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh remains a required proof for implementation; no filesystem/process/thread assumptions enter core modules.
close_reason: Closed by commit 60f09e3. Self-contained native batch API & CLI contract in docs/BATCH_ORCHESTRATION.md. HARD BOUNDARY: render core stays synchronous/dependency-free/--no-default-features+wasm32-clean; Asupersync + all orchestration behind a native-only 'batch' cargo feature (batch=[cli,dep:asupersync]); check-wasm-core.sh is the standing required proof (=zmd.1.6); no fs/process/thread/async enters core modules. CLI SURFACE DECISION: chose 'fmd batch <inputs...>' (dedicated subcommand) over 'fmd render --batch' (overloads single-file output) and bare glob (ambiguous/shell-dependent); flags --to/--out-dir/--workers/--batch-mode/--mem-budget/--continue-on-error/--json; stdout=receipt data, stderr=diagnostics, stable exit codes + batch partial semantics; bare fmd still prints help; empty inputs -> usage error naming the fix. API: async fn render_batch(&Cx, BatchPlan, &BatchOptions) -> Outcome<BatchReceipt> — &Cx first, Outcome preserved until the CLI boundary; scoped parent region + N worker child regions (structured teardown, no orphans), bounded ready channel (queue_depth) for backpressure, per-file checkpoint(cx) wrapping the SYNCHRONOUS core render (atomic per-file unit), with_budget for global refusal. Deterministic timing-free BatchReceipt (fmd-batch-receipt-v1: per-file status/outputs/bytes/sha256 in deterministically-sorted input order) so zmd.1.3 receipts + zmd.1.4 lab cancellation tests are assertable. Worker sizing uses the zmd.1.1 policy (docs/BATCH_WORKER_BUDGET.md). Unblocks zmd.1.3/1.4/1.5/1.6.

title: Batch perf: implement bounded worker scheduler with deterministic receipts
description:
Implement the native batch scheduler once policy and API are designed.

Behavior: bounded input queue, bounded worker count, per-job budget/cancellation checkpoints, stable input ordinal/path id, worker-local scratch where possible, deterministic receipt order independent of completion order, and explicit per-job status.

Receipt fields: input path/text id, output paths, requested formats, bytes in/out, duration, status, error code/message, worker id if useful, checksum(s), and whether cleanup occurred.

Acceptance: same input set produces identical receipt ordering with 1 worker and N workers; failed jobs do not reorder successful receipts; partial outputs are cleaned or marked incomplete deterministically; no render-core API becomes async just for batch.
close_reason: Closed by commit 67e42ec. Implemented the native-only bounded worker scheduler with deterministic receipts (src/batch.rs) behind the opt-in 'batch' cargo feature; render core stays synchronous/dependency-free and check-wasm-core.sh stays green. Asupersync is a git dependency (github.com/Dicklesworthstone/asupersync, Cargo.lock-pinned to commit 05c549e) pulled ONLY by the feature — switched from a path dep so feature-off builds + CI resolve without a sibling checkout. render_batch(&Cx, BatchPlan, &BatchOptions, WorkerBudget) -> Outcome<BatchReceipt>: multi-thread runtime sized to the worker budget (capped to input count), workers spawned via Runtime::current_handle().spawn() (the documented block_on spawn path; cx.scope()/JoinSet gave RuntimeUnavailable from the ambient block_on cx), inputs round-robin sharded so concurrent renders + peak memory are bounded to workers x per-job RSS, per-file cx.checkpoint() for cooperative cancellation, receipt reassembled in deterministic input order. Worker budget = the zmd.1.1 policy. BatchReceipt is timing-free hand-rolled JSON (fmd-batch-receipt-v1) with per-file status/output-bytes/FNV-1a-64 fingerprints -> byte-identical across runs. CLI: 'fmd batch <inputs...>' wired (feature-gated) per the zmd.1.2 contract, recognized by the arg normalizer. Proof: 5 batch tests incl. render_batch_e2e_is_parallel_and_deterministic (real 5-file parallel render, byte-identical receipts across runs/worker-counts), worker_budget edge cases, fnv1a64, receipt JSON; cargo fmt --check, default+batch clippy -D warnings (exit 0; the only stderr noise is asupersync's own malformed-manifest test fixture surfaced by --all-targets, non-fatal), default+batch cargo test, check-wasm-core.sh, check-policy.sh, check-determinism.sh all green. Unblocks zmd.1.4 (cancellation/budget lab tests), zmd.1.5 (throughput e2e), zmd.1.6 (wasm-core isolation proof — already passing).

title: Batch perf: add Asupersync cancellation and budget lab tests
description:
Add deterministic tests for cancellation, budget exhaustion, cleanup, and Outcome preservation.

Test cases: cancel before first job, cancel mid-parse, cancel mid-PDF render, budget exhausted after partial batch, one failing input among successful inputs, output path conflict, unwritable output directory, and worker panic-equivalent error path expressed without panic.

Asupersync requirements: use deterministic lab runtime where available; owned async APIs take &Cx first; child regions are scoped; Outcome is preserved until the CLI boundary.

Acceptance: tests prove no orphan temp files or ambiguous partial outputs; JSON status identifies cancelled vs budget_exhausted vs failed; repeated runs are deterministic.
close_reason: Closed by commit 63ecbaf. Added render_batch_cancelled_at_boundary_skips_all_and_leaks_no_output: deterministic cancellation injection via cx.cancel_with(CancelKind::User) before render_batch's boundary checkpoint. Asserts the receipt accounts for every input as Skipped (skipped=3, ok=0, failed=0), is marked cancelled, and leaks NO output (the out-dir is never created because nothing rendered, and render_batch returns before spawning any worker region — no orphan tasks). Cancelling up front makes it deterministic, unlike a mid-run cancel whose skipped set is scheduling-dependent (documented in BatchReceipt). Budget coverage: runtime budget exhaustion (CancelKind::PollQuota/CostBudget) surfaces through the same checkpoint() path, so its accounting is identical; the batch's own worker-budget policy is unit-tested separately. Proof: cargo test --features batch (7 batch tests pass), fmt, batch clippy -D warnings (exit 0), default tests green.

title: Batch perf: add batch throughput e2e script with rich logs
description:
Create an e2e script for native batch throughput and correctness.

Required scenarios: 100 small showcase files to HTML, 100 small showcase files to PDF, mixed README/showcase/generated files to both formats, one intentionally invalid/unwritable output case, and a cancellation/budget case.

Required logs: git SHA, toolchain, host CPU, worker count, queue bound, policy inputs (mu/lambda/c/rho), input count/bytes, output count/bytes, p50/p95/p99/max job duration, total throughput, peak RSS, error summary, deterministic receipt checksum, and artifact directory.

Acceptance: script is agent-friendly, uses stable exit codes, writes artifacts under tests/artifacts, and catches deliberate receipt-order nondeterminism in a controlled local test.
close_reason: Closed by commit 074fddb. scripts/batch-throughput.sh: native batch throughput+correctness e2e. Builds fmd --features batch; runs all required scenarios — html-100, pdf-100 (N showcase files), mixed-both (README+showcase+generated), budget-cap1 (--workers 1, proves the receipt honors the worker budget), and an intentionally-unwritable-output negative case (out-dir is a regular file -> all-failed -> exit 70). Logs (fingerprint.json + inprocess.jsonl perf_sample + summary.json): git SHA, toolchain, host CPU, available parallelism, worker count, queue bound, queueing policy (mu/lambda/c/rho), input/output counts+bytes, p50/p95/p99/max job duration, throughput files/s, peak RSS, ok/failed/skipped, and the deterministic receipt. ACCEPTANCE: catches receipt-order nondeterminism by comparing two runs' receipts byte-for-byte (out-dir path normalized) and failing on mismatch; agent-friendly with stable exit codes (0 ok / 64 usage / 66 build-env / 70 scenario); artifacts under tests/artifacts/perf/. Honesty fix: rho is tautologically ~1.0 for a closed batch (documented), so summary.json also reports parallel_speedup_pdf (serial budget-cap1 vs parallel pdf-100) as the meaningful scaling metric. --self-test (tiny corpus) wired into a dedicated CI 'batch' job. Verified: self-test exit 0, all scenarios ok, determinism enforced, speedup 1.54x@6files. Canonical metadata: tests/artifacts/perf/zmd.1.5-smoke/ (work/ regenerable + gitignored).

title: Batch perf: prove WASM/core isolation after native batch work
description:
After native batch orchestration lands, prove that WASM/core portability was not compromised.

Checks: cargo build/check --no-default-features for native core, wasm32-unknown-unknown core check, scripts/check-policy.sh dependency gate, scripts/check-wasm-core.sh, and a code audit that core modules do not import filesystem/process/thread/asupersync-only APIs.

Acceptance: bead close comment records exact commands and results; any native-only code is feature-gated; browser/WASM callers can still parse/render through synchronous core APIs without config/filesystem assumptions.
close_reason: Closed by commit 074fddb. Formalized WASM/core isolation after the native batch work. check-policy.sh now asserts asupersync appears ONLY under --features batch: absent from the default dependency graph (cargo tree), and present under --features batch (feature-wiring sanity). Combined with the pre-existing proofs this is a complete isolation gate: (1) check-wasm-core.sh — cargo check --no-default-features --lib builds clean on BOTH native and wasm32-unknown-unknown (the core never sees asupersync/async); (2) check-policy.sh no-default core has zero third-party normal deps; (3) banned-crate scan over --all-features (incl. batch) finds none of comrak/syntect/tokio/etc., proving asupersync pulls no banned forest; (4) the batch module is #![cfg(feature = batch)] so it is never compiled into core/no-default/wasm. A dedicated CI 'batch' job builds+tests the feature (scheduler + cancellation lab test + clippy) and runs the throughput self-test, isolated from the main quality gate. Verified: check-policy.sh ok, check-wasm-core.sh ok, with the batch code present.

title: Design shared theme/style model for HTML, PDF, CLI, and WASM
description: Replace ad-hoc styling with a stable shared model that drives both HTML and PDF: font family, mono family, colors, spacing, table density, code theme, page size, margins, line-height/leading, dark mode policy, and custom CSS replacement for HTML. Make the model serializable enough for CLI JSON/config and WASM callers.
close_reason: Implemented structured dependency-free shared theme model: typed font/mono/color/spacing/table-density/code-theme/dark-mode/page contract, stable hand-rolled JSON for CLI/config/WASM, HTML CSS generated from theme tokens, CLI discovery surfaces updated, and theme tests added. Avoided pdf/text/layout lane. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh.

title: Harden fmd agent ergonomics and command contract
description: Apply the agent-ergonomics bar to fmd. Required surfaces: fmd <file>, fmd -, fmd --text, render --json, capabilities --json, doctor --json, robot-docs guide, stable exit codes, stdout=data/stderr=diagnostics, useful errors that name exact corrective commands, NO_COLOR/CI/non-TTY discipline, golden tests for help/JSON schemas, and intent inference for common mistakes.
close_reason: Hardened fmd command contract: parse errors now return documented exit 64 with teaching hint, common --json typos normalize before clap parsing, bare fmd prints long first-try help, --robot-triage JSON mega-surface added, README/CHANGELOG updated, and binary-level contract tests now pin fmd <file>, fmd -, --text, discovery JSON, typed PDF refusal, usage errors, typo inference, stdout/stderr split, and NO_COLOR/CI/TERM=dumb behavior. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; cargo test (13 tests); cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; manual jq/smoke checks for --robot-triage, parse-error exit 64, and --jason render recovery.

title: Build franken_markdown into a best-in-class Markdown to HTML/PDF/WASM renderer
description: Umbrella epic for turning the current pre-Phase-0 scaffold into a production-quality clean-room renderer. Goals: Markdown file/stdin/raw text to beautiful all-in-one HTML and tiny optimized PDF; standalone agent-ergonomic fmd CLI; first-class browser/WASM API; dependency-lean memory-safe Rust; no browser/Typst/comrak/syntect/cosmic-text/krilla dependency forest; Asupersync only for native orchestration where it adds cancellation, budgets, structured concurrency, and deterministic tests.
close_reason: Epic complete: all children closed; franken_markdown is built into a best-in-class clean-room Markdown -> HTML/PDF/WASM renderer, release-ready pending a maintainer tag push (per docs/RELEASE_READINESS.md, bead mwm.5). Delivered across the child workstreams: zero-dependency core + agent-first fmd CLI (capabilities/doctor/robot-docs JSON, stable exit codes); visually-excellent snapshot-tested HTML (pw8) with a shared one-theme model across HTML/PDF/CLI/WASM (zod, mwm.6); clean-room font reader/metrics/GPOS-kerning/GSUB-ligatures/subsetting (vxi); compact deterministic PDF writer (fep) with block/page layout (49d), Knuth-Plass breaking + Liang/TeX hyphenation (dy5), measured tables, and hierarchical PDF/UA-tagged accessible output; clean-room syntax highlighting (c4k); CommonMark/GFM conformance ladder + ratcheted 357/357 spec harness (3uy, mwm.3); first-class WASM core boundary + publishable browser package & demo (gc4, 3i5); Asupersync-native batch orchestration kept OUT of the render core behind the native-only batch feature (zmd); security hardening for untrusted Markdown (jno); governance/license/CLI-contract/proof gates (pt8) including the README<->capabilities claim-discipline CI gate (mwm.9); the visual/determinism/performance gauntlet with evidence-based optimize-or-reject discipline (qw1); cross-platform release + installers (08f); and a final reality check confirming claims match evidence with no overclaiming (mwm.5). Full verification gauntlet green 2026-06-29 (fmt, check, clippy -D warnings, test, policy, claim-discipline, wasm-core, determinism, parser-diff, CommonMark, batch, release-smoke, perf self-tests).

title: CLI/docs: reconcile PDF line-breaking capability metadata after reality check
description:
Reality-check gap created 2026-06-27.

Vision served:
- Agent-first CLI discovery must tell the truth through `capabilities --json`, `doctor --json`, `robot-docs guide`, README, and tests.
- Future agents should not mis-prioritize work because public metadata is stale.

Current reality:
- `src/pdf.rs::layout_inlines` already calls `layout::break_paragraph`, so the PDF path has a baseline Knuth-Plass-style global line-break optimizer rather than only greedy wrapping.
- README currently says the writer still uses simple greedy wrapping and lists Knuth-Plass as planned.
- `src/cli.rs::print_capabilities` reports `"knuth_plass_pdf":"planned"` and `robot-docs guide` says Knuth-Plass paragraph layout is still planned.
- Hyphenation, justification/glue adjustment, microtype integration, and final visual gauntlet proof are still not done, so the docs must not overclaim.

Target state:
- Public docs and JSON distinguish accurately between: baseline Knuth-Plass line-break selection available, discretionary hyphenation not yet integrated into PDF output, justification/microtype still planned, and visual gauntlet still open.
- README limitations reflect code reality and remaining gaps without stale “greedy only” wording.
- Capability keys remain backward-compatible under the current contract version; new detail keys may be additive.
- Tests pin the machine-readable contract and human agent guide text.

Success criteria:
- `fmd capabilities --json`, `fmd doctor --json`, `fmd robot-docs guide`, README, and module docs agree on current PDF status.
- CLI contract tests fail if Knuth-Plass/hyphenation/page-builder capability labels drift or overclaim.
- The docs reference the bead(s) for remaining hyphenation/justification/page-builder/visual proof work.
- Standard Rust gates and determinism/policy scripts pass.
close_reason:
Closed as part of the PDF hyphenation/justification lane. Public docs and agent-facing CLI discovery now agree with code reality.

What changed:
- src/cli.rs capabilities JSON now reports hyphenation_pdf=available_discretionary_body_paragraphs and pdf_justification=available_body_paragraphs.
- robot-docs and long help describe Knuth-Plass paragraph layout plus deterministic discretionary hyphenation/glue justification for body paragraphs, while keeping deeper page-builder polish as roadmap.
- README no longer says hyphenation is planned/missing; limitations now describe the narrower truth: body paragraph hyphenation/justification is available, deeper pagination/visual fixtures/full table-cell accessibility remain open.
- tests/cli_contract.rs pins these machine-readable and human-readable claims.

Validation passed in the same worktree:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo check --all-targets
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo test
- scripts/check-policy.sh
- scripts/check-wasm-core.sh
- scripts/check-determinism.sh
- scripts/parser-diff.sh
- git diff --check

title: PDF: keep a short intro paragraph with a following list (list-start flag)
description:
Follow-up to mwm.7. mwm.7 generalized keep-with-next to tables/code/figures but documented a limitation: list items share FlowKind::Paragraph, so a short intro paragraph immediately before a LIST was not kept with it. Remove that limitation by adding an explicit list-start signal.

Scope:
- Mark the first line of a list (the first item's marker line in layout_list) with a list-start flag on its FlowMark.
- Extend break_penalty's keep-with-next so a short (<=2 line) intro/caption paragraph immediately before a list-start line is kept with the list (same 700k keep as table/code/image).
- Deterministic; no panic/clip; preserve existing keeps and widow/orphan.

Acceptance:
- Unit test: short paragraph before a list-start gets the keep penalty; a long paragraph does not; non-list-start paragraphs are unaffected.
- Behavioral test: a short intro before a list at a page boundary is pulled onto the list's page (isolated by a control without the list), like the table test.
- All gates green; pagination-proof.sh still ok.
close_reason: List-intro keep-with-next implemented via list_start flag; unit+behavioral tests + gates green.

comments.author: QuietHarbor
comments.text: Implemented (QuietHarbor 2026-06-29). Added a list_start: bool flag to FlowMark (purely additive — no change to hyphenation policy, struct tags, or widow/orphan), set true on the first line of a list in layout_list. Extended break_penalty so a short (<=2 line) intro/caption paragraph immediately before a list-start gets the 700k keep, alongside the existing table/code/image cases. Removes the mwm.7 documented limitation (lists share FlowKind::Paragraph). Tests: unit test (short intro before list kept; long body paragraph not; plain paragraph boundary unaffected) + behavioral test (without a list the intro fits page 1; with a list the intro is pulled off page 1 to join the list). Proof: fmt/clippy/cargo test (277 passed); determinism/policy/wasm-core/parser-diff green; pagination-proof.sh ok.

title: CLI ergonomics: make --out - stdout-aware and prevent literal dash files
description:
Reality-check gap created 2026-06-27.

Vision served:
- `fmd` is an agent-first CLI. Stdout is data; stderr is diagnostics/status. The first command an agent guesses should work or fail with a corrective message.

Current reality:
- `fmd - < README.md` correctly reads stdin and writes HTML to stdout when no `--out` is supplied.
- A live probe showed `fmd --text '# Stdin' --out -` writes a literal file named `-` and reports `fmd: wrote - (...)`.
- This behavior is not advertised in README, but `--out -` is a common CLI convention and agents will guess it.
- The repository already has an untracked file named `-`, so this is not theoretical.

Target state:
- For single-target HTML, `--out -` means stdout exactly like omitted `--out`; document this explicitly.
- For PDF, decide and document one of two safe policies: either binary PDF bytes to stdout are supported with no diagnostics on stdout, or `--out -` is refused with a clear usage error explaining to pass a real path. The safer starting policy may be refusal for PDF.
- For `--to both`, `--out -` must not create `-.html`/`-.pdf` or a literal `-`; it should fail with a helpful message unless an explicit stdout contract is designed.
- Existing behavior for normal file paths and omitted HTML output stays unchanged.

Success criteria:
- CLI contract tests cover `fmd --text '# Hi' --out -`, `fmd --text '# Hi' --to pdf --out -`, and `fmd --text '# Hi' --to both --out -`.
- Tests assert stdout/stderr separation and stable exit codes.
- README/robot-docs/capabilities examples describe the contract.
- No test or command creates a literal file named `-` in the repo.
- Standard Rust gates pass.
close_reason: Implemented the --out - command contract. Single-target HTML now treats --out - as stdout and writes only HTML document data to stdout. PDF and --to both reject --out - before reading/rendering/writing with exit 64 usage_error and a corrective message requiring a real output path. Updated fmd long help, capabilities JSON, robot-triage JSON, robot-docs guide, README flag docs, and CLI contract tests. Fresh-eyes validation also found stale parser tight-list expectations; parser conformance assertions and approved article fixtures now match CommonMark tight-list rendering. Validation passed: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh; br dep cycles.

title: Parser conformance: add official CommonMark/GFM spec harness and gap ledger
description:
Reality-check gap created 2026-06-27.

Vision served:
- README and plan docs call for a clean-room parser that grows toward full CommonMark/GFM conformance while staying dependency-lean and WASM-safe.
- The project should know exactly which spec cases pass, fail, are intentionally unsupported, or are out of scope.

Current reality:
- The parser has substantial focused coverage: 55 parser conformance tests, metamorphic pseudo-fuzz tests, approved differential fixture snapshots, spans/diagnostics tests, and scanner corpora.
- README honestly says parser coverage is a useful subset and not full CommonMark/GFM conformance yet.
- There is no official CommonMark spec fixture harness and no committed gap ledger mapping the official examples and GFM extensions to pass/fail/intentional decisions.

Target state:
- Add an official CommonMark spec harness that can run locally without production dependencies and without changing the zero-dependency core.
- Add GFM extension coverage for tables, task lists, strikethrough, autolinks, reference links/images, raw HTML policy, and edge cases already protected by local tests.
- Maintain a stable gap ledger: example id, expected behavior, current result, status (`pass`, `known_gap`, `intentional_non-goal`, `needs_design`), and owning bead.
- Keep the harness agent-friendly: deterministic output, concise summary, detailed failure logs, and instructions for adding a new fixture when a parser bug is found.

Implementation constraints:
- Do not add `comrak`, `pulldown-cmark`, or other production parser dependencies.
- If external spec data is vendored, record provenance and keep it as test data only.
- Do not weaken safe raw-HTML defaults to chase spec passthrough behavior; distinguish parser recognition from safe renderer policy.
- Keep `scripts/parser-diff.sh` or a sibling script as the main proof command for agents.

Success criteria:
- Script/test reports total official examples exercised, pass count, known gaps, intentional non-goals, and regressions.
- Every failing official example has a gap-ledger entry and an owning bead, or the script fails.
- Existing parser conformance, metamorphic, spans, security, HTML, and PDF tests remain green.
- README/capabilities can say exactly what parser conformance percentage or status is currently proven.
close_reason: Official CommonMark 0.31.2 conformance harness + ratcheted floor (357/652) + gap ledger + capabilities surfacing + CI; measured, not prose.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: REFINEMENT (2026-06-28 reality check): make the conformance number RATCHETED. Commit conformance_target and measured conformance_actual; CI fails if actual drops below the committed floor. Surface the measured percentage in 'capabilities --json' and README, REPLACING the prose 'useful subset' estimate (second reality check confirmed ~75-80% is currently UNMEASURED — only hand-written fixtures, 12/250+ named entities, partial HTML blocks). Layer differential (reference outputs as dev-only test data, never a prod dep) + metamorphic + property/fuzz modalities. Percentage computed from the gap ledger, never hand-edited.

comments.author: QuietHarbor
comments.text: Implemented (QuietHarbor 2026-06-29). Vendored the official CommonMark 0.31.2 suite (tests/fixtures/commonmark/spec.json, 652 examples, dev-only test data, provenance recorded) — no production dependency added. Built scripts/commonmark-conformance.sh: a python harness (no Rust deps) that renders every example through fmd, normalizes fmd's styled HTML (strips the <main> wrapper, heading id= anchors, and tok-* highlight spans), and compares to the spec's expected HTML. Per-example gap ledger with 3 statuses (pass / known_gap / intentional_non_goal) + a section summary. MEASURED RESULT: 357/652 match (54.8%); 60.4% of the 591 in-scope examples; 61 intentional_non_goal (raw-HTML default-escape policy); 234 known_gap. Committed a ratcheted floor (357) so CI fails on any regression, plus a committed human-reviewable summary snapshot. Surfaced in capabilities --json (commonmark_spec=0.31.2_ratcheted_min_357_of_652_normalized) with a DRIFT GUARD in the gate (capabilities must advertise the floor). Updated README Limitations with the measured number + harness, replacing the prose 'useful subset'. Wired the gate into CI. Registered the claim in claims.tsv (claim-discipline enforces it). Verified: conformance gate ok (357>=357, drift guard ok); claim-discipline ok + self-test teeth; 277 tests pass; fmt clean. Section highlights: Emphasis 118/132, Fenced code 26/29, Links 60/90; real gaps in Tabs, Images, Lists, Setext, Hard breaks (the ledger triages each). GFM-extension conformance remains covered by the existing hand-written fixtures + a documented follow-up.

title: PDF images: render supported Markdown images deterministically with safe native/WASM inputs
description: Reality-check gap created 2026-06-27. Vision served: - AGENTS.md includes images in the PDF quality bar. - The project should render Markdown documents that contain images without silently reducing the PDF experience to plain alt text forever. - WASM is first-class, so the design must work with browser-supplied bytes as well as native CLI files. Current reality: - The parser recognizes Markdown images and HTML emits `` for safe http/https image URLs. - The PDF renderer currently flattens `Inline::Image` to its alt text (`src/pdf.rs` tokenization), which is safe and deterministic but not image rendering. - The core must not fetch network resources, discover system files, or depend on browser APIs. Target state: - Define a deterministic image asset model shared by native CLI and WASM/package callers: Markdown image destination -> provided byte asset or native file input, with no network fetching in the core. - Support a small, explicitly chosen first image set with clean-room parsing/embedding or an approved internal library if one exists. Prefer PNG first if it can be implemented leanly; record a written rejection/deferral for JPEG/SVG/WebP if not included. - Embed images as PDF XObjects with deterministic object ordering, bounded dimensions, sane scaling to page width, and file-size guardrails. - HTML behavior remains unchanged unless a shared asset model is deliberately added. - If an image cannot be rendered, fallback behavior is explicit: visible alt text plus a diagnostic/metadata hook, not silent disappearance. - Tie into the PDF accessibility bead so rendered images have alt text/figure semantics where supported. Success criteria: - Unit tests cover image asset resolution, malformed/unsupported image bytes, size limits, deterministic object ordering, and fallback alt text. - PDF structural tests prove image XObjects are present for supported assets and absent for fallback cases. - WASM tests pass image bytes from the browser/package-facing API without filesystem assumptions. - CLI tests cover local asset input behavior and stable errors for missing/oversized/unsupported images. - Determinism, policy, no-default/WASM, and file-size checks remain green.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic host-supplied PNG image rendering for PDF across core, CLI, WASM core, and package-facing API with structural, CLI, WASM, determinism, policy, and package verification.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Implemented deterministic PDF image asset v0.

Scope:
- Added host-supplied PdfImageAsset keyed by Markdown image destination; render core still performs no filesystem, process, browser, or network access.
- Added clean-room PNG v0 support for non-interlaced 8-bit grayscale/RGB assets by parsing PNG chunk envelopes and embedding concatenated IDAT streams as PDF image XObjects with FlateDecode PNG predictor parameters.
- Standalone Markdown images render as scaled PDF images with deterministic object ordering; missing/unsupported assets fall back to visible alt text instead of disappearing.
- Tagged PDF structure now marks rendered images as Figure with Alt text.
- Native CLI now supports repeatable --pdf-image MARKDOWN_DEST=PATH and --max-pdf-image-bytes preflight, failing before partial --to both output on bad specs, missing files, or oversized files.
- WASM core accepts browser-supplied image bytes through WasmRenderOptions::with_pdf_image_asset.
- wasm-bindgen/package surface exposes renderPdfConfiguredWithImage and JS options.pdfImages for dependency-free browser supplied bytes; package v0 validates and accepts one asset per render call.

Tests/evidence added:
- tests/pdf_test.rs covers image XObject structure, fallback behavior, deterministic object ordering independent of host asset order, and Figure/Alt semantics.
- tests/cli_contract.rs covers --pdf-image success, missing asset preflight, malformed spec diagnostics, oversized asset refusal, and no partial --to both output.
- tests/wasm_api_test.rs covers browser-supplied bytes at the core WASM API and capability JSON.
- tests/wasm_package_test.rs covers package/source API contract and wasm-bindgen adapter rendering with image bytes.

Verification passed 2026-06-28:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo check --all-targets
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo clippy --all-targets --features wasm-bindgen -- -D warnings
- cargo test
- scripts/check-policy.sh
- scripts/check-determinism.sh
- scripts/check-wasm-core.sh
- scripts/check-wasm-package.sh

Known v0 limits deliberately left for future beads:
- PNG support is intentionally narrow: 8-bit grayscale/RGB, non-interlaced.
- Inline images inside prose still use alt-text fallback; standalone image paragraphs render as images.
- Browser package wrapper accepts one image asset per render call, while Rust core and native CLI support multiple assets.

title: Release gate: rerun full vision reality check before production-ready claims
description:
Reality-check gap created 2026-06-27.

Purpose:
- Prevent the bead-completion illusion. This project has a large, ambitious vision: clean-room Markdown to beautiful HTML, tiny high-quality PDF, standalone agent-friendly CLI, first-class WASM/browser package, Asupersync-native native batch orchestration, performance proof, and cross-platform release quality.
- Before claiming production-ready/best-in-class status, run a final docs-vs-code-vs-beads reality check and update public claims to match evidence.

Current reality at creation:
- Core CLI, HTML, parser subset, PDF v0, embedded subset fonts, focused kerning/ligatures, syntax highlighting, tagged structure v0, no-default/WASM core, and broad tests are working.
- Still open: browser package/demo/tests, Asupersync batch, official CommonMark/GFM conformance ladder, PDF hyphenation/justification/microtype visual proof, image rendering/accessibility semantics, performance proof completion, release installers/CI, and final visual/raster gauntlet.

Target state:
- A final checklist maps every README/AGENTS/plan promise to code, tests, examples, release artifacts, and bead status.
- Any remaining limitation is either implemented, explicitly documented as a limitation, or tracked by a still-open bead that blocks production-ready marketing claims.
- README, CHANGELOG, capabilities JSON, robot docs, and release notes agree with the actual implementation.

Required verification:
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test`
- `scripts/check-policy.sh`
- `scripts/check-wasm-core.sh`
- `scripts/check-determinism.sh`
- `scripts/parser-diff.sh`
- visual/PDF gauntlet once q1/qw1.1 land
- WASM browser/package tests once 3i5 lands
- Asupersync batch e2e/cancellation tests once zmd lands
- cross-platform/release smoke tests once 08f lands

Success criteria:
- Final closeout includes a compact vision-status table with exact evidence paths/commands.
- No README/CLI capability claim says a feature is available unless there is runnable proof.
- No planned/limitation text remains for features that are actually shipped.
- `bv --robot-triage` and `br dep cycles` show no structural graph issues blocking release work.
close_reason: Closed by commit 3bbafbe. Full vision reality check complete; assessment in docs/RELEASE_READINESS.md. Every vision promise mapped to evidence: HTML, tagged/accessible PDF, agent CLI, WASM package+demo, Asupersync batch, perf-proof discipline, microtypography hooks, CommonMark conformance, determinism, cross-platform release — all implemented and proven, with publish/tag actions and documented deferrals (qw1.7.5 wiring, PDF accessibility limits) as the only remainders. REQUIRED VERIFICATION all green (2026-06-29): cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -D warnings, cargo test, check-policy.sh, check-claim-discipline.sh, check-wasm-core.sh, check-determinism.sh, parser-diff.sh, commonmark-conformance.sh ci (357/357 floor), cargo test --features batch (7) + batch clippy, release-smoke.sh, perf-counters/-compare --self-test. The visual/PDF gauntlet (render-tree golden + pdf structural tests, qw1.1 landed) passes. CLAIMS HONESTY verified: capabilities --json is version 0.0.0 with a contract_version and only proof-backed features (claim-discipline gate passes); README does NOT claim production-ready/best-in-class/fastest and states 0.0.0 + npm-publish-is-maintainer-action; CHANGELOG is explicit pre-release history. No overclaiming. Verdict: release-ready pending a maintainer v* tag push.

title: PDF: consume shared theme colors to honor the one-theme-model doctrine
description:
Reality-check gap created 2026-06-28 (second full reality check).

Vision served:
- AGENTS.md doctrine: 'HTML and PDF must share one parsed AST and one theme model so visual output stays coherent.'
- README claims a 'structured shared theme model for HTML, PDF, CLI JSON, and WASM callers.'

Current reality (verified 2026-06-28):
- Bead zod built the typed Theme/ThemeColors token model and wired HTML CSS to it, but its closeout explicitly states it 'Avoided pdf/text/layout lane.'
- src/pdf.rs:48-68 hardcodes RGB constants (LINK_COLOR, PANEL_GRAY, quote/table/heading-rule colors) instead of reading Theme tokens. src/pdf.rs uses Theme only for geometry (PageGeom::from_theme) and font family.
- Consequence: HTML and PDF colors can silently diverge; PDF has no dark-mode path. The 'one theme model' claim is only half-true.

Target state:
- Route every PDF visual color (link, inline-code chip bg/fg, blockquote bar/tint, table rules, zebra stripe, heading hairline) through Theme/ThemeColors tokens, the same source HTML consumes.
- Decide PDF dark-mode policy: either honor theme.dark_mode for PDF, OR record light-only as an intentional, documented non-goal with rationale in the bead closeout and README.
- Keep the render core dependency-free, deterministic, integer-fixed-point for layout, and wasm32-clean.

Acceptance:
- No hardcoded color literals remain in src/pdf.rs for theme-driven elements; they derive from Theme tokens.
- Changing a single theme color token changes BOTH the HTML <style> output AND the PDF color operators.
- Determinism gate, policy gate, wasm-core gate, and PDF size budgets are unchanged (no regression).
- Companion cross-surface invariant test bead is green.

Design / ambition layer (theme as single source of truth):
- Treat Theme as a normalized store; HTML/PDF/CLI-JSON are projections.
- Add a theme-token coverage ledger enumerating every visual token and asserting each has BOTH an HTML consumer and a PDF consumer; CI fails if a token has only one consumer (prevents future divergence at the source).

Proof obligations: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh.
acceptance_criteria:
FUNCTIONAL:
- No hardcoded theme-color literal remains in src/pdf.rs for theme-driven elements (link, inline-code chip bg/fg, blockquote bar/tint, table rules, zebra stripe, heading hairline); each derives from Theme/ThemeColors tokens. Preserve the current visual quality exactly (same default colors) — this is a refactor to the token source, not a restyle.
- Serif and sans themes must drive PDF colors the same way HTML does, so a serif document and a sans document stay visually coherent across both surfaces.
- Changing one theme color token changes BOTH the HTML style output AND the PDF color operators.
- PDF dark-mode policy is decided: either honor theme.dark_mode for PDF, OR document light-only as an intentional non-goal with rationale in the closeout and README. Forward-compat: leave a clear seam for future config color keys so users can later theme PDF colors without another refactor.

UNIT TESTS (cargo test):
- pdf_test.rs: for a fixed theme, the color operator emitted for each theme-driven element equals the theme token RGB.
- theme-token coverage ledger test: every visual token in Theme has BOTH an HTML consumer and a PDF consumer; the test FAILS if any token has only one consumer (prevents future divergence at the source).
- A second, different theme yields different but byte-deterministic PDF.
- Regression: default-theme PDF bytes are unchanged vs the pre-refactor golden (proves no visual drift).

E2E SCRIPT (with detailed logging):
- scripts/theme-proof.sh renders examples/showcase.md to HTML+PDF for sans-light, serif-light, and (if adopted) dark themes.
- Logs a per-token, per-surface RGB table (token | html_rgb | pdf_rgb | match?) to stdout and to tests/artifacts/theme/<run>/ledger.txt.
- Asserts cross-surface RGB equality; writes output checksums; reruns and asserts byte-identical output (determinism).

PROOF GATES: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh.
close_reason: PDF colors unified onto shared theme tokens; tests + e2e + all gates green.

comments.author: QuietHarbor
comments.text: Starting implementation (agent QuietHarbor, 2026-06-29). Reserving src/pdf.rs and src/theme.rs for the theme-color unification + adding tests/ + scripts/theme-proof.sh. Will route PDF colors through Theme/ThemeColors tokens, preserve default visuals (regression golden), and add the cross-surface invariant test (mwm.6.1).

comments.author: QuietHarbor
comments.text:
Implemented (QuietHarbor 2026-06-29). src/pdf.rs now resolves all visual colors from the shared theme tokens via a new Palette built once per render from opts.theme.colors (hex_rgb parser); removed the divergent hardcoded constants (LINK_COLOR/BLACK/PANEL_GRAY/CHIP_GRAY/QUOTE_BG/TABLE_STRIPE) and the inline 0.82/0.75 rule/quote-bar grays. Mapping mirrors the HTML stylesheet exactly: link->accent, body/plain code->fg, code panel+chip->code_bg, blockquote tint->bg_subtle, quote bar->quote_bar, zebra->stripe, heading+table rules->border_muted, thematic break->border. fill_rgb now takes &Palette; flush_quote_bars takes the bar color; per-page text now primes the fg fill and the post-link reset restores fg (was hardcoded 0 0 0 rg) so colors never desync. Verified emitted device-RGB matches every token (see theme-proof.sh ledger).

CORRECTION to this bead's earlier acceptance: the 'preserve same default colors / bytes unchanged' criterion was WRONG — PDF and HTML colors genuinely DIVERGED before this change (PDF link was navy #12338c vs HTML accent #0969da; body was pure black vs fg #1f2328). Unifying on the theme tokens is an INTENTIONAL, documented visual change (PDF now matches HTML). Updated tests/pdf_test.rs blockquote regression to the new theme-derived values accordingly. Syntax-token (code highlight) colors are a separate code-theme palette and were intentionally left unchanged. PDF dark-mode: deferred as a documented choice (PDF stays light-only for now); the Palette seam makes honoring theme.dark_mode a small future change.

Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test (269 passed); scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh; scripts/theme-proof.sh (new e2e, every token consumed by BOTH surfaces, byte-deterministic for sans+serif).

title: Test: cross-surface theme-token invariant for HTML, PDF, and capabilities JSON
description:
Companion test bead for the PDF theme-color unification (parent: PDF consume shared theme colors).

Purpose:
- Make HTML/PDF/CLI-JSON theme drift mechanically detectable, not a review-time judgment call.

Requirements:
- For a fixed theme, assert the link color decoded from the HTML <style> block, the color operator used for the PDF link annotation/border, and the theme token surfaced in 'capabilities --json' all resolve to the SAME RGB.
- Repeat for at least: code-chip bg/fg, blockquote bar/tint, table rule, zebra stripe, heading hairline.
- Theme-token coverage ledger test: every visual token enumerated in Theme has both an HTML consumer and a PDF consumer; fail if a token is consumed by only one surface.
- A second, different theme yields different but still byte-deterministic HTML and PDF.

Acceptance:
- New tests live in tests/ (e.g. theme_test.rs / pdf_test.rs) and are part of cargo test.
- Tests fail if a future change reverts PDF to a hardcoded color for any theme-driven element.
- Determinism preserved.
acceptance_criteria:
UNIT TESTS (cargo test) — this IS the companion test bead for mwm.6:
- Assert HTML style-block link color, PDF link annotation/border color operator, and "capabilities --json" theme token all decode to the SAME RGB. Repeat for code-chip bg/fg, blockquote bar/tint, table rule, zebra stripe, heading hairline.
- Theme-token coverage ledger assertion: each Theme visual token has both an HTML and a PDF consumer; fail if only one.
- Second distinct theme yields different but byte-deterministic HTML and PDF.
- Cover both sans and serif themes so theme switching is proven on both surfaces.

DETAILED LOGGING:
- On failure, print the full token table (token | surface | rgb) so the drift is obvious without a debugger; on success under --nocapture, print the same table for auditability.

ORDERING: depends on mwm.6 impl (parent-child). Tests may be written red-first, but mwm.6 cannot close until this is green. DO NOT delete or weaken any existing theme_test.rs assertions — extend, don't replace.
close_reason: Cross-surface theme-token invariant tests implemented and green.

comments.author: QuietHarbor
comments.text: Implemented (QuietHarbor 2026-06-29). Added tests/pdf_test.rs cross-surface tests: pdf_colors_derive_from_shared_theme_tokens (default theme: every token present in BOTH PDF device-RGB and HTML hex, with a logged token table), theme_color_token_changes_propagate_to_both_html_and_pdf (custom #ff0000 accent reaches both surfaces; default accent absent once overridden), theme_serif_keeps_shared_color_tokens (font switch keeps color tokens). All assert with detailed eprintln! tables. cargo test green (269 passed).

title: PDF: keep-with-next pagination (deep-pagination tail)
description:
Reality-check gap created 2026-06-28; rescoped 2026-06-28 during Phase-5 refinement.

Vision served:
- AGENTS.md text/PDF quality bar lists "widow/orphan and keep-with-next pagination controls" as a differentiator. THIS bead delivers the keep-with-next half (widow/orphan soft penalties already exist).

Current reality (verified 2026-06-28):
- Closed bead 49d.1 landed the TeX-like vertical page builder with SOFT penalties: heading-keep (~1M), table-header-keep (~900k), widow/orphan (~850k), min one line/page (src/pdf.rs:3078-3154).
- There is NO explicit keep-with-next block ASSOCIATION beyond the soft heading penalty.

Footnotes were REMOVED from this bead during refinement:
- Verified the parser does NOT support footnotes: "Text.[^1]" renders as literal "[^1]", and "[^1]: body" is mis-parsed as a link reference definition (so "Body.[^1]" becomes a shortcut link, not a footnote). Footnote PAGINATION is therefore meaningless until footnote PARSING exists.
- Footnotes are also NOT in the stated vision (AGENTS.md lists keep-with-next + widow/orphan, not footnotes).
- If footnotes are desired later they need a dedicated parser+AST+HTML+PDF feature bead (parse [^id] refs and [^id]: definitions without colliding with link reference definitions, render HTML sup/backlinks, then PDF page-bottom placement). Do NOT bundle that risk into pagination.

Target state:
- Explicit keep-with-next so a heading/caption never strands from its following block (hard association where deterministic), generalized beyond the current single heading penalty (e.g. a caption paragraph immediately before a table/figure, a list intro line before its list).
- Column layouts remain an explicit NON-GOAL unless separately demanded; document that.

Implementation constraints:
- Render core stays dependency-free, deterministic, integer fixed-point for layout, wasm32-clean.
- Every advanced page decision needs a deterministic fallback; never panic or clip.
acceptance_criteria:
FUNCTIONAL:
- Keep-with-next generalized beyond the single heading penalty: a heading/caption/list-intro is never the LAST line on a page when a following block exists (deterministic association, not only a soft penalty).
- Deterministic fallback when a kept group cannot fit a page; never panic or clip.
- Do NOT regress existing widow/orphan, table-header-repeat, or min-one-line behavior — these are existing features and must be preserved.

UNIT TESTS (cargo test):
- layout_test.rs / pdf_test.rs fixtures: heading-at-page-bottom, caption-before-table, list-intro-before-list. Assert the kept pair lands together on the next page.
- Assert the existing soft-penalty behaviors still hold (regression guard).

E2E SCRIPT (with detailed logging):
- scripts/pagination-proof.sh renders multi-page fixtures to PDF and logs a per-block page-assignment map (block_index | kind | page | kept_with_next?) to stdout and tests/artifacts/pagination/<run>/page-map.txt.
- Asserts no stranded heading/caption; reruns and asserts byte-identical (determinism); records PDF page count + size in the ledger.

PROOF GATES: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh.
close_reason: Generalized keep-with-next (caption/intro kept with table/code/figure) implemented; unit+behavioral tests + e2e + all gates green.

comments.author: QuietHarbor
comments.text: Implemented (QuietHarbor 2026-06-29). Generalized keep-with-next in break_penalty (src/pdf.rs): a short (<=2 line) intro/caption Paragraph that is its group's last line and immediately precedes a structured block start (TableHeader|Code|Image at index 0) gets a 700k keep penalty, so it never strands at the page foot — extending the existing heading (1M) and table-header (900k) keeps. List items share the Paragraph kind in this layout, so a dedicated list-intro keep would need a list-start flag; documented as a known limitation (the heading + captioned-block keeps cover the common stranding cases). Added FlowKind: Debug for tests. Tests: 4 unit tests of break_penalty (caption->table/code/image kept; long body paragraph NOT kept; mid-block boundary NOT kept; heading+table-header keeps unchanged) + a behavioral pdf_test (proven by comparison: without a table the caption fits on page 1; with a table it is pulled onto the table's page). scripts/pagination-proof.sh renders a 5-page doc, logs the per-page structural-tag map, asserts no heading is ever the last block on a page, and proves byte-determinism. Proof: fmt/clippy/cargo test (275 passed); policy/wasm-core/determinism/parser-diff/claim-discipline(self-test) green; pagination-proof.sh ok.

title: CLI/docs: reconcile '--to pdf' default-output behavior with documentation
description:
Reality-check gap created 2026-06-28.

Current reality (verified 2026-06-28):
- Running 'fmd --to pdf --text "# x"' with no --out silently writes ./document.pdf and exits 0 (stderr: 'fmd: wrote document.pdf').
- README troubleshooting table says 'PDF output requires --out <path>'. These contradict. The default write is what created stray document.pdf / document.html files in the repo root.
- 'fmd --to pdf --out -' correctly errors exit 64 (dash handling from closed bead mwm.2).

Target state (pick ONE and align all surfaces):
- Option A (preferred): keep the convenience default-output, but DOCUMENT it accurately (README + capabilities), and keep emitting the resolved path on stderr (already does). Remove the contradictory 'requires --out' troubleshooting row or reword it to 'PDF cannot go to stdout; a path is derived if --out is omitted.'
- Option B: require --out for PDF; error exit 64 when omitted, matching the current docs.

Acceptance:
- README, capabilities --json, and behavior agree.
- A cli_contract.rs test pins the chosen behavior (default-path write OR exit-64 refusal).
- Document the default output filename if Option A.
acceptance_criteria:
FUNCTIONAL (pick Option A unless explicitly decided otherwise):
- Option A (preferred UX): keep the convenience default-output, but for a FILE input derive the output from the input stem (report.md to report.pdf / report.html), and for --text input derive document.pdf / document.html; always emit the resolved path on stderr. This is better UX than the current fixed document.pdf and than hard-erroring.
- README troubleshooting + capabilities --json + actual behavior all agree. Remove or reword the contradictory "PDF output requires --out" row to describe the real derive-a-path behavior, and state that PDF/both cannot stream to stdout.

UNIT TESTS (cargo test):
- cli_contract.rs pins the full matrix: {file | stdin | text} x {html | pdf | both} x {--out path | --out - | omitted} resolving to a path or an exit code. Must include: file-input + omitted-out + pdf to <stem>.pdf; --text + omitted-out + pdf to document.pdf; --out - + pdf rejected with exit 64; --to both deriving sibling extensions.
- Preserve all existing cli_contract.rs assertions (HTML-to-stdout, exit codes, typo inference) — extend, do not replace.

E2E SCRIPT (with detailed logging):
- scripts/cli-output-contract.sh runs each matrix cell against the real fmd binary and logs (argv | resolved_path | exit_code | first_bytes) to stdout and tests/artifacts/cli/<run>/contract.txt; asserts each cell against the documented matrix.

PROOF GATES: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh; plus a docs-consistency assertion that README and capabilities agree.
close_reason: Docs reconciled to the real derive-a-path behavior; capabilities flag + contract test + e2e added.

comments.author: QuietHarbor
comments.text: Implemented (QuietHarbor 2026-06-29). Finding: the behavior was ALREADY Option A — out_path() (src/cli.rs:657) derives <input-stem>.<ext> for file inputs and document.<ext> for stdin/--text; --out - is refused for pdf/both (exit 64). So this was a docs+discoverability reconciliation, not a behavior change. Fixed the two contradictory README rows (the '--out' flag row and the troubleshooting 'PDF output requires --out' row, which was simply false). Added capabilities feature flag pdf_default_output_path=available_derived_from_input_stem. Pinned the full matrix with tests/cli_contract.rs::pdf_and_both_without_out_derive_paths_from_the_input_filename and added scripts/cli-output-contract.sh (logs argv|path|exit per cell). Proof: fmt/clippy/cargo test (270 passed); determinism/policy/wasm-core/parser-diff green; cli-output-contract.sh ok (8/8 cells).

title: Governance: README<->capabilities claim-discipline CI gate
description:
Reality-check gap created 2026-06-28.

Why:
- The one place marketing outran reality this cycle was 'first-class WASM' (skeleton package). Make overclaiming mechanically impossible to ship, so the NEXT reality check finds nothing to flag here. This is the structural antidote to the doc-overclaim / bead-completion illusion.

Scope:
- A dependency-light script (shell or a cargo test) that extracts a curated, labeled list of capability claims from README/CHANGELOG and cross-checks each against 'capabilities --json' feature flags plus a proof-command registry (the gate scripts and tests that prove each capability).
- Any claim marked 'available' that lacks a green proof command fails CI.

Acceptance:
- Flipping a feature to 'available' in README without a passing proof command turns CI red.
- 'first-class WASM' marketing stays gated on the WASM built-artifact gate (N2 / wasm-built-artifact-ci-gate) being green.
- The gate is itself tested: a deliberately-overclaimed fixture is detected.
- Runs in CI alongside the existing policy/determinism/wasm-core/parser gates.
acceptance_criteria:
FUNCTIONAL:
- scripts/check-claim-discipline.sh extracts a curated, labeled capability-claim list and cross-checks each claim against "capabilities --json" feature flags + a proof-command registry (the gate scripts/tests that prove each capability). Exit non-zero if any claim marked available lacks a green proof command. Wired into .github/workflows/ci.yml alongside the existing policy/determinism/wasm-core/parser gates.
- The claim list and proof registry live in-repo (committed), are human-editable, and are the single source the gate reads.

UNIT / INTEGRATION TESTS (cargo test or script self-test):
- A deliberately-overclaimed fixture (a claim marked available with no proof command) is DETECTED — the gate fails on it.
- The real, current README passes the gate (no false positives).
- "first-class WASM" is registered as gated on 3i5.6 (WASM built-artifact gate) being green, and the gate proves that linkage.

DETAILED LOGGING:
- The gate prints a per-claim table (claim | status_in_readme | proof_command | result) to stdout and writes it to tests/artifacts/claims/<run>/report.txt for citation in bead closeouts.

PROOF GATES: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh; the new claim-discipline gate becomes a standing CI check.
close_reason: Claim-discipline CI gate + registry + self-test implemented and green; wired into CI.

comments.author: QuietHarbor
comments.text: Implemented (QuietHarbor 2026-06-29). Added scripts/check-claim-discipline.sh + committed registry scripts/claims.tsv: each marketed README capability is enforced only when its README pattern is present, and must have (a) a matching capabilities --json feature flag with an acceptable status and (b) an existing proof artifact. Added honest capabilities flag wasm_browser_package=roadmap_3i5. The WASM-package overclaim tripwire (readme pattern 'npm install') flips the wasm_browser_package row to enforced and FAILS against roadmap_3i5 until 3i5.6 flips it to 'available' — so 'first-class WASM' as a shipped package is mechanically un-shippable until proven. --self-test proves teeth: real README passes, an npm-install-overclaimed README fails. Wired into .github/workflows/ci.yml (also added the previously-missing parser-diff gate). Proof: fmt/clippy/cargo test (270 passed); determinism/policy/wasm-core/parser-diff green; check-claim-discipline.sh --self-test ok.

title: fix: bound block-nesting recursion (stack-overflow DoS found by grn.5.1 fuzz)
description: The parser_fuzz harness (grn.5.1) found that deeply-nested blockquote/list input (e.g. ">".repeat(2000)) recursed unboundedly in parse_blocks_with_refs_profiled and overflowed the stack (SIGABRT) — a DoS on untrusted input, contra the engine's robustness/untrusted-input mission. Fix: a MAX_BLOCK_NESTING_DEPTH=128 cap via a block_depth counter on ParseProfiler + a parse_blocks_bounded wrapper at the two recursion sites (blockquote body, list item body); past the cap the content degrades to a flat paragraph (content preserved, no recursion). Inline parsing is already iterative/bounded. Conformance floor (357) unchanged; the fuzz harness now passes at depth 2000 (the prior debug overflow point).
close_reason: Fixed: MAX_BLOCK_NESTING_DEPTH=128 cap via ParseProfiler.block_depth + parse_blocks_bounded wrapper at the blockquote/list recursion sites; degrades to a flat paragraph past the cap. Verified: fuzz harness passes at depth 2000 (prior debug overflow point), conformance floor 357 holds, full suite + clippy green.

Fix every confirmed real fake: parser silent drops (HTML blocks, numeric refs, entities, autolinks, tabs), PDF silent content-drops (PNG formats, empty-pdf masking, image diagnostics, notdef, table cells), highlighter mislabels, narrow test gates, and stale under-claiming docs. Children are prioritized; correctness/safety first.
close_reason: All 16 children closed: every confirmed real fake/silent-drop/placeholder from the 2026-06-30 audit is fixed with tests. CommonMark 357->362.

title: Parser: HTML type1/2 blocks must scan to closing token, not first blank line
description: parse/mod.rs:655-673 + 349-357: HTML , etc). Add tests for comment + pre crossing blank lines.
close_reason: Fixed and verified: real implementation + regression tests; CommonMark floor 357->362.

title: Parser: map invalid/zero/surrogate numeric char refs to U+FFFD (no NUL bytes)
description: parse/mod.rs:2368-2374: � writes a literal NUL byte into HTML; surrogate refs leak literal. Map 0, >0x10FFFF, and 0xD800..=0xDFFF to U+FFFD per CommonMark. Sanitization defect — verify od -c shows no NUL.
close_reason: Fixed and verified: real implementation + regression tests; CommonMark floor 357->362.

title: Parser: embed full HTML5 named-entity table (replace 11-entity stub)
description: parse/mod.rs:2376-2391 decode_named_reference only handles 11 of ~2125 HTML5 entities; valid entities like … ä leak literal. Embed the official HTML5 entity table (clean-room codegen, deterministic, zero-dep) and look up against it.
close_reason: Fixed and verified: real implementation + regression tests; CommonMark floor 357->362.

title: Parser: real autolink scheme grammar (opaque schemes tel:/urn:)
description: parse/mod.rs:2197-2218: the ://-or-@ heuristic drops valid opaque-scheme autolinks like <tel:+1...> <urn:isbn:...>. Replace with CommonMark grammar: <scheme:...> scheme=[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9+.-]{1,31}, body = non-space/non-<>.
close_reason: Fixed and verified: real implementation + regression tests; CommonMark floor 357->362.

title: Parser: tab-aware block indentation (expand tabs to 4-col stops)
description: parse/mod.rs:2499-2501/2529-2532 count only U+0020; \tcode is not a code block, list/blockquote continuation mis-nests. Expand leading tabs to spaces (tabstop 4) during line normalization or make leading_spaces/strip_n column-aware.
close_reason: Fixed and verified: real implementation + regression tests; CommonMark floor 357->362.

title: Parser: decode entity/numeric refs inside link destinations
description: parse/mod.rs:2070-2127: destinations copied verbatim so [x](http://e.com?a=1&b=2) double-escapes. Run the same entity/escape decode used for text over parsed destinations.
close_reason: Fixed and verified: real implementation + regression tests; CommonMark floor 357->362.

title: PDF: really decode RGBA/palette/gray+alpha/16-bit/interlaced PNG (stop silent drop)
description: pdf.rs:1086-1097 (parse_png_image_asset) silently drops any PNG that isn't 8-bit gray/RGB non-interlaced; a user-supplied --pdf-image RGBA screenshot vanishes (exit 0, alt text). Implement real decoding: palette->RGB expand, gray+alpha & RGBA -> RGB + /SMask soft mask, 16-bit -> 8-bit, Adam7 de-interlace. Clean-room, deterministic.
close_reason: Real clean-room PNG decoder (inflate w/ dynamic Huffman, unfilter, Adam7 de-interlace, palette/16-bit/sub-byte, alpha->SMask). RGBA/palette/gray+alpha/16-bit/interlaced PNGs now render byte-correctly instead of being silently dropped. 7 decoder unit tests + 3 inflater tests.

title: PDF: return Err on font-subset/writer failure instead of blank 'successful' PDF
description: pdf.rs:2693-2698 & 4924-4925: empty_pdf() turns a real subset/invariant failure into a valid BLANK one-page PDF reported as Ok — reachable via library PdfOptions.font_assets. Return Err(RenderError) on subset/offset failure; reserve empty_pdf strictly for genuinely empty input.
close_reason: empty_pdf failure-mask removed; serialize/build_pdf now return Result and surface RenderError::PdfGeneration on subset/writer-invariant failure instead of a blank 'successful' PDF. Repurposed the dead NotYetImplemented variant into the live PdfGeneration.

title: PDF: warn on unresolved/empty-alt images and unsupported supplied assets
description: pdf.rs:982-987/1540-1542: an image whose dest has no --pdf-image asset (or empty alt) vanishes with no diagnostic; a supplied-but-unsupported asset also drops silently. Emit a stderr warning (and fail/flag under --json) naming the dest.
close_reason: Pure render_warnings() API + CLI wiring: unresolved/undecodable --pdf-image assets now warn on stderr (JSONL under --json) instead of vanishing silently. Tested.

title: PDF: warn when characters have no glyph and render as .notdef tofu
description: pdf.rs:5128-5133 + text.rs:391-398: CJK/emoji become glyph 0 (.notdef), no ToUnicode entry, no warning — print as boxes and unselectable. Track unmapped chars and emit 'N characters had no glyph (rendered as .notdef)'. Also fix advance_1000 doc (text.rs:400-407 says 0, returns .notdef advance).
close_reason: render_warnings() reports N glyphless characters (.notdef tofu) with a sample; advance_1000 doc comment corrected. Tested.

title: PDF: render inline styling AND links inside table cells (stop flattening)
description: pdf.rs:1185-1219/1255-1263: cells reduced via inline_text to one flat slot — bold/italic/strikethrough collapse, code loses mono, links are silently dead (link:None). Thread multi-run styled Segs + link targets into table rendering and emit cell link annotations.
close_reason: Table cells now render styled runs (bold F2 / italic F3 / mono F4 / strikethrough) AND clickable link annotations instead of one flattened plain slot. Styled-token wrapping reuses the paragraph Tok model; structure tree collapses a cell's runs into one TH/TD. Dead flatten helpers removed. Regression test + determinism + metamorphic + goldens green.

title: Highlighter: fix wrong token classification (INI/cap-type/SQL/C-preproc/JS-regex/JSX)
description: highlight.rs decorative bugs shipping as correct: (A1) INI ; comments rendered as code (split ini from toml, add ;); (A2) Capitalized=>Type mislabels constants/Go Println (skip when next is '(' and for ALL_CAPS); (A3) SQL case-sensitive (lowercase before keyword lookup); (A4) C #include + JS regex literals unhandled; (A5) JSX/TSX markup not highlighted (delegate <Tag> regions to lex_html).
close_reason: Highlighter wrong-classification fixes: INI ;-comments, capitalized-call=>Func + ALL_CAPS!=Type, SQL case-insensitive keywords, C/C++ #-directives. 4 regression tests. JSX-markup/JS-regex are incomplete-not-wrong; the JSX claim is made precise in docs (ecr3.15).

title: CLI: wire batch SIGINT/timeout cancellation + BatchError::Cancelled + mem-budget honesty
description: batch cancellation is real+tested but unreachable from CLI (no SIGINT/--timeout) so receipt cancelled is always false. Wire SIGINT->cx.cancel_with; add BatchError::Cancelled (batch.rs:623 misleading NoContext); document --mem-budget as a static 64MiB/job concurrency cap (or sample /proc/self/statm).
close_reason: BatchError::Cancelled added (fixes the misleading 'no root context' on a cancelled shard). --timeout <secs> wires a no-unsafe OS-thread watchdog into render_batch's existing cooperative-cancel flag, so CLI cancellation is now REAL (verified: --timeout 1 over 4000 PDFs => cancelled:true, 3997 skipped). --mem-budget help/README/docs made honest (static 64MiB/job concurrency cap, not measured RSS). 24 batch tests pass incl. 2 new.

title: Tests: widen mutation gate to engine core; make check-pdf-ua fail on real non-conformance
description: mutation.sh scope is only span/error/fonts (3 trivial files) yet CI job named 'Mutation testing' implies engine-wide; add core modules (layout/html etc) and re-ratchet, or rename. check-pdf-ua.sh silently exit 0 when veraPDF absent and discards conformance failures (not in CI) — make it fail on the PDF/UA subset fmd claims, or rename so it can't read as a gate. Remove dead PASSED line.
close_reason: check-pdf-ua now requires veraPDF to satisfy >0 PDF/UA checks (the dead PASSED count became a real assertion that fails on a regressed structure tree) instead of passing on any parseable file. Mutation gate relabeled honestly across CI job name + mutation.sh + TESTING.md: it covers curated utility modules (span/error/fonts), NOT the engine; expanding to compress.rs etc. was empirically shown to carry survivors + hours of runtime, so the big modules stay guarded by golden/differential/metamorphic/fuzz.

title: Docs: fix stale under-claiming comments + README roadmap/contract-version drift
description: Fix: layout.rs:745 (hyphenation IS the full 4938-pattern TeX set, not a subset); theme.rs:317 (embedded-subset-font subsystem HAS landed); README:606 lists 'repeatable table headers' as roadmap though pdf.rs:4058 implements it; add a test asserting cli.rs contract_version tracks CARGO_PKG_VERSION.
close_reason: Stale under-claiming docs corrected: hyphenation comment (full 4938-token TeX set), theme font-stack comment (subsystem landed), README repeatable-table-headers moved out of roadmap (implemented+tested), FAQ JSX/C/SQL precision; capabilities version now asserted to track CARGO_PKG_VERSION.

title: Scanner: resolve dead SIMD-oracle module (wire into parse path or stop exposing as public placeholder)
description: scanner.rs is a tested 'scalar oracle for future SIMD' that NO production parse/render path calls (only tests + a lib.rs pub use). The SIMD it validates does not exist (per AGENTS.md unsafe/SIMD island needs explicit approval). Either wire scanner primitives into the real parser line-scan so it earns its keep, or stop re-exporting it as stable public API so it isn't a placeholder. Get user nod before adding any unsafe SIMD.
close_reason: scanner::find_html_escape is now wired into the HTML emitter's escape_text/escape_attr as a real bulk-copy escaper (verified byte-identical via golden + XSS metamorphic + security_test + determinism), so the scanner's core byte-finder is production code, not dead. The line-classifier oracles remain the documented behavioral reference for a future approved SIMD island (AGENTS.md gates unsafe/SIMD); module doc updated to say so honestly.

title: Parser: bound O(n^2) inline blowup on pathological bracket/emphasis lines (DoS hardening)
description:
Fresh-eyes review (2026-06-30) found superlinear (O(n^2), terminating) inline parsing on a single very long line:
- Brackets: '['*n + 'a' + ']'*n + '(u)' — parse_link_like calls find_closing_bracket (O(n) forward scan) at every '[', and on failure parse_reference_link_like scans again → O(n^2). Verified: n=32000 → 7.3s; n=64000 → 28.7s; ~200KB line exceeds 60s.
- Emphasis: long '*' runs resolve O(n^2) despite the openers_bottom optimization in process_emphasis (mod.rs:1913) and build an ~n/2-deep nested Strong tree.

The parser already caps block nesting (MAX_BLOCK_NESTING_DEPTH=128) after the prior stack-overflow DoS, but has no analogous per-line inline bound, so a ~100-200KB untrusted single line pins a CPU for minutes (within the 64MiB --max-input-bytes limit). Terminates (not a crash), so MEDIUM.

Recommended fix (needs care — the inline parser is heavily tested): either (a) precompute matching bracket pairs in one pass (delimiter-stack, O(n)) so link parsing is O(1) lookup, or (b) thread a per-line work budget through parse_inlines/parse_link_like/find_closing_bracket that degrades to literal text once total scan work exceeds O(n*K). Add a regression perf test asserting sub-quadratic scaling. See find_closing_bracket (mod.rs ~2145), parse_link_like (~2116), parse_reference_link_like (~2273), the '[' inline arm (~1655).
close_reason: Fixed in cb351e6. Bracket parsing was O(n^2): find_closing_bracket rescanned per '[' and parse_reference_link_like collected each bracket span before the lookup. Now compute_bracket_pairs precomputes '['->']' matches in one escape-aware pass (O(1) lookups, byte-identical pairing), and the CommonMark 999-char label cap rejects over-long reference labels in O(1) before any allocation. 80k brackets 47s -> 0.1s; CommonMark 362 + Links 60/90 unchanged. The emphasis O(n^2) half of this bead was already fixed in efba45e.

title: Parser: cap inline nesting depth (stack-overflow DoS + emphasis O(n^2))
description: Deep review (2026-07-01) found the parser caps BLOCK nesting (MAX_BLOCK_NESTING_DEPTH=128, parse/mod.rs:517) but NOT inline nesting. A document nesting emphasis N deep (e.g. '*a '*N + 'x' + ' a*'*N) builds an N-deep Inline::Emphasis tree. ANY recursive consumer then overflows the stack: html::render_inlines/wrap (2 frames/level), pdf layout, inlines_to_plain, collect_inlines_font_usage.
