Verified: N=2000 aborts on a 512KB stack (SIGABRT under panic=abort); ~depth 4000 on a 1MB WASM stack (~24KB input), ~8000 on 2MB batch worker threads. Remote-triggerable DoS on WASM + batch surfaces. SAME root cause also amplifies the emphasis O(n^2): emit_inline_el (parse/mod.rs:1844) does out.push(inl.clone()), re-cloning the growing subtree per wrap level -> O(n^2) time/mem (n=4000 -> 2.5GB). Fix: cap inline tree depth in the parser (mirror the block cap), flattening deeper delimiters to literal text -> bounds render recursion AND the clone blowup. Verify golden + add a deep-nesting no-crash test. (Bracket O(n^2) is separate: see the inline-quadratic-dos bead.)
close_reason: Fixed in efba45e. Added MAX_INLINE_NESTING_DEPTH=1000 (mirrors block cap) so deep emphasis nesting flattens to literal text instead of overflowing the stack at render/drop. Replaced emit_inline_el clone with a move (emit_inline_el_owned) killing the O(n^2) clone blowup on single delimiter runs. Also added a linear back-walk budget for the alternating both-open-and-close O(n^2). Regression test fuzz_pathological_emphasis_runs_stay_linear_and_bounded covers all three; CommonMark floor 362 + Emphasis 118/132 unchanged.

title: Layout: admit overfull lines into Knuth-Plass instead of dropping to greedy for the whole paragraph
description: layout.rs:1447-1449 treats an overfull line (badness>=INF_PENALTY) as an ILLEGAL edge (continue). If any required sub-segment has no feasible break (e.g. a single word/URL wider than the line), states[last] stays None and the code discards the whole DP and re-runs greedy first-fit over the ENTIRE paragraph (1481-1487). So one too-wide token (common in technical Markdown) silently disables optimal breaking for the whole paragraph. Fix: cap badness at INF_PENALTY and add a large FINITE overfull demerit so overfull lines stay selectable, keeping greedy only as a true last resort. NOTE: changes PDF output for paragraphs containing an unbreakable-too-wide token -> regenerate render-tree/golden and review. Also layout.rs:1474 keeps only one BreakState per candidate (fitness classes collapsed) -> optionally key states by (candidate, fitness_class) for true KP optimality.
close_reason: Fixed in 1c5ad03. Inter-candidate overfull lines are now selectable at a large finite demerit (line_badness caps at INF_PENALTY -> line_demerits ~1e8) instead of illegal, so a too-wide token is isolated on one overfull line while the rest of the paragraph keeps optimal breaking; greedy is now a true last resort. Whole-prefix (start=0) overfull deliberately excluded to avoid cramming the whole paragraph onto one line. Feasible paragraphs byte-identical (goldens unchanged). The OPTIONAL secondary refinement (key BreakState by (candidate,fitness_class)) is deferred as a separate enhancement (changes normal-paragraph output; needs golden regen; not required for the greedy-fallback bug).

title: text: subsetter should degrade gracefully on a bad component/cmap glyph, not fail the whole font
description: text.rs:749 (subset_glyph_bytes new_of.get(comp_old)?) and :785 (build_cmap4 new_of.get(old)?) fail the ENTIRE subset (-> RenderError::PdfGeneration 'font could not be subset') if a needed composite references a component gid >= num_glyphs, or a cmap char maps to a glyph not in the subset set. One malformed glyph denies rendering of an otherwise-usable document. Fail-closed (error, not corruption) so LOW/robustness. Fix: drop just the bad composite (emit .notdef-substituted) and skip un-subsettable cmap codes rather than aborting.
close_reason: Fixed in c90e456. subset_glyph_bytes substitutes .notdef (new gid 0) for an out-of-subset composite component instead of aborting; build_cmap4 skips a cmap char whose glyph is not in the subset instead of aborting. Malformed/untrusted fonts now render best-effort rather than failing the whole document. Byte-identical for valid fonts (goldens unchanged); new unit tests cover both graceful-degradation paths.

title: batch: don't abort the whole run when one input path is bad; honor continue-on-error in expand_inputs
description: batch.rs:315 (metadata(p)?) and collect_dir (read_dir(dir)? + recursive ?) ?-propagate the first I/O error (missing top-level path, unreadable subdir) as a whole-run failure (exit 66), rendering NOTHING even with valid files present and --continue-on-error set. Verified: 'fmd batch corpus/ does_not_exist.md --continue-on-error' -> input_error, renders none of corpus/'s 24 files. BatchOptions.continue_on_error is not even read in batch.rs. Fix: collect per-path expansion errors as per-file Failed entries (or skip-and-warn) instead of ?-propagating, at least under continue_on_error.
close_reason: Fixed in 011f7fd. expand_inputs is now total (returns ExpandedInputs{inputs,errors} instead of ?-propagating the first metadata/read_dir I/O error). CLI honors continue_on_error: strict mode still exits 66 on the first bad path; --continue-on-error appends unexpandable paths as per-file Failed entries and renders the valid files. Verified e2e: corpus + missing path + --continue-on-error -> inputs:3 ok:2 failed:1, valid files written.

title: batch: reject --workers 0 (currently silently means auto) + document best-effort --timeout
description: NIT batch findings: (1) --workers 0 collapses with unset (unwrap_or(0) -> auto/24 workers); prefer a usage error. (2) --timeout is best-effort at file boundaries (the render core never checkpoints), so a single large file ignores the deadline until it completes; document this. (3) run_batch_blocking:664 '_ => Err(Cancelled)' is unreachable given the current Outcome contract (render_batch folds cancellation into Ok) — harmless but not the safety net it looks like.
close_reason: Fixed in 35716dc. (1) --workers 0 now rejected with usage error (exit 64) instead of silently meaning auto. (2) --timeout documented as best-effort/file-boundary on both the CLI flag and BatchOptions. (3) run_batch_blocking Outcome match is now explicit (Ok/Err/Cancelled/Panicked); added BatchError::Panicked so a panic is no longer mislabeled a timeout. Verified e2e.

title: wasm/pdf: add a decoded-BYTES image cap for the browser package (24MP RGBA16 => ~380MB transient)
description: pdf.rs MAX_PDF_IMAGE_PIXELS=24_000_000 caps PIXELS but a 24MP 16-bit RGBA PNG whose IDAT is a few KB (zlib run of zeros) drives ~380MB transient allocation (RGBA8 96MB + zlib 192MB + samples 72MB + alpha 24MB) from a <=32MB compressed input, reachable via renderPdfConfiguredWithImage. Bounds are correct (no overflow, no OOM on this host) but on a memory-constrained browser/worker wasm instance this can hit a memory trap. Fix: cap decoded BYTES (npx*channels*depth) not just pixels, and/or a smaller default pixel cap for the browser package.
close_reason: Fixed in fb62de6. Added bit-depth-aware MAX_PDF_IMAGE_DECODED_BYTES=96MiB checked in IHDR validation (pixels*channels*bytes/sample), refusing the 24MP RGBA16 ~380MB-transient case that the pixel-only cap missed, while keeping 8-bit RGBA up to the pixel cap. Uniform on native+wasm (determinism preserved). Over-cap images degrade to alt text. Golden unchanged; new test covers RGBA16 reject / RGBA8 accept / small-RGBA16 accept.

title: Deep wide-net code review (2026-07-01): remediation
description: Remediation epic for the 6-agent deep wide-net review of the whole franken_markdown codebase (parser, layout, pdf, text/font, html/highlight/theme, cli/config/batch, wasm/lib/error). Core typography (layout/text/pdf/compress) verified well-hardened with no high/med correctness bugs. Real findings cluster in: parser silent-drops + DoS, highlighter DoS, CLI output-path data-loss + contract gaps, and WASM parity/silent-drop-signal gaps. Children track each fix.
close_reason: All 18 children closed. Deep wide-net review remediation complete: parser silent-drops (ref-def-para + container refs) + 2 DoS fixed, CLI data-loss guard + broken-pipe + batch typed exit codes, WASM render-warnings/parity/multi-image, Unicode emphasis flanking, low-severity hardening; highlighter cosmetics documented. CommonMark 362->371 (floor ratcheted). Full gauntlet + wasm-package gate green; nothing pushed (standing rule).

title: parser: char-reference ';' scan is O(n^2) — bound to spec-max entity length
description: parse_character_reference (src/parse/mod.rs:2534) scans chars[i+1..] to EOF for every '&'. On input of many '&' with no ';' this is O(n^2): timed 80KB -> 16.4s (debug). CommonMark 0.31.2 bounds valid refs: named entity names <=31 chars, decimal <=7 digits, hex <=6 digits. Fix: cap the ';' search window at ~33 chars (spec-faithful; zero behavior change for any valid ref). Add in-module #[cfg(test)] tests for coverage floor. Same DoS class prior audit fixed in emphasis/bracket but missed here.
close_reason: Fixed in commit: bounded char-ref ';' scan to 33-char spec-max window (MAX_CHAR_REF_BODY_LEN). Linear on '&'-dense DoS shape, byte-identical for all valid refs (conformance 362 unchanged), in-module char_ref_dos_tests added.

title: cli: refuse render when an output path resolves to the input file (data-loss guard)
description: run_render (src/cli.rs:312) reads the source fully into memory then std::fs::write()s to out_path() with NO check that output != input. 'fmd README.md --out README.md', 'fmd notes.md --to pdf --out notes.md', 'fmd doc.pdf --to pdf' (md misnamed .pdf), and 'fmd page.html --to both' all silently overwrite/destroy the user's source. Fix: preflight same-file check (canonicalize input; for each output path that exists, canonicalize+compare) before any write; refuse with exit 64. Must cover --to both (two output paths) and default-derived paths. Add tests.
close_reason: Fixed in 7246bce: run_render preflight refuses (exit 64) when any output path canonicalizes to the input file; covers html/pdf/both + derived paths; stdin/--text exempt. In-module + cli_contract tests, verified end-to-end (source preserved).

title: parser: link reference definitions must not interrupt a paragraph (silent content drop)
description:
collect_link_reference_metadata (src/parse/mod.rs:471-511) extracts ref-defs in a flat global pre-pass with no block context, so a '[label]: dest'-looking line that is a paragraph continuation is silently DELETED. 'foo
[bar]: /url
baz' -> parser drops the middle line and merges foo/baz; CommonMark keeps all three in one paragraph. 'Foo
[bar]: /baz

[bar]' spuriously resolves the link. Highest-value bug class (silent content drop). Fix: only treat a ref-def as a definition when it does NOT interrupt an open paragraph (ref-defs decided during block parse / at block boundaries). Verify against CommonMark spec examples 214/192. May raise conformance floor.
close_reason: Fixed in 277c5c0: collect_link_reference_metadata now tracks open-paragraph state and only extracts a ref-def at a block boundary (line_is_paragraph_text mirrors the block parser). Silent drop of interrupting ref-def lines fixed; CommonMark 362->363 (floor ratcheted, capabilities/README/docs in lockstep); in-module tests added.

title: highlight: '#'-directive line-start check is O(n^2) on dense '#' lines — short-circuit
description: lex_generic (src/highlight.rs:138) does code[..pos].rsplit('\n').next() for EVERY '#' in a hash_directives language (c/cpp/h/...). On a single long line of '#', each '#' pays O(distance-to-newline) -> Theta(L^2). Library/WASM callers have no input cap. Fix: replace with a cheap backward byte scan that stops at first non-space/tab (O(1) amortized on dense lines), preserving 'only whitespace before it on this line'. Add in-module test.
close_reason: Fixed in 528cf91: replaced rsplit('\n').trim() line-start check with O(1) line_prefix_is_blank backward scan. Byte-identical highlighting, 200K-'#' line instant, in-module DoS+tiling tests added.

title: wasm: surface render_warnings (dropped images / missing glyphs) to browser hosts
description: src/wasm.rs render_html/render_pdf build diagnostics only from parse-time parsed.diagnostics; render_warnings()/RenderWarning are never called (grep-confirmed). Native CLI reports them (cli.rs:438). So renderPdf('![x](missing.png)\n\n中文') returns valid PDF with diagnostics:[] — the dropped image and missing glyphs are invisible to browser hosts, violating the project's no-silent-drop doctrine. Fix: fold render_warnings into WasmRenderOutput.diagnostics for both html and pdf. Add wasm_api test.
close_reason: Fixed in 1e9ca4d: render_pdf now folds render_warnings into WasmRenderOutput.diagnostics as 'warning' entries; browser hosts see dropped images + missing glyphs (parity with native CLI). In-module + wasm_api tests added.

title: cli: batch exit-code mapping + --out-dir '-' refusal + --to both write-atomicity + broken-pipe
description: Cluster of cli.rs/batch.rs contract gaps: (C-B) batch strict-mode flattens input(66)/write(73/74) errors to exit 70, contradicting docs/BATCH_ORCHESTRATION.md; (C-C) documented '--out-dir -' refusal not implemented (creates a dir named '-'); (C-D) '--to both' writes HTML then can fail on PDF, leaving stale HTML — render both in memory before writing; (C-F) discovery/JSON commands println!-panic (exit 101) on broken pipe e.g. 'fmd capabilities --json | head'; (C-G) batch validates SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH even for --to html. Fix the safe subset that doesn't change good-path bytes; add cli_contract tests.
close_reason: Core contract fixes delivered in 8b288a9: batch --out-dir '-' refusal (exit 64), PDF-only SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH validation, and --to both write-atomicity (render both before writing). Feature-gated batch tests added. The two harder items split into focused beads: broken-pipe hardening (br-best-in-class-markdown-renderer-fmd-agent-ergonomics-commonma-l036.16) and batch strict-mode exit-code classification (br-best-in-class-markdown-renderer-fmd-agent-ergonomics-commonma-l036.17).

title: parser: collect link reference definitions inside blockquotes / list items
description: src/parse/mod.rs:184-214 collects ref-defs only at top level; nested parsing (parse_blocks_bounded, strip_blockquote output) never runs collect_link_references. '[foo]\n\n> [foo]: /url' leaves the link unresolved and leaks the ref-def text into blockquote output; CommonMark (ex 218) resolves it and empties the blockquote. Shares architectural root with refdef-para-drop; coordinate the two fixes. May raise conformance floor.
close_reason: Fixed in ce57e10: collect_nested_references descends blockquotes (reusing strip_blockquote + lazy continuation), collects nested defs into the shared map, and the blockquote branch strips consumed def lines. Spec ex 218 works; conformance 370->371, no regression. List items deferred to l036.18.

title: pdf: accept valid PNGs with trailing bytes after IEND
description: src/pdf.rs:1429 rejects any PNG with bytes after the IEND chunk CRC (len!=0 || next!=bytes.len() -> None), dropping real PNGs that carry trailing data (some exporters, EXIF-ish trailers, accidental concatenation, trailing newline) to alt-text + missing_image warning even though every real decoder renders them. Fix: stop scanning at IEND and ignore trailing bytes. Add a test with a valid PNG + trailing bytes.
close_reason: Re-applied in adf3c53 with maintainer approval: parse_png_chunks stops at IEND, ignores trailing bytes (non-empty IEND still rejected). pdf_test + in-module tests updated.

title: wasm_abi: preserve verbatim title/author/css to keep native<->WASM byte parity
description: src/wasm_abi.rs empty_to_none()/blank_to_none() TRIM titles/authors and drop whitespace-only CSS, but native passes them verbatim (cli.rs). Title '  Draft  ' -> native emits <title>  Draft  </title>/PDF (  Draft  ), WASM emits <title>Draft</title>/(Draft): bytes differ, breaking the README's byte-identical parity claim. The core wasm.rs WasmRenderOptions is already verbatim; only the ABI/JS layer diverges. Fix: only map truly-empty ('') to None, don't trim non-empty values; keep whitespace CSS. Add parity test.
close_reason: Fixed in 4916269: ABI now preserves title/author/custom_css verbatim (nonempty_verbatim); only truly-empty maps to None. empty_to_none retained for font/dark_mode. Parity test added.

title: parser: '***foo***' nesting order must be <em><strong> (strong innermost)
description: process_emphasis (src/parse/mod.rs:2056-2115) wraps em-outer/strong-inner for both-odd runs >=3, yielding foo. CommonMark consumes delimiters nearest the content first: foo (spec ex 467, 416). Wrong (visually identical) AST/HTML for a very common construct. NOTE: tests/parser_conformance.rs:637 currently locks in the wrong behavior — update that test + any golden + conformance ledger. Should raise conformance count.
close_reason: Fixed in 8167077: removed the both-odd emphasis special case so ***foo*** nests <em><strong> per CommonMark. Conformance 363->366 (floor+capabilities+docs lockstep); tests/snapshot updated.

title: parser: emphasis flanking must treat Unicode punctuation/symbols, not ASCII-only
description: emphasis_flanking (src/parse/mod.rs:1812-1828) uses c.is_ascii_punctuation(); CommonMark 0.31.2 treats Unicode P (punctuation) and S (symbol) categories as punctuation for flanking. '*£*bravo.' -> parser £bravo (spec ex 354: literal). Narrow to non-ASCII punct/symbol adjacent to */_. Needs a small Unicode punctuation/symbol classifier (no deps — clean-room ranges). Changes output; regen golden/ledger. Should raise conformance.
close_reason: Fixed in 21db75f: clean-room is_unicode_punctuation (Unicode 15.1 P+S range table, binary search) wired into emphasis_flanking; *£*bravo. now literal per spec ex 354. Conformance 369->370 (floor+capabilities+docs lockstep); tests added.

title: parser: strip fenced-code opening-fence indentation from content lines
description: Fenced-code body (src/parse/mod.rs:589-597) pushes lines verbatim; CommonMark removes up to N leading spaces per content line where N = opening fence indent. '   ' keeps '  code' instead of 'code'. Formatting only, no drop. Changes output; regen golden/ledger. May raise conformance.
close_reason: Fixed in e0421ca: strip_fence_indent removes up to N columns (N=opening fence indent) from each fenced code content line, tab-safe. Conformance 366->369 (floor+capabilities+docs lockstep); in-module tests added.

title: wasm_abi: accept multiple PDF image assets (parity with core wasm + native CLI)
description: src/wasm_abi.rs takes a single image_destination/image_bytes; wasm/franken_markdown.js throws 'at most one image asset'. Core wasm.rs (pdf_image_assets: Vec) and native (repeatable --pdf-image) accept many. Multi-image docs cannot achieve native<->WASM parity via the shipped package. Fix: plumb a Vec of {destination, bytes} through the wasm-bindgen ABI + JS wrapper. Bigger change; add a multi-image parity test to the wasm package gate corpus (currently 0 images).
close_reason: Fixed in d039f35: renderPdfConfiguredMulti accepts N images via parallel arrays; JS wrapper flattens all pdfImages (byte-identical for 0/1). check-wasm-package.sh green incl. new multi-image smoke; native parity preserved.

title: low-severity hardening: host font-byte cap, non-finite theme CSS, empty-font error msg, stale doc
description: Bundle of low-severity items: (W-D) no size cap on host-supplied font bytes though images are capped (add MAX_FONT_BYTES to FontAssets/wasm_abi set_font_asset); (H-D) public HtmlOptions/ThemeSpacing accept non-finite floats -> css_num emits 'NaN'/'inf' (validate is_finite in the library API, matching config.rs); (W-E) lib.rs:334 doc says render_html_document 'infallible' but validate()? makes it fallible + empty-font-bytes gives two different messages across HTML vs PDF path — unify. Small, defensive, no good-path change.
close_reason: Fixed in dbb6d1d: 32MiB font-byte cap (MAX_FONT_ASSET_BYTES); css_num folds non-finite -> 0; render_html_document doc corrected. In-module tests added.

title: highlight: cosmetic mis-coloring (unterminated string / JS regex) — evaluate + optionally fix
description: Byte-preserving (no content drop) mis-coloring: (H-B) an unterminated string literal runs to EOF coloring the rest of the block as string; (H-C) JS/TS '/regex/' literals are tokenized as division so a quote inside opens a spurious string span. Prior audit (ecr3.12) deliberately scoped highlighter classification. Low value; regex-vs-division needs real lexer context. Evaluate whether worth the complexity; may just document as known.
close_reason: Evaluated in 4dc4569: both are byte-preserving cosmetic mis-colorings; a correct fix needs per-language lexer state disproportionate to the value + regression risk (prior audit left it too). Documented as known limitations with rationale + a byte-preservation test.

title: cli: broken-pipe on discovery/JSON output should exit cleanly, not panic
description: Split from l036.6 (C-F). Discovery/JSON commands (capabilities, doctor human+json, robot-triage, config get/show/path, batch receipt) emit via println!, which unwraps its write result. With #![forbid(unsafe_code)] there is no SIGPIPE handler, so 'fmd capabilities --json | head' makes println! return Err -> panic (exit 101 + 'failed printing to stdout: Broken pipe' on stderr), corrupting the clean-JSON/exit-code contract agents rely on. Fix cleanly: build each command's output as one String and write it via a helper that treats ErrorKind::BrokenPipe as a clean exit (0) rather than panicking. ~20 println! sites across run_doctor/run_config/print_capabilities/print_robot_triage/batch receipt — do as one focused, reviewable commit.
close_reason: Fixed in 3b8f2d8: emit_stdout treats BrokenPipe as clean exit (0); all discovery/config commands + batch receipt converted (byte-identical output, cli_contract 44/46). Regression test added; verified 'fmd capabilities|true' exits 0 not 101.

title: cli/batch: strict-mode should map input(66)/write(73/74) failures to their codes, not flatten to 70
description: Split from l036.6 (C-B). Without --continue-on-error, any per-file Failed entry yields exit 70 (hard_failure), but docs/BATCH_ORCHESTRATION.md promises 66 for input errors (missing/oversized), 73/74 for output-write errors, 70 only for render failures. e.g. 'fmd batch big.md --max-input-bytes 16' (input error) exits 70 not 66; an unwritable out-dir exits 70 not 73. Fix: in run_batch strict mode, classify the first Failed entry's reason (batch.rs FileEntry) and return the documented exit code. Needs batch.rs failure-reason typing so the CLI can distinguish input vs write vs render failures. Add feature-gated cli_contract assertions.
close_reason: Fixed in 181b47d: FileErrorKind on FileEntry + first_failure_kind(); run_batch maps input->66/output->73/render->70. Receipt JSON gains error_kind. Verified 66/73/0 + feature-gated test.

title: parser: collect link reference definitions inside list items (follow-up to l036.7)
description: l036.7 landed blockquote-nested ref-def collection (collect_nested_references descends blockquotes; the blockquote branch strips consumed def lines). List-item-nested defs are still not collected/stripped — deferred because replicating parse_list's item-content extraction (marker + content-indent stripping, tight/loose, lazy continuation, sub-lists) in the collector risks divergence from the block parser. To do it safely: factor parse_list's per-item content extraction into a shared helper used by BOTH parse_list and collect_nested_references, then descend list items there and strip their def lines in parse_list's item body. Gate on full conformance + golden + no-regression like l036.7.
close_reason: Fixed in b604350: factored split_list_items (verbatim, behavior-preserving) shared by parse_list + collect_nested_references; list-item nested defs now collected + stripped. Conformance 371 unchanged (no list regression); full suite green.

title: Ref-def between two adjacent pipe tables merges them into one garbage table
description:
Found in the 2026-07-01 fresh-eyes ref-def review ("BUG5", MEDIUM-LOW).

REPRO (no blank lines between the tables and the definition):
    | a | b |
    | - | - |
    | 1 | 2 |
    [x]: /y
    | c | d |
    | - | - |
    | 3 | 4 |

    use [x]

EXPECTED: two separate tables, plus [x] -> /y (so 'use [x]' links).
ACTUAL: one merged table whose body rows are 1|2, c|d, -|-, 3|4 (the second
table's delimiter row '-|-' renders as a DATA row). The def is still collected
and 'use [x]' resolves correctly; only the table structure is wrong.

ROOT CAUSE: the collect/strip/re-parse architecture. table_extent() correctly
stops the first table at the non-pipe definition line, and the def line is
legitimately collected + stripped. But removing that line makes the two tables
textually contiguous, and parse_table_profiled's body loop greedily consumes any
'|'-containing line, absorbing the second table's header+delimiter as data rows.
NB: even in pure GFM, two pipe tables with NO separator between them are one
table; the def line was the only separator, and stripping it removes the block
boundary it provided.

WHY DEFERRED: esoteric input (two pipe tables separated ONLY by a ref-def, no
blank lines); GFM tables are an extension so 'correct' output is debatable; and
the obvious fixes are invasive/risky against the 371-conformance parser:
 - replacing a stripped def line with a blank would preserve boundaries but
   changes tight/loose-list and paragraph grouping broadly (high blast radius);
 - making the collector not strip (only mark) is a large architectural change.
Confirmed still present after the four sibling ref-def fixes landed.
close_reason: Fixed by preserving a non-rendered separator only when a consumed reference-definition run sits between two valid pipe-table extents. Added regression coverage proving the repro parses as two table blocks and the later [x] reference still resolves. Verification: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; cargo build --no-default-features; git diff --check.

title: Line breaker measures without GPOS kerning / GSUB ligatures (layout↔render metric divergence)
description:
Found in the 2026-07-02 fresh-eyes typography review (text/fonts investigator).

SYMPTOM: the Knuth-Plass line breaker measures word/space/hyphen widths through
Font::kerning_1000 (layout.rs PairMetrics impl), which consults ONLY the legacy
'kern' table (self.kern0). All 9 bundled faces ship GPOS+GSUB(liga) and NO legacy
'kern' table, so kern0 is always None -> layout measures with ZERO kerning and NO
ligatures. The PDF RENDERER, however, applies GPOS kerning (kerned_tj/kern.pair)
and GSUB ligatures (shape_run/substitute_with_spans). So line-break + justification
decisions are computed from widths systematically WIDER than what is rendered.

EFFECT: justified body lines fall slightly short of the right margin / get looser
interword spacing; break points are mildly suboptimal. One-directional (kerning and
ligatures both TIGHTEN), so it does NOT overflow the margin with the bundled fonts.
capabilities advertises gpos_kerning_pdf + gsub_ligatures_pdf as shipped, so this is
a live layout↔render inconsistency, not disabled code.

ROOT: layout::measure_advances() exists and is documented as the bridge for shaped
(GSUB/GPOS) advances, but is DEAD CODE (never called). The measurement path
(measure_text_with_pairs -> kerning_1000) predates the render-side shaping.

FIX DIRECTION: shape each run once (GSUB then GPOS) and feed the shaped/kerned
advances through measure_advances so the breaker sees the same widths the renderer
draws; or, minimally, have kerning_1000 fall back to GPOS pair kerning when the
legacy 'kern' table is absent (still misses ligatures).

BLAST RADIUS / WHY DEFERRED: changes EVERY PDF's line breaking -> the byte-fingerprint
golden PDFs (tests/golden_output.rs) and render-tree goldens all move and must be
regenerated + visually QA'd to confirm the new breaking is BETTER (a slightly-off
shaped measurement could newly OVERFLOW, the exact failure the ffkj overfull work
guards against). This is a deliberate typography increment, not a bug-hunt quick fix.
Deferred from the exploration pass for that reason.
close_reason: Fixed PDF layout measurement to use per-face GSUB ligature shaping and GPOS pair adjustments for word boxes, spaces, hyphen penalties, greedy wrapping, table/code text widths, and segment decoration advances. Added focused GPOS/GSUB measurement tests; regenerated intentional PDF/render-tree goldens. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, and cargo build --no-default-features.

title: PDF trailer omits /ID (spec-recommended, deterministic ID natural fit)
description:
Found in the 2026-07-02 pdf.rs review. build_pdf writes the trailer
'<< /Size N /Root 1 0 R /Info M 0 R >>' with NO /ID array (confirmed absent by parse).
The PDF spec strongly recommends /ID in all files (required for encryption/PDF-A);
many validators warn on its absence. For a renderer that markets deterministic output,
a deterministic /ID (e.g. a hash of the content, gated by SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH like the
other metadata) is the natural fit. Not malformed today; readers accept it. Adding /ID
changes every PDF's bytes -> regenerate golden fingerprints. LOW priority.
close_reason: Added deterministic PDF trailer /ID generation using bytes already serialized before the trailer plus stable trailer fields, writing identical 16-byte hex IDs for generated files. Added pdf_trailer_has_deterministic_content_sensitive_file_id coverage and regenerated rendered PDF snapshots for the intentional byte/fingerprint drift. Verification: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; cargo build --no-default-features; git diff --check.

title: PDF content-stream coord formatting: route raw {:.2}/{:.3} through non-finite guard
description:
Found in the 2026-07-02 pdf.rs review (defense-in-depth; THEORETICAL,
could not be triggered). pdf_num/append_pdf_num correctly coerce NaN/inf->0 and are used
for MediaBox/image cm/rects/dests. But draw_seg (Tm/Tf/strike/underline), rule strokes,
and rounded_rect_fill use raw format!("{:.2}")/("{:.3}"). A NaN/inf coordinate would
emit a malformed 'NaN'/'inf' token, and rounded_rect_fill's x1<=x0 guard is bypassed by
NaN (NaN comparisons are false). Currently unreachable: all page geometry is clamped by
positive_finite/nonnegative_finite, and adversarial inputs (200-col table, 5000-char word,
50-deep nesting, 500-word cell) produced no NaN/inf.

WHY NOT A DROP-IN FIX: append_pdf_num STRIPS trailing zeros (5.0 -> '5') whereas raw
'{:.2}' emits '5.00', so naively routing these sites through pdf_num would change EVERY
coordinate's bytes and churn all golden PDFs. A correct fix needs a format-preserving
non-finite guard (e.g. a helper that emits fixed-2/3 decimals but coerces non-finite to 0),
then convert the raw sites. LOW priority / defensive.
close_reason: Added format-preserving pdf_fixed2/pdf_fixed3 helpers that coerce NaN/inf to 0 while retaining existing finite fixed-width bytes. Routed content-stream color, text matrix, stroke, quote-bar, and rounded-rectangle coordinates through them; rounded_rect_fill now sanitizes before its degenerate guard. Added unit coverage for finite format preservation, non-finite coercion, and rounded-rectangle NaN handling. Verification: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; cargo build --no-default-features; cargo test --test golden_output; git diff --check.

title: PDF font subset: strip glyph instructions when dropping fpgm/prep/cvt, and preserve true lsb
description:
Found in the 2026-07-02 fresh-eyes subsetting review (both LOW, benign in mainstream viewers; the subsetter is otherwise verified sound — table dir, checksums, loca/glyf, composite gid rewrite, cmap all correct).

F1: subset_glyph_bytes (text.rs ~744-751) copies each glyph VERBATIM including its TrueType instruction bytecode, but the emitted sfnt drops fpgm/prep/cvt/gasp (text.rs ~654-664). The retained bytecode CALLs functions from the dropped fpgm and reads the dropped cvt, so it is a non-conformant half-state (no standard subsetter emits this). Benign in practice: FreeType-based viewers (poppler/mupdf) run non-pedantic hinting, swallow the errors, and fall back to the correct unhinted outline. But it bloats the subset with dead bytecode and could misrender under a strict/partial TrueType interpreter. Fix: strip glyph instructions when dropping the dependency tables (simple glyph: zero instructionLength + drop bytes; composite: clear WE_HAVE_INSTRUCTIONS + drop trailing instructions), OR retain fpgm/prep/cvt.

F2: hmtx leftSideBearing is hard-coded to 0 for every glyph (text.rs ~622-623) instead of the true xMin. Benign for this pipeline (Identity-H + CIDToGIDMap /Identity: advances come from /W+/DW, outlines from absolute coords, and head.flags bit 1 'lsb==xMin' is clear so compliant rasterizers won't shift by lsb-xMin). A correct subsetter preserves the original lsb. Could cause a small horizontal shift only under a rasterizer that honors lsb during placement.

WHY DEFERRED: both change every embedded subset's bytes -> churn all golden PDF fingerprints + font-embedding tests, and F1 is a non-trivial per-glyph glyf edit. Quality refinements, not correctness bugs (text renders correctly today).
close_reason: Implemented PDF font subset hint stripping for simple and composite glyphs, preserved true hmtx left side bearings, added regressions, and refreshed output goldens

title: Batch robustness cluster: queue_depth staleness, collect_dir depth bound, alias collisions, Both-format partial-write
description:
Found in the 2026-07-02 fresh-eyes batch review (all LOW/cosmetic/niche, THEORETICAL — the shipped binary is built without the batch feature; batch concurrency was verified deterministic and sound otherwise: input-order reassembly, correct atomics, cycle-safe walk, panic isolation).

1. queue_depth stale after the worker clamp: batch.rs ~820-828 clamps budget.workers = min(inputs.len()) but does NOT re-derive budget.queue_depth, so the receipt reports e.g. workers=2 with queue_depth=16 (8 CPUs, 2 inputs). Deterministic but misleading; re-derive queue_depth after the clamp.
2. collect_dir (batch.rs ~425-455) recurses without a depth bound — a genuinely deep real directory tree can overflow the stack (symlink cycles ARE already broken). Add a depth cap mirroring the parser's MAX_BLOCK_NESTING_DEPTH approach.
3. detect_output_collisions (batch.rs ~487-500) keys on a LEXICAL PathBuf, so two inputs whose outputs resolve to the same physical file via a case-insensitive FS or ./..//symlink alias escape the guard -> concurrent write race + nondeterministic receipt hash. Normalize/canonicalize output keys.
4. render_one with OutputFormat::Both writes the HTML, then if the PDF render fails returns failed() with outputs:Vec::new() (batch.rs ~544-595) -> the written .html is left on disk, unrecorded and uncleaned (stale on re-run). Same class as the CLI --to both partial-write (documented render-atomic-only scope). Consider temp-file+rename or recording/cleaning the partial output.

Also noted (documented limitation, not filed as a fix): --timeout cannot interrupt a single long/hung render (cancellation is only observed at the per-file checkpoint between files; batch.rs:154-160 documents this).
close_reason: Fixed batch queue_depth receipt staleness after worker clamp, bounded directory recursion, normalized output collision keys for case/alias paths, and prevented Both-format partial output leaks; added batch regressions and passed default plus batch-feature gates

title: Fresh-eyes hardening and verification follow-up program
description:
Background:
A fresh-eyes review pass over the current franken_markdown dirty change set found and fixed concrete issues around helper-script cleanup safety, installer checksum semantics, CLI stdout/stderr contracts, staged output rollback, syntax highlighting, PDF code/table/image quality, and documentation drift. This epic preserves the reasoning, evidence, and remaining follow-up work as self-contained Beads so future agents do not need to reconstruct intent from chat history.

Project goals served:
- Agent-first CLI behavior: stdout remains data; stderr remains diagnostics; JSON contracts stay machine-readable.
- Safety and reliability: helper scripts must never let caller-controlled run IDs move cleanup outside the intended artifact subtree.
- Release trust: installers should verify exact archive checksums from combined SHA256SUMS or per-archive sidecars, and explicit checksum skips should be unambiguous.
- PDF quality: table widths, code fences, diagrams, PNG transparency, and render-tree goldens should support polished Markdown-to-PDF output.
- WASM-first portability: no-default-features and wasm-package gates must stay healthy.

Definition of done for this epic:
All completed hardening slices remain represented with evidence; remaining follow-up beads are either closed with verification or deliberately deferred with rationale; `br dep cycles` stays empty; `.beads/issues.jsonl` is flushed for git.

Important standing rules:
Use only `br` for Beads mutations, never bare `bv`, keep work on main, do not delete files, and run the Rust verification gates after substantive Rust changes.
close_reason: All fresh-eyes hardening follow-up children closed with verification evidence

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Planning note:
This epic intentionally mixes closed evidence beads and open follow-up beads. The closed beads capture work already present in the dirty tree; the open beads turn the fresh-eyes findings into a durable roadmap for the next agents. If a future agent wonders why a test exists, this graph should answer that question without requiring the original conversation.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Closing readiness check:
- All parent-child dependents shown by br show are closed, including the fresh follow-up beads c3rr, wdu1, rniy, 4o22, rezb, and uppt.
- Final verification bead uppt recorded current pass evidence for cargo fmt/check/clippy/test/no-default, shell syntax and shellcheck, targeted render-tree and installer/helper tests, git diff --check, and br dep cycles.
- bv --robot-next reports no actionable non-epic item available.
- br dep cycles is clean.

This satisfies the epic definition of done: the hardening slices and follow-ups are represented with evidence, remaining actionable work is closed, and the Beads graph is cycle-free.

title: Harden CLI output contract stream assertions and cleanup boundary
description:
Background:
`scripts/cli-output-contract.sh` exercises the fmd output-path matrix. The fresh-eyes review found two concerns: it needed stronger stdout/stderr assertions, and the artifact work directory was derived from caller-provided `RUN_ID` before cleanup. Without validation, a traversal-style run id could move forced recursive cleanup outside `tests/artifacts/cli`.

Implemented scope:
- Validate `RUN_ID` against `^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9._-]{0,79}$` before constructing `ART`/`WORK`.
- Root artifacts under the absolute repo path `tests/artifacts/cli`.
- Run forced recursive cleanup only after validation and with option terminator protection.
- Split stdout and stderr into per-case artifacts.
- Assert HTML-to-stdout, JSON status-to-stderr, error JSON, empty stdout, and both HTML/PDF outputs for `--to both`.

Acceptance criteria:
Unsafe run IDs exit 64 before cleanup. Safe runs still exercise the matrix. File renders keep stdout empty and status on stderr. HTML stdout renders do not produce a status line.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in scripts/cli-output-contract.sh.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Evidence from implementation pass:
`bash -n scripts/cli-output-contract.sh` passed. `shellcheck --severity=warning scripts/cli-output-contract.sh` passed. `scripts/cli-output-contract.sh ../bad` exited 64 with the validation message before constructing or cleaning a work directory. Earlier real matrix run with a safe id passed after the stream split.

title: Guard WASM package gate run IDs before work cleanup
description:
Background:
`scripts/check-wasm-package.sh` builds and validates the browser/WASM package. Like the CLI contract script, it accepted a positional `RUN_ID` and performed forced recursive cleanup under `tests/artifacts/wasm/${RUN_ID}`. The same cleanup-boundary risk applies if the run id contains slashes or traversal.

Implemented scope:
- Validate `RUN_ID` with the same artifact-safe regex before building `ART` or `WORK`.
- Root artifacts under `$repo_root/tests/artifacts/wasm`.
- Clean `$WORK` only after validation and with option terminator protection.
- Preserve all existing no-default-features, wasm-bindgen, manifest, parity, and size-budget behavior.

Acceptance criteria:
Invalid run ids exit 64 before cleanup; safe existing wasm package gate semantics are unchanged.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in scripts/check-wasm-package.sh.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Evidence from implementation pass:
`bash -n scripts/check-wasm-package.sh` passed. `shellcheck --severity=warning scripts/check-wasm-package.sh` passed. `scripts/check-wasm-package.sh ../bad` exited 64 with the validation message before cleanup. Full `cargo build --no-default-features` also passed after the change.

title: Pin destructive helper-script run-id guards with source tests
description:
Background:
The cleanup-boundary bug class is easy to reintroduce because helper scripts often derive artifact paths from human-supplied run ids. A source-shape test is appropriate here because the invariant is about ordering: validation must happen before any destructive cleanup.

Implemented scope:
- Add `tests/script_sources_test.rs`.
- Verify both `scripts/cli-output-contract.sh` and `scripts/check-wasm-package.sh` contain the exact run-id validation.
- Verify each script roots artifacts under the intended directory.
- Verify validation text appears before forced recursive cleanup of `$WORK`.
- Avoid `expect()` in tests because repo clippy denies `expect_used`.

Acceptance criteria:
`cargo test --test script_sources_test` passes. `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes without test-only panic helpers.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in tests/script_sources_test.rs.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Evidence from implementation pass:
`cargo test --test script_sources_test` passed. Initial clippy caught `expect_used`; the test was corrected to return `io::Error` via `ok_or_else`. Final `cargo fmt --check`, clippy, and full test suite all passed.

title: Normalize installer checksum lookup, sidecar fallback, and CHECKSUM=SKIP semantics
description:
Background:
The release installers are part of the project trust boundary. The earlier implementation had brittle checksum lookup behavior and PowerShell could treat `CHECKSUM=SKIP` as if it were a literal checksum instead of an explicit opt-out. Fresh-eyes review aligned both installers with exact archive-name matching and clear skip behavior.

Implemented scope:
- Unix installer parses combined `SHA256SUMS` through `checksum_from_sums_file`, matching exact archive basenames rather than regex substrings.
- Unix installer falls back to per-archive `${DOWNLOADED_URL}.sha256` through `checksum_from_sidecar_file`.
- Unix installer centralizes default checksum URL selection with latest-release fallback when no tag is pinned.
- Unix installer handles `CHECKSUM=SKIP` case-insensitively and emits `Checksum verification explicitly skipped by CHECKSUM=SKIP`.
- PowerShell installer centralizes default checksum URL selection, parses exact basenames, adds sidecar fallback, and handles `CHECKSUM=SKIP` case-insensitively.

Acceptance criteria:
Exact matching prevents selecting the wrong archive checksum. Sidecar fallback works conceptually for per-asset releases. Explicit skips are obvious in logs and never fed to SHA-256 tools.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in install.sh, install.ps1, and installer source tests.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Evidence from implementation pass:
`bash -n install.sh` passed. `shellcheck --severity=warning install.sh` passed. `tests/installer_sources_test.rs` pins exact checksum parsing, sidecar fallback, latest-release SHA256SUMS URLs, and explicit skip messages for Unix and PowerShell installers.

title: Pin installer checksum source behavior with tests
description:
Background:
Installer security regressions are expensive because they usually surface during release or user installation. A no-network source-shape test keeps the exact parsing and fallback expectations visible in the Rust test suite.

Implemented scope:
- Add `tests/installer_sources_test.rs`.
- Assert Unix installer uses `checksum_from_sums_file`, `checksum_from_sidecar_file`, `default_checksum_url`, latest-release SHA256SUMS fallback, and explicit `CHECKSUM=SKIP` reporting.
- Assert Unix installer no longer contains the older regex grep against `${TAR_BASENAME}`.
- Assert PowerShell installer parses checksum/file entries, compares exact archive basenames, uses sidecar fallback, and treats `CHECKSUM=SKIP` specially.

Acceptance criteria:
The test should fail if future edits reintroduce substring checksum matching, lose sidecar fallback, or make explicit skip behavior ambiguous.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in tests/installer_sources_test.rs.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Evidence from implementation pass:
`cargo test --test installer_sources_test` passed. Full `cargo test` and clippy passed after this test was added.

title: Preserve batch receipt stdout write failures as output errors
description:
Background:
For `fmd batch --json`, stdout is data: the receipt JSON. A broken pipe should not panic, but a real stdout write failure should not be hidden behind a successful or unrelated batch status. This is part of the agent-first CLI contract.

Implemented scope:
- Track the result of `emit_stdout(&receipt.to_json())` in `run_batch`.
- Return a non-success output error when stdout write fails for reasons other than swallowed broken-pipe behavior.
- Preserve existing human summary behavior on stderr for non-JSON batch runs.

Acceptance criteria:
Machine consumers can trust that if receipt JSON could not be emitted, the process reports that output failure instead of silently reporting the batch result.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in src/cli.rs.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Evidence from implementation pass:
`cargo check --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `cargo test` passed. The change is localized to `src/cli.rs` around batch receipt emission.

title: Make staged output commits robust to broken symlinks and directory races
description:
Background:
The renderer writes HTML/PDF/config outputs through staged temp files so multi-output operations do not leave partial files. Fresh-eyes review focused on filesystem edge cases where `Path::exists()` is misleading: broken symlinks return false, and a directory can appear at the destination between staging and commit.

Implemented scope:
- Use `symlink_metadata` in commit-time destination checks so broken symlink destinations are treated as existing entries and backed up/restored correctly.
- Refuse directory destinations at commit time with `AlreadyExists`.
- Use `symlink_metadata` when probing backup temp path availability.
- Add regression tests for rollback restoring a broken symlink destination and for refusing a directory that appears after staging.

Acceptance criteria:
Rollback must restore the original broken symlink exactly. Directory races must leave the directory intact, remove staged temp files, and leave no backup artifacts.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in src/file_write.rs.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Evidence from implementation pass:
Targeted tests `rollback_restores_broken_symlink_destination` and `commit_refuses_directory_that_appears_after_staging` passed. Full `cargo test` and clippy passed.

title: Add PowerShell syntax highlighting for installer snippets
description:
Background:
The README and install documentation include PowerShell one-liners such as `irm ... | iex`. Without a PowerShell lexer, generated HTML/PDF previews miss useful syntax highlighting for Windows install instructions.

Implemented scope:
- Add `powershell`, `pwsh`, and `ps1` aliases to the generic lexer table.
- Add case-insensitive PowerShell keywords and common install aliases (`irm`, `iwr`, `iex`, `curl`, `wget`).
- Preserve string and comment highlighting for install snippets.

Acceptance criteria:
PowerShell install snippets highlight aliases, quoted URLs, and comments without dropping bytes or affecting unsupported language fallback.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in src/highlight.rs.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Evidence from implementation pass:
Unit test `powershell_install_snippet_highlights_aliases_strings_and_comments` passed. Full highlight tests and full cargo test passed.

title: Preserve and fit ASCII diagrams in PDF code fences
description:
Background:
The user observed that ASCII diagrams looked bad when code fences wrapped like ordinary source code. For diagrams, preserving geometry is more important than wrapping at the usual code font size. The PDF renderer should shrink diagram-like code fences within bounds rather than destroying alignment.

Implemented scope:
- Introduce `CODE_FONT_SIZE` and `CODE_DIAGRAM_MIN_FONT_SIZE`.
- Detect diagram-preserving languages (`mermaid`, `mmd`, `dot`, `graphviz`, `ascii`, `diagram`) and diagram-like plain/text fences.
- Normalize language metadata such as `language-text,diagram`.
- Fit preserved diagrams by shrinking monospace font size down to a safe minimum, accounting for optional line-number columns.
- Keep ordinary source code wrapping behavior unchanged.

Acceptance criteria:
Diagram source rows remain one rendered row when fitted. Ordinary code still wraps in narrow measures. Line numbers are included in fit calculations.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in src/pdf.rs tests.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Evidence from implementation pass:
Table/code tests `ascii_diagram_text_fence_preserves_rows_and_fits_by_shrinking`, `diagram_preservation_accepts_language_metadata_suffixes`, `ascii_diagram_fitting_accounts_for_line_number_column`, and `ordinary_source_code_still_wraps_when_requested` passed.

title: Replace heuristic PDF table widths with min/max-content badness allocation
description:
Background:
The performance-plan PDF had too-narrow table headers, causing poor wrapping. A heuristic fix was not enough: the better design computes min-content and max-content per column and allocates constrained widths to minimize total wrapping badness, with heavier penalties for header wrapping.

Implemented scope:
- Measure table cells into min-content, max-content, per-line word/space widths, and header/body weights.
- Allocate column widths using a bounded dynamic-programming search over extra width units.
- Penalize header wrapping more heavily than body wrapping.
- Avoid spending width on empty columns before content columns have usable space.
- Balance surplus after all columns fit.
- Keep rendered table structure, styling, links, and alignment intact.
- Update render-tree golden positions after intentional width changes.

Acceptance criteria:
The exact performance-plan EV matrix keeps compact headers readable, does not starve the long Candidate column or code-like First bead column, and materially improves measured wrapping badness over proportional max-content allocation.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in src/pdf.rs and render-tree golden.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Evidence from implementation pass:
Tests `table_allocator_preserves_compact_header_columns_under_pressure`, `table_allocator_does_not_spend_width_on_empty_columns`, and `table_allocator_balances_surplus_after_all_columns_fit` passed. `tests/golden/render_tree/table.txt` was updated for deterministic render-tree drift.

title: Harden PNG parsing, transparency, and resource bounds for PDF embedding
description:
Background:
PDF image embedding accepts user-provided PNG bytes. The parser/decoder must reject malformed PNG structures cleanly, enforce decoded-memory bounds, and handle transparency exactly enough that PDFs do not silently embed broken or misleading images.

Implemented scope:
- Reject invalid color-type/bit-depth combinations during chunk parsing.
- Reject malformed PLTE/tRNS ordering, duplicate PLTE/tRNS, palette over-capacity, and invalid tRNS on alpha-bearing color types.
- Enforce bit-depth-aware decoded byte caps before allocating decoded buffers.
- Validate predictor fast-path IDAT payloads before passing through to PDF.
- Decode grayscale/RGB tRNS using exact raw samples, including 16-bit sample comparisons.
- Permit trailing bytes after a valid empty IEND while still rejecting non-empty IEND.
- Update stale parser comments so docs match parser-side validation behavior.

Acceptance criteria:
Malformed PNGs fall back cleanly instead of panicking or producing broken PDF XObjects. Valid common PNGs, transparency masks, and predictor fast path remain accepted.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in src/pdf.rs PNG tests.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Evidence from implementation pass:
All `png_decode_tests` passed, covering invalid combos, malformed tRNS, decoded-byte caps, exact 16-bit transparency, predictor validation, trailing bytes after IEND, and non-empty IEND rejection. Full `cargo test` passed.

title: Align batch orchestration docs to the implemented API and receipt contract
description:
Background:
`docs/BATCH_ORCHESTRATION.md` had stale design-contract language and names from earlier Asupersync sketches. Future agents need the docs to describe the code that exists now: `BatchPlan` with `Vec<PathBuf>`, separate HTML/PDF options, worker budget input, FNV receipt fingerprints, timeout semantics, and no built-in glob parser.

Implemented scope:
- Mark the document as implemented contract.
- Update CLI flags (`--timeout`, `--css`, no internal glob parser).
- Update Rust API sketch to match `src/batch.rs`.
- Replace stale region/queue/lab harness wording with current runtime shard/worker behavior.
- Update receipt example from SHA-256/budget-refused fields to FNV-1a-64 fingerprints and current error fields.

Acceptance criteria:
A future agent can implement or debug batch work from the docs without chasing stale names like `BatchInput`, `BudgetRefusal`, `RegionMode`, or `SporkAppHarness`.
close_reason: Implemented in docs/BATCH_ORCHESTRATION.md.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Evidence from implementation pass:
Searches for stale terms in the batch doc found no remaining obsolete identifiers except intentional source/test references outside this doc. Full cargo gates passed after documentation changes.

title: Run full verification gates for the fresh-eyes hardening set
description:
Background:
The changed surface spans shell scripts, installer source, CLI code, file writing, syntax highlighting, PDF layout/image logic, docs, and golden output. A narrow smoke test is not enough; the full project gates are the proof that the hardening did not break portability, clippy policy, deterministic goldens, or core behavior.

Implemented verification:
- `bash -n scripts/cli-output-contract.sh scripts/check-wasm-package.sh install.sh`
- `shellcheck --severity=warning scripts/cli-output-contract.sh scripts/check-wasm-package.sh install.sh`
- invalid-run-id probes for CLI contract and WASM package scripts
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test`
- `cargo build --no-default-features`
- targeted installer/script source tests, PNG tests, table allocator tests
- `git diff --check`

Acceptance criteria:
All gates pass; any known non-failing diagnostic is documented.
close_reason: All listed verification gates passed.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Evidence from implementation pass:
All listed gates passed. Default shellcheck (without severity filtering) still reports info-only SC2317 reachability notes in install.sh around helper/trap functions; warning-and-above is clean. That nuance is recorded so future agents do not chase false failures.

title: Audit every artifact-producing helper script for caller-controlled cleanup paths
description:
Background:
The confirmed bug class was caller-controlled run ids influencing artifact paths that are later cleaned with forced recursive cleanup. Two modified scripts are now guarded, but the repository contains many helper scripts with `RUN_ID`, `ART`, `ARTIFACT_DIR`, `WORK`, or e2e artifact paths. Some only create directories; others remove work directories or aggregate outputs. We need a systematic audit beyond the files touched in the fresh-eyes pass.

Goal:
Find every script where a human/CI/agent-supplied identifier contributes to a path that is later deleted, recursively overwritten, or used as a staging root. Add validation or restructure paths so traversal, absolute paths, spaces, shell metacharacters, and dot-only identifiers cannot escape the intended artifact root.

Initial search handles to start from:
- Search for `RUN_ID`, `ART=`, `ARTIFACT_DIR`, `WORK=`, cleanup commands, `mktemp`, and `validate_run_id` across `scripts/`.
- Known scripts with run ids but not yet fully audited in this bead: `check-claim-discipline.sh`, `check-pdf-ua.sh`, `commonmark-conformance.sh`, `coverage.sh`, `mutation.sh`, `theme-proof.sh`, `pagination-proof.sh`, `scripts/e2e/lib.sh`, `scripts/e2e/run-all.sh`, `batch-throughput.sh`, `parser-perf.sh`, `layout-perf-proof.sh`, `perf-gauntlet.sh`, and any newer helpers.

Acceptance criteria:
- Produce a list of every artifact-producing script and classify it as safe, fixed, or intentionally not applicable.
- For unsafe cases, patch with minimal validation before cleanup and add/extend tests in `tests/script_sources_test.rs` or an equivalent no-network harness.
- Do not delete artifacts manually during audit.
- Run `bash -n` and `shellcheck --severity=warning` on touched scripts.
- Run targeted source tests and `git diff --check`.

Future-self notes:
Use the existing CLI/WASM run-id regex unless a script already has an equal-or-stricter `validate_run_id`. If a script needs richer IDs, document why and make the cleanup root proof explicit.
close_reason: Completed helper-script run-id artifact path audit and hardening; validation/tests/probes pass

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Why this remains open:
The fresh-eyes pass fixed the confirmed modified scripts, but a repo-wide helper audit was intentionally split into its own bead to avoid mixing broad shell policy changes into the immediate bug fix. This should be the next security/reliability task.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Completed implementation pass for the helper-script artifact cleanup audit.

Root cause diagnosed:
Several proof/e2e helper scripts accepted a caller-supplied run id and interpolated it into tests/artifacts/... paths before any validation. The riskiest cases were cleanup paths such as scripts/e2e/run-all.sh and scripts/e2e/lib.sh, where an unsafe run id could redirect recursive cleanup outside the intended artifact root. Other helpers wrote artifact bundles without cleanup, but the same path construction pattern deserved a consistent guard.

Fixed in this pass:
- Added the existing strict run-id policy (^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9._-]{0,79}$) before artifact path use in scripts/pagination-proof.sh, scripts/theme-proof.sh, scripts/check-pdf-ua.sh, scripts/check-claim-discipline.sh, scripts/commonmark-conformance.sh, scripts/coverage.sh, scripts/mutation.sh, scripts/batch-throughput.sh, scripts/perf-gauntlet.sh, scripts/e2e/run-all.sh, and scripts/e2e/lib.sh.
- Moved batch-throughput validation ahead of the release build so invalid ids fail fast before expensive work.
- Kept the existing already-fixed guards in scripts/cli-output-contract.sh and scripts/check-wasm-package.sh.
- Extended tests/script_sources_test.rs so every guarded artifact-producing script is pinned, including the e2e cleanup paths.

Classified as already safe:
- scripts/parser-perf.sh, scripts/layout-perf-proof.sh, and scripts/pdf-perf-proof.sh already had validate_run_id before tests/artifacts/perf/$RUN_ID construction.
- scripts/cli-output-contract.sh and scripts/check-wasm-package.sh already validated before work-directory cleanup.

Classified as not applicable for this run-id cleanup class:
- scripts/perf-counters.sh accepts an explicit --out directory by design and does not recursively clean that caller path; its self-test cleanup uses mktemp-owned state.
- scripts/perf-compare.sh, scripts/check-test-doubles.sh, and scripts/release-smoke.sh clean mktemp-owned temporary directories rather than run-id-derived artifact roots.
- scripts/e2e/cli-surface.sh, render-matrix.sh, error-paths.sh, and installer.sh inherit the central e2e_init guard from scripts/e2e/lib.sh.

Verification run:
- bash -n on all touched helper scripts: pass.
- shellcheck --severity=warning on all touched helper scripts: pass.
- cargo fmt --check: pass.
- cargo test --test script_sources_test: 4 passed.
- git diff --check: pass.
- Invalid run-id smoke loop across patched entry points plus e2e run-all and cli-surface: every case exited 64 before artifact cleanup/path work.

No renderer code was changed for this bead. The remaining follow-up bead about shared run-id policy can now decide whether to factor these repeated inline guards into a reusable shell helper; this bead intentionally chose direct local guards to remove the immediate security/reliability risk without changing script loading behavior.

title: Introduce shared run-id validation policy for artifact helper scripts
description:
Background:
The CLI contract and WASM package scripts now duplicate the same artifact-safe run-id regex. Duplication is acceptable for a hot fix, but it invites drift as more scripts are audited. A small shared convention can make future helpers safer and easier to review.

Goal:
After the repo-wide audit identifies all patterns, decide whether to add a shared shell helper (for example under `scripts/` or `scripts/e2e/`) or a documented inline pattern. Keep it dependency-free and portable to the shells the scripts already target.

Acceptance criteria:
- Choose a single project-standard run-id grammar for artifact directories.
- Explain when a script may deviate and what proof it must provide.
- Apply the standard to scripts that perform cleanup or recursive overwrite under caller-influenced artifact roots.
- Add source or behavior tests proving validation appears before destructive cleanup.
- Keep `bash -n`, shellcheck warning-and-above, and relevant Rust source tests green.

Constraints:
Do not introduce a heavy shell framework. Do not make scripts less portable. Do not create hidden cleanup side effects.
close_reason: Centralized helper-script artifact run-id validation policy and pinned it with source tests

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Dependency note:
This depends on the repo-wide audit bead because the right abstraction should be based on the full set of scripts, not just the two fixed during the immediate fresh-eyes pass.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Completed shared run-id policy implementation.

Decision:
A shared helper is justified because the same artifact run-id grammar appeared across many helper scripts. The policy is now centralized in scripts/validate-run-id.sh as FMD_RUN_ID_PATTERN and fmd_validate_run_id. The helper is source-only, Bash-native, dependency-free, and exits 64 for invalid ids.

Applied standard:
- Direct cleanup/work-dir scripts now source the helper before cleanup/path use: scripts/cli-output-contract.sh, scripts/check-wasm-package.sh, scripts/e2e/lib.sh, scripts/e2e/run-all.sh.
- Artifact-producing proof/perf scripts now source the same helper before tests/artifacts path construction: pagination-proof, theme-proof, check-pdf-ua, check-claim-discipline, commonmark-conformance, coverage, mutation, batch-throughput, perf-gauntlet, parser-perf, layout-perf-proof, and pdf-perf-proof.
- scripts/e2e/run-all.sh validates derived aggregate and suite run ids before cleanup/build, so near-limit base ids cannot pass the base check and then fail later after work has started.

Test coverage:
- tests/script_sources_test.rs now pins the helper grammar, helper function, exit 64 behavior, and validates that each artifact-producing script sources the helper and calls fmd_validate_run_id before artifact path use or cleanup.

Verification:
- bash -n on all touched helper scripts: pass.
- shellcheck --severity=warning on all touched helper scripts: pass.
- cargo fmt --check: pass.
- cargo test --test script_sources_test: 5 passed.
- invalid run-id smoke loop over positional and --run-id scripts: pass, all rejected with exit 64.
- overlong e2e aggregate derived id probe: pass, rejected with exit 64 before cleanup/build.
- git diff --check: pass.

Notes for future self:
Scripts that intentionally accept arbitrary output directories, such as perf-counters --out, are outside this run-id policy unless they start recursively cleaning caller-controlled paths. New tests/artifacts run-id scripts should source scripts/validate-run-id.sh and call fmd_validate_run_id before deriving or cleaning artifact roots.

title: Add no-network behavioral installer checksum parser tests
description:
Background:
`tests/installer_sources_test.rs` pins security-relevant source shape, which is valuable, but behavioral tests would give stronger proof for exact basename matching, sidecar fallback, and `CHECKSUM=SKIP` behavior. The tests should avoid live GitHub, real releases, or requiring PowerShell on platforms where it is unavailable.

Goal:
Create no-network installer tests or harnesses that exercise checksum parsing behavior against local fake archives and fake checksum files. Prefer lightweight shell tests for Unix behavior and optional/skip-cleanly PowerShell checks when `pwsh` is available.

Acceptance criteria:
- A checksum for `fmd-x.tar.gz` must not match `other-fmd-x.tar.gz` or any substring-neighbor archive.
- Sidecar `.sha256` files with a leading 64-hex token are accepted.
- `CHECKSUM=SKIP`, including case variants, bypasses checksum tools and logs an explicit warning.
- Tests do not contact the network, do not install binaries, and clean only their own `mktemp` directories.
- Source-shape tests remain in place as an additional guard.
close_reason: Added no-network Unix installer checksum behavior harness and Rust integration test

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Why this remains open:
The implemented source tests are adequate for the immediate regression, but release-trust code benefits from real parser behavior tests. Keep this separate from the installer fix so it can be designed without rushing.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Completed no-network Unix installer checksum behavior tests.

Implementation:
- Added a hidden install.sh self-test mode gated by FMD_INSTALLER_CHECKSUM_SELF_TEST=1. It runs after checksum parser helpers are defined and before acquisition/extraction, so it does not download artifacts or install binaries. The test invocation uses --from-source, --dest <temp>, --force, --quiet, and --no-gum to keep preflight local and quiet.
- Added tests/installer_behavior_test.rs. The Rust test creates a private temp dest, runs the hidden installer self-test, asserts success, asserts the visible CHECKSUM=SKIP warning path is exercised, and asserts no fmd binary was installed.

Behavior covered:
- Exact SHA256SUMS basename matching: fmd-x.tar.gz does not match other-fmd-x.tar.gz or a substring neighbor.
- Path-qualified SHA256SUMS entries are reduced to exact archive basenames.
- Sidecar .sha256 parsing accepts a leading 64-hex token.
- CHECKSUM=SKIP is case-insensitive for SKIP/skip/sKiP and does not accept SKIPPING.
- Default checksum URL behavior covers both latest and tagged releases.

PowerShell note:
pwsh is not available in this environment, so PowerShell remains covered by tests/installer_sources_test.rs source-shape assertions for exact basename matching, sidecar fallback, and CHECKSUM=SKIP. The new behavioral harness is Unix-only and no-network.

Verification:
- bash -n install.sh: pass.
- shellcheck --severity=warning install.sh: pass.
- cargo fmt --check: pass.
- cargo test --test installer_behavior_test: 1 passed.
- cargo test --test installer_sources_test: 1 passed.
- git diff --check: pass.

title: Add golden regression for performance-plan table, syntax highlighting, and ASCII diagrams
description:
Background:
The user originally noticed three visual problems in generated PDFs: syntax highlighting was absent, ASCII diagrams looked poor, and `docs__PERFORMANCE_ACCELERATION_PLAN.pdf` table headers were too narrow. Unit tests now pin the underlying table allocator and diagram fitting logic, but an exact fixture/golden that renders the representative markdown would guard the user-visible workflow more directly.

Goal:
Create a deterministic golden or structural artifact test around the performance-plan table and representative code/diagram fences. The test should catch regressions in header wrapping, code syntax color spans, and diagram line preservation without relying on manual visual inspection.

Acceptance criteria:
- Use a stable fixture containing the exact performance-plan EV matrix shape, compact numeric headers, code-like bead labels, and long Candidate text.
- Include at least one PowerShell or Rust highlighted fence and one ASCII/mermaid-like diagram fence.
- Assert deterministic render-tree or PDF-structure properties that correspond to the visual issue: header segments remain one line where intended; diagram source rows are not hard-wrapped; syntax fill tokens appear for supported languages.
- If a PDF byte golden is used, document regeneration commands and keep it deterministic under `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH`.
- Run the targeted golden test plus full cargo gates after any renderer changes.
close_reason: Added performance-plan render-tree golden and direct assertions for table headers, syntax highlighting, and diagram preservation

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Why this remains open:
The allocator/diagram fixes have strong unit tests, but this project values golden visual/output fixtures before claiming PDF quality. This bead connects the lower-level tests to the exact user-observed PDF workflow.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Completed performance-plan render-tree regression coverage.

Implementation:
- Extended the existing Linux-only render-tree golden harness in src/pdf.rs with a new performance-plan fixture named performance-plan.
- The fixture uses the representative EV matrix shape from the performance acceleration plan: Candidate, Impact, Confidence, Effort, Score, and First bead columns; compact numeric headers; long Candidate text; and code-like bead labels such as fep.6, zmd.1, and qw1.5.
- Added a PowerShell fenced block using irm/iex plus a comment so the supported highlighter must emit syntax-colored PDF code segments.
- Added a mermaid/ascii-like diagram fence with a deliberately long row so the PDF code-fence line preservation/shrinking path is covered by the render-tree artifact.
- Added direct render-tree assertions for the original user-visible regressions: Impact/Confidence/Effort/Score header segments must each appear as one table-header segment; the long diagram source row must stay one rendered code row; and PowerShell irm, iex, and comment spans must render with non-body fill colors.
- Added the committed golden tests/golden/render_tree/performance-plan.txt. This uses the existing documented regeneration path: UPDATE_RENDER_TREE=1 cargo test render_tree. No PDF byte golden was added, avoiding byte-level brittleness while still pinning layout, structure, x positions, font sizes, and fill colors.

Verification:
- UPDATE_RENDER_TREE=1 cargo test render_tree: pass; wrote the new golden.
- cargo test render_tree: pass; 3 render-tree tests passed without update mode.
- cargo fmt --check: pass.
- cargo check --all-targets: pass.
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings: pass.
- cargo test: pass; full suite passed.
- cargo build --no-default-features: pass.
- git diff --check: pass.

title: Wire fresh-eyes helper-script gates into aggregate verification
description:
Background:
New source-shape tests protect run-id cleanup order and installer checksum source shape, but helper scripts also live outside the normal Rust unit-test mental model. Future agents should have a single aggregate path that runs the relevant script hygiene checks.

Goal:
Decide how `tests/script_sources_test.rs`, `tests/installer_sources_test.rs`, `bash -n`, and shellcheck warning-and-above should be represented in `scripts/test-all.sh` and/or CI. Avoid making optional heavy gates mandatory unless prerequisites are available.

Acceptance criteria:
- Aggregate verification includes source-shape tests.
- Shell syntax checks cover changed helper scripts or a documented curated set.
- Shellcheck runs warning-and-above where shellcheck is available, and skips clearly otherwise if used in optional local scripts.
- CI/test-all output remains agent-friendly with clear failure messages.
- No helper script performs unsafe cleanup during the check itself.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Dependency note:
This should wait until the repo-wide script audit and shared policy decisions are complete enough that CI does not enshrine a half-finished convention.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Completed aggregate helper-script gate wiring.

Implementation:
- Added a curated HELPER_SHELL_SCRIPTS list to scripts/test-all.sh covering the artifact/helper scripts changed by the cleanup work plus install.sh and test-all itself.
- Added a helper shell syntax gate that runs bash -n over that curated list and fails clearly if any expected script is missing or syntactically invalid.
- Added an optional helper shellcheck gate that runs shellcheck --severity=warning when shellcheck is installed and prints a clear skip message when it is not available.

title: Document artifact-safe helper-script conventions for future agents
description:
Background:
The project already documents no destructive git, stdout/stderr rules, WASM portability, and Beads workflow. The fresh-eyes cleanup bug shows we also need an explicit convention for helper scripts that accept run ids and write under `tests/artifacts`.

Goal:
Add concise project documentation explaining artifact root safety: validate caller-controlled path components before cleanup, root artifacts under the repo, use option terminators with destructive commands, prefer `mktemp` for temporary work, and add source/behavior tests when scripts clean paths derived from arguments.

Acceptance criteria:
- Documentation is short, actionable, and consistent with AGENTS.md.
- It names the project-standard run-id grammar or points to the shared helper/policy.
- It explains when source-shape tests are appropriate and when behavior tests are stronger.
- It does not encourage broad deletion or manual cleanup.
close_reason: Documented artifact-safe helper-script conventions in AGENTS.md

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Dependency note:
Document after the audit/shared-validator work so the policy reflects what the scripts actually do.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Completed helper-script artifact safety documentation.

Implementation:
- Added a concise Helper Script Artifact Safety section to AGENTS.md so future agents see the convention during normal repo onboarding.

- Required fmd_validate_run_id before constructing, cleaning, or recursively overwriting run-id-derived artifact directories.

title: Run full verification after remaining hardening follow-ups
description:
Background:
The current dirty tree passed the full gates after the immediate fixes. The remaining follow-ups may touch many scripts, installer tests, CI wiring, and PDF golden coverage. They need their own final verification bead rather than relying on old evidence.

Acceptance criteria:
After follow-up implementation, run the relevant full gate set:
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test`

Record exact pass/fail evidence in comments before closing.
