title: pdf: replace text task-list markers with vector checkboxes
close_reason: Rendered task-list markers as vector PDF checkboxes while preserving invisible selectable marker text; focused and full verification passed.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Completed PDF task-list marker rendering.

Implementation:
- List marker segments now carry task-list state from the parsed list item.
- Checked and unchecked task markers draw native vector checkbox shapes in PDF instead of visible bracket text.
- The original [x]/[ ] marker text is still emitted with invisible text rendering mode, preserving selectable text and assistive extraction.
- The intentional visual output shift is pinned in the lists PDF golden.

Verification:
- cargo test --lib task_checkbox_marker_writer_draws_box_and_checkmark: pass.
- cargo test --test pdf_test pdf_tags_task_list_markers_and_keeps_them_selectable: pass.
- cargo test --test golden_output rendered_html_and_pdf_match_committed_goldens: pass.
- Full final gates passed: cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, br dep cycles, cargo build --no-default-features, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test.

title: pdf: URLs/long tokens overflow the measure - add separator + emergency break points
close_reason: Added URL/separator and emergency non-hyphen PDF break points for long tokens; focused and full verification passed.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Completed long-token PDF wrapping.

Implementation:
- PDF paragraph word construction now merges dictionary hyphenation with separator break points for URLs, paths, and identifier punctuation.
- Long separator-free spans gain emergency non-hyphen break points, preventing a single token from overflowing the measure.
- Separator and emergency breaks do not inject visible hyphens, so URLs and identifiers do not gain characters they did not contain.
- Dictionary break points now obey the same two-character edge guard and uniqueness invariant as synthetic break points.

Verification:
- cargo test --lib long_pdf_words_gain_separator_and_emergency_break_points: pass.
- cargo test --test pdf_test pdf_wraps_long_urls_instead_of_overflowing: pass.
- Full final gates passed: cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, br dep cycles, cargo build --no-default-features, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test.

title: pdf: absurd word-spacing when an unbreakable box forces an early break
close_reason: Rejected over-shrunk line candidates as infeasible and updated render-tree golden; focused and full verification passed.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Completed over-shrink badness hardening.

Implementation:
- line_badness now treats lines that require more shrink than available glue as infeasible instead of merely high-badness.
- This matches TeX-style shrink semantics and prevents the breaker from crushing interword spaces toward zero to dodge a later feasible break.
- The intentional render-tree shift for prose justification is pinned in tests/golden/render_tree/prose.txt.

Verification:
- cargo test --lib line_badness_rejects_shrink_past_available_glue: pass.
- cargo test --lib render_tree_goldens_match: pass.
- Full final gates passed: cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, br dep cycles, cargo build --no-default-features, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test.

title: ci: lib coverage regressed below ratcheted floor (lines 92.08<95, regions 91.30<94, branches 75.75<85, functions 95.34<97)
description: CI coverage gate failing on main (first confirmed in run 28946769903 at commit 8bc618c, but likely accumulating over the 2026-07-07/08 perf-commit wave). The floor counts --lib tests only; most recent perf fast-paths and the PDF quality work are behavior-tested via integration/golden suites which do not count. Branch coverage gap (-9.25pts) is the largest. Options: (a) add lib-level tests for the hottest uncovered modules (worst per CI: src/cli.rs=80%, src/batch.rs=90%, src/compress.rs=90%, src/text.rs=90%, src/file_write.rs=90%), or (b) an explicitly justified floor adjustment via scripts/coverage.sh --update-floor — which is a governance decision for Jeffrey, not an agent. Claim-discipline self-test failure (the other red CI gate) was fixed separately in cbe0804.
close_reason: Fixed: coverage gate passes at the committed ratchet floor; final run coverage-after-final-highlighter-guard-20260709T0455Z is green and required Rust verification gates passed.

Dominant contributor: src/pdf.rs with 1992 of 2874 missed lines (69%) and 1380 of 1885 missed branches (73%) at 90.5% lines / 69.5% branches; next: cli.rs 79.6%, batch.rs 89.5% (55% branches), compress.rs 89.9%, text.rs 90.2%, file_write.rs 90.3%. Options: targeted lib-test campaign over pdf.rs hot paths, or justified floor adjustment via scripts/coverage.sh --update-floor (governance call for Jeffrey). title: pdf svg: clipPath/mask bodies outside <defs> render as visible elements
description: Found during the coverage-floor test campaign (bead 983h). parse_svg_document's main element dispatch loop (around src/pdf.rs:3236-3244) skips defs|style|script|foreignobject bodies but NOT clippath/mask elements placed outside <defs>. Their child shapes are emitted as ordinary visible elements, while browsers never paint clipPath/mask contents regardless of placement. Fix: add clippath|mask to the skip list (children should only be consumed via their def registries). Test to pin: an SVG with a top-level <clipPath><rect .../></clipPath> must not emit the rect as a drawable.
close_reason: Fixed current SVG parser behavior so top-level clipPath/mask bodies are definition-only, added PDF regression proving they do not paint while references still work, and completed focused plus full Rust verification.

comments.author: AzurePine
comments.text:
Completed the top-level SVG clipPath/mask visibility fix and regression proof.

Root cause: parse_svg_document collected clip/mask resources before drawing but did not skip top-level clipPath or mask containers during visible element dispatch, so their child shapes could be appended as ordinary painted elements when the definitions were outside <defs>.

Implementation/proof: current src/pdf.rs history skips clippath and mask in the same non-painting container list as defs/style/script/foreignObject. Added pdf_svg_top_level_clip_path_and_mask_bodies_do_not_paint in tests/pdf_test.rs. The regression proves red/white definition children do not paint while the same top-level clipPath and mask still apply to visible blue/green rectangles via clip-path and mask references.

Verification: fresh perf profile svg-skip-containers-before-20260709T0037Z; focused pdf_test filters for top-level clip/mask, clip paths, masks, and object-bounding-box clip/mask; cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; full cargo test.

title: text subsetter: dangling MORE_COMPONENTS composite fails whole subset while glyph_components tolerates it
description: Found during the coverage campaign (bead 983h), pinned by test subset_aborts_on_composite_whose_last_record_dangles_more. subset_glyph_bytes (src/text.rs:838) returns None for a composite glyph whose final record sets MORE_COMPONENTS with no following record, which aborts the ENTIRE font subset; glyph_components (src/text.rs:360) tolerates the same glyph by stopping the walk. A single malformed composite in an otherwise fine font thus kills embedding. Decide one consistent policy: either tolerate-and-truncate in subset_glyph_bytes (mirroring glyph_components) or substitute .notdef for the bad glyph rather than failing the subset.

title: cleanup: dead/defensive-unreachable code inventory from coverage campaign
description: Agents in the coverage-floor campaign (bead 983h) verified these as unreachable; each is a candidate for removal or a debug_assert, pending Jeffrey's preference (no deletions done): [batch] src/batch.rs:901 unwrap_or("output") never falls back; :759 .then() false-arm unreachable. [cli] src/cli.rs:1441-1443 global_with_value is a zero-length array so its consume branch is dead; :492 'PDF output requires --out' unreachable (rejected earlier at 315-322); :856 None-arm dead (every CONFIG_KEYS entry resolves). [text] src/text.rs:808-810 final body-pad loop unreachable (post is always last and 4-aligned); ~20 usize-overflow guard arms on off()/off_mul() provably can't overflow after prior bounds checks. [highlight] pervasive 'let Some(ch) = ... else break' char-boundary guards (11 sites) are dead; :504/:896/:938 boundary guards dead. [html] src/html.rs:810/:847 escaper _=>{} arms dead (scanner only returns escaped set); :1090 css_num empty-string fallback dead. [layout] breaker invariant guards 1650/1653-1654/1751-1752/1761 and hyphen-builder arms 1155/1163/1205 dead by construction. [compress] cl-decoder guards 407/853/856-857 and arms at 361/379/397/762 dead (decoder yields only 0..=18; loop invariant forces len==total). [pdf] layout_inlines_greedy + trim_trailing_spaces/token_width (~74 lines) unreachable: build_paragraph always appends a final forced break so candidates are never empty.

title: pdf svg: drop-shadow() with fewer than two lengths panics (eager then_some indexing)
description: Found by the round-3 coverage agent (campaign bead 983h). parse_svg_drop_shadow_args (src/pdf.rs:10776) ends with (lengths.len() >= 2).then_some(SvgShadowLayer { dx: lengths[0], dy: lengths[1], .. }). Bool::then_some evaluates its argument EAGERLY, so lengths[0]/lengths[1] are indexed even when the guard is false: any user-supplied SVG with e.g. style="filter: drop-shadow(red)" or drop-shadow(foo bar) panics with index out of bounds — a DoS-class crash reachable from documents. Fix: swap then_some(..) for then(|| SvgShadowLayer { .. }) and add a regression test rendering both malformed forms (shape must still paint, no shadow, no panic). Fix is staged to land as soon as the peer's src/pdf.rs WIP clears.
close_reason: Fixed in f21485a: then_some -> then(||) with a three-form regression test proven to panic pre-fix

title: ci: wasm browser package gate red on main — size budget exceeded (size_fail=1, parity clean)
description: The 'WASM browser package gate' job fails on main (seen on run 28986028497 at ca22dcc and later): native<->WASM byte parity is fully IDENTICAL for all four outputs, but the gate reports 'wasm-package gate: FAILED (size_fail=1 parity_fail=0)' — the wasm binary has outgrown its ratcheted size ceiling in scripts/check-wasm-package.sh. Almost certainly the accumulated svg/pdf feature work (vector-effect stroke, pattern stroke tiling, clip/mask pinning, list-tail orphan control) rather than test commits (tests do not ship in the binary). Owner should decide: trim wasm size (feature-gate? opt-level tweaks?) or justify a budget ratchet upward. The other two red cross-platform gates from that run are fixed (c33a5ce windows test gating, 41ac8f9 bash-3.2 perf-counters); coverage floor is green as of the campaign (983h).
